jreleaser.yml.short_description: Release and signing configuration for JReleaser
jreleaser.yml.description: Configures JReleaser project metadata, GitHub release behavior, changelog formatting, files to include, signing, and Maven deploy target. It ensures releases are signed, published to Maven Central via Sonatype, and uses a preset changelog format for consistent release notes.

insights.title: Add SBOM files and adjust signing config for JReleaser
insights.problem: Want to include BOM outputs (cyclonedx) in release artifacts but avoid signing them in certain runs.
insights.root_cause: Releaser configuration didn't publish SBOM artifacts.
insights.solution: Added target/bom.xml and target/bom.json as artifacts; set signing active to NEVER and added files config.
insights.lesson_learned: Release tooling needs to be aligned with project SBOM generation; ensure signing and artifact lists match expected CI behavior.

insights.title: Update JReleaser project name to rebranded maven-lockfile
insights.problem: jreleaser config still used old name (integrity-maven-plugin) which would produce misleading release metadata.
insights.root_cause: Rebranding across codebase did not update JReleaser metadata.
insights.solution: Change project.name to maven-lockfile and update descriptions.
insights.lesson_learned: Tooling metadata (JReleaser) must be kept in sync with codebase rebrands to produce correct release artifacts.

insights.title: Adjust JReleaser release formatting and options
insights.problem: jreleaser settings included outdated append setting and needed to be simplified for gitmoji formatting.
insights.root_cause: Generated configuration drift and manual edits.
insights.solution: Simplify release block and remove append toggles; set arguments: full-release later in pipeline to control release steps.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep generated release configs concise and validate with dry-run before full release.

qodana.yml.short_description: Qodana static analysis profile for CI
qodana.yml.description: Defines the Qodana inspection profile and groups to include/exclude for static analysis runs. It disables or relaxes specific inspections and excludes generated or vendor paths to reduce noise in CI checks.

renovate.json.short_description: Dependency automation rules for Renovate bot
renovate.json.description: Configures Renovate's update and automerge behavior, commit semantics, package rules and ignores for certain authors and packages. It automates dependency updates, pins action digests, and customizes how different update types are semantically committed and merged.

maven_plugin/mvnw.short_description: Unix shell Maven Wrapper that downloads and runs Maven

insights.title: Lockfile metadata and versions updated across commits

insights.root_cause: Release/version bumps and metadata inclusion (config, metadata) changes.
insights.solution: Updated lockfile.json files and included metadata (osName, mavenVersion, javaVersion) and moved toward including Config contents.
insights.lesson_learned: Lockfiles are treated as part of the system contract; when format changes (new metadata/config fields) ensure backward compatibility and warn when older lockfiles are missing fields.

action.yml.short_description: Composite GitHub Action that runs the Maven lockfile plugin to generate or validate a lockfile
action.yml.description:
This YAML defines a composite GitHub Action named "Maven-Lockfile" that orchestrates checkout, Java setup, change-detection, and invocation of the maven-lockfile Maven plugin to either generate or validate a lockfile.json. It exists to provide a reusable CI step that repositories can call from workflows to ensure their Maven dependency lockfile is kept in sync with pom.xml (and optionally workflow files) and to optionally commit updated lockfiles back to the repository.

The action was designed to be conservative and explicit: the inputs expose behavior that callers need to control (github-token, commit-lockfile, commit-message, include-maven-plugins, lockfile-name, workflow-filename, skip-older-regeneration). The core logic is implemented in shell inside a single run step: it converts inputs to Maven -D properties, examines the git diff to set a POM_CHANGED env var (used to decide generate vs validate), and defines a helper function execute_maven_command to run mvn goals (generate/validate) and report results via GitHub log annotations and the GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY. The action also includes a post-run conditional commit step that uses stefanzweifel/git-auto-commit-action when inputs.commit-lockfile == 'true'.

The action is sensitive to PR contexts: checkout is explicitly provided with ref and repository pointing at the pull request head to ensure the plugin runs against the correct branch/contents. It also contains logic to avoid overwriting lockfiles generated by newer plugin versions (skip-older-regeneration) by parsing .metaData.config.mavenLockfileVersion from the existing lockfile using jq and comparing it with the hard-coded PLUGIN_VERSION (5.14.2) using sort -V. The action maps inputs to -D system properties consumed by the Maven plugin (e.g., includeMavenPlugins and lockfileName) so the plugin receives equivalent configuration when invoked from CI.

key_constructs.name: github-token
key_constructs.purpose: Input token used to checkout the repository and to allow commits from the action.
key_constructs.reasoning: This input is required and determines which token is used for actions/checkout and commit steps; when editing the action ensure downstream steps expecting a token still receive it and that callers understand token permissions.

key_constructs.name: commit-lockfile
key_constructs.purpose: Input flag controlling whether the action will attempt to commit an updated lockfile back to the repository.
key_constructs.reasoning: This input gates the final commit step; actions or automation that rely on committing back must set this to 'true' and provide suitable token permissions.

key_constructs.name: include-maven-plugins
key_constructs.purpose: Input flag passed to the Maven plugin via -DincludeMavenPlugins to include plugins in the generated lockfile.
key_constructs.reasoning: The action converts this to a -D system property for the Maven plugin; callers who depend on plugin-inclusion behavior will toggle this input.

key_constructs.name: lockfile-name
key_constructs.purpose: Input allowing callers to override the lockfile filename written by the Maven plugin (default lockfile.json).
key_constructs.reasoning: This value is forwarded as -DlockfileName and used locally to check for existing lockfile presence; edits must preserve consistent mapping and tests should verify non-default names.

key_constructs.name: workflow-filename
key_constructs.purpose: Input name of workflow file to include in changed-file detection (default Lockfile.yml).
key_constructs.reasoning: This input was introduced to detect changes in repository-level workflow files that impact the lockfile; modifications should maintain the change-detection usage.

key_constructs.name: skip-older-regeneration
key_constructs.purpose: Input flag to prevent the action from regenerating a lockfile if it was generated by a newer plugin version.
key_constructs.reasoning: This prevents older pinned action versions from overwriting lockfiles produced during a release run by newer plugin versions; changing semantics requires care because it affects whether CI will update lockfiles.

key_constructs.name: changed-files
key_constructs.purpose: Step id that runs a shell snippet to set POM_CHANGED in the environment based on git diff.
key_constructs.reasoning: This step is central to the decision whether to run generate vs validate; it relies on correct BASE_REF selection and fetch-depth configuration so edits must preserve its logic or align with alternate changed-file approaches.

key_constructs.name: execute_maven_command
key_constructs.purpose: Shell function defined inside the 'Maven Lockfile Action' run step that executes mvn commands, captures success/failure, and writes summaries/annotations.
key_constructs.reasoning: All mvn invocations route through this helper to ensure consistent messaging and summary formatting. Agents editing the run script should preserve its parameters and exit semantics to retain proper action behavior and summary content.

key_constructs.name: PLUGIN_VERSION
key_constructs.purpose: Hard-coded plugin coordinate version (5.14.2) that the action invokes when running the maven-lockfile plugin.
key_constructs.reasoning: This constant determines which plugin artifact/version the action runs. Changing this value changes the public behavior of the action and can affect skip-older-regeneration comparisons and caller expectations; treat it as part of the action's public contract.

key_constructs.name: COMMIT_UPDATED_LOCKFILE
key_constructs.purpose: Environment variable set from inputs.commit-lockfile used to conditionally run the commit step and to compute whether to generate vs validate.
key_constructs.reasoning: This environment variable is relied upon across steps; case and exact naming must be preserved to avoid breakage (history shows case-sensitivity issues).

key_constructs.name: POM_CHANGED
key_constructs.purpose: Environment variable set by the changed-files step indicating whether pom.xml or the workflow-filename changed in the PR/commit range.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to decide generate vs validate. The exact name (POM_CHANGED) is expected by the run-step shell logic and must not be renamed without updating all readers.

key_constructs.name: SKIP_OLDER_REGENERATION
key_constructs.purpose: Local variable read from input skip-older-regeneration used to decide whether to skip generation when an existing lockfile was produced by a newer plugin version.
key_constructs.reasoning: Alters whether the action overwrites an existing lockfile; it's part of backwards-compatibility behavior and should be handled carefully when adjusting version-compare logic.

key_behaviors: checks out pull request head contents
detects changed pom.xml or configured workflow file and sets POM_CHANGED
maps inputs to Maven -D properties for plugin invocation
runs maven-lockfile:generate or :validate depending on change-detection and commit settings
optionally commits updated lockfile back to repo using git-auto-commit-action
skips regeneration if existing lockfile was generated by a newer plugin version

pitfalls.mistake: Changing the environment variable name POM_CHANGED (or its casing) without updating all consumers.
pitfalls.consequence: The run-step shell logic will not detect changed POMs, causing incorrect generate/validate decisions and possible CI failures or missed updates.
pitfalls.prevention: Retain the exact POM_CHANGED name and casing when editing the action or update all references consistently.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering PLUGIN_VERSION or its comparison flow without considering skip-older-regeneration semantics.
pitfalls.consequence: Older pinned actions could overwrite newer lockfiles or the action could incorrectly skip valid regenerations; this changes public behavior of the action consumers rely on.
pitfalls.prevention: Treat PLUGIN_VERSION as part of the action's public contract; any version bump should include verification of the skip-older-regeneration logic and documentation updates.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing repository/ref fields from the actions/checkout step or changing how checkout is performed in PR contexts.
pitfalls.consequence: CI may check out the wrong commit (merge commit or base branch) instead of the PR head, causing the plugin to run against incorrect code and producing invalid or unexpected lockfiles.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve explicit repository and ref usage for PR runs, and ensure fetch-depth is appropriate (fetch-depth: 0 is used here).

pitfalls.mistake: Breaking the execute_maven_command function signature or exit behavior (e.g., not returning non-zero on failure).
pitfalls.consequence: Errors from mvn goals may be swallowed or incorrectly reported, leading to false successes or confusing step summaries.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the function's contract: print command, run mvn, emit notice/error annotations, write to GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY, and return non-zero on failure.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming jq is present or that sorting with 'sort -V' behaves the same on all runners.
pitfalls.consequence: Version comparison or jq extraction may fail, preventing proper skip-older-regeneration behavior or producing shell errors.
pitfalls.prevention: When modifying logic that parses existing lockfile metadata or compares versions, respect that these commands must be available on the runner or add fallback handling consistent with existing behavior.

reading_guide.start_here: inputs (top-level) and the 'Maven Lockfile Action' run step
key_sections: inputs: defines public contract and options callers set
checkout repository step: ensures correct repo/ref/token for PRs
changed-files (id: changed-files): computes POM_CHANGED and chooses BASE_REF
Maven Lockfile Action run step: maps inputs to -D args, define execute_maven_command, decides generate vs validate, and implements skip-older-regeneration logic
commit-lockfile step: conditional auto-commit using git-auto-commit-action
skip_unless_needed: print POM-CHANGED step (simple debug print)
detailed echo lines that only affect logging/summary

related_files.relationship: The Maven plugin goal invoked by this action (io.github.chains-project:maven-lockfile:GENERATE). Changes to how the action maps inputs to -D properties or to the expected plugin version should be cross-checked here.
related_files.reason_to_check: If altering input names, default property mappings, or PLUGIN_VERSION, verify the plugin mojos accept the same properties and behavior.

related_files.relationship: The validate goal invoked when no POM changes or generation is skipped. Ensure that action-side validation semantics align with what this mojo expects.
related_files.reason_to_check: If the action changes when it invokes validate or changes flags passed, verify the mojo's config and exit codes still match expectations.

related_files.relationship: Default workflow filename referenced by the workflow-filename input and the changed-files detection.
related_files.reason_to_check: If callers change the default workflow filename or semantics of workflow files that affect lockfile generation, update the action input defaults or callers accordingly.

tests_to_run: mvn test (run plugin module tests locally to ensure plugin behavior unchanged)
Run the composite action in a test repository (or use act) to validate checkout, change-detection and commit flow
data_constants_to_check: PLUGIN_VERSION constant value matches released maven-lockfile plugin artifact
Default input values (lockfile-name, workflow-filename, commit-lockfile, skip-older-regeneration)

insights.title: GitHub Action: allow regenerating lockfile when workflow file changes
insights.problem: Changes to the workflow file could modify behaviour that should trigger lockfile regeneration, but the action only watched pom.xml by default.
insights.root_cause: The action's changed-files check didn't include workflow filename, so PRs that only changed workflow wouldn't regenerate lockfile.
insights.solution: Add a workflow-filename input (default Lockfile.yml) and include **/${{ inputs.workflow-filename }} in the files watched for changes.
insights.lesson_learned: When using repository-level workflow files that influence build outputs, include them in change-detection logic for dependent CI actions.

insights.title: Expose include-maven-plugins input for GitHub Action
insights.problem: Users couldn't request plugin inclusion when running the Action.
insights.root_cause: Action inputs were missing an option to include maven plugins in lockfile.
insights.solution: Added include-maven-plugins input (default false).
insights.lesson_learned: Action inputs should mirror important plugin options; keep defaults conservative to avoid unexpected changes in consumers' workflows.

insights.title: Expose commit message and commit author inputs for the GitHub Action
insights.problem: The composite action hard-coded EndBug/add-and-commit defaults (author) and did not allow customizing the commit message, making commits non-configurable and potentially causing incorrect attribution.
insights.root_cause: Action inputs lacked fields for commit message and commit author; values passed to EndBug/add-and-commit were static.
insights.solution: Added inputs commit-message and commit-author and wired them to EndBug/add-and-commit's message and default_author parameters.
insights.lesson_learned: Make action inputs configurable for attributes that affect VCS commits (message, author) to support different CI workflows and correct attribution. When using third-party actions, expose their key parameters instead of hard-coding them.

insights.title: Checkout step needs explicit repository when running on PRs from forks
insights.problem: Checkout used implicit default repository; when running on PRs from forks this can lead to checking out the wrong repository/refs.
insights.root_cause: The actions/checkout step omitted the repository field, relying on implicit defaults that differ for forked PRs.
insights.solution: Added repository: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name }} to the checkout step to explicitly target the PR head repository.
insights.lesson_learned: Always specify repository and ref explicitly in workflows that must act on PR head forks to avoid ambiguous checkout behavior across different event contexts.

insights.title: Fix wrong environment variable name
insights.problem: Environment variable name used in the action steps was inconsistent (POM-CHANGED vs POM_CHANGED / pom-changed), causing downstream Java action logic that reads POM_CHANGED to not detect changed POMs.
insights.root_cause: Case-sensitive and inconsistent naming of environment variables across action steps and the action's runtime logic.
insights.solution: Normalized the environment variable to POM_CHANGED in the action writer step and consumers; updated prints and step names to use the consistent env var name. Also added step to set ref for checkout in PRs.
insights.lesson_learned: Environment variable names in GitHub Actions are case-sensitive and must be consistently set/read. When other components (e.g., the Java GitHub Action) expect a specific env var, the workflow must set that exact name.

insights.title: Add commit-lockfile option and improved change detection flow
insights.problem: There was no configurable way to have the composite action commit updated lockfiles back to the repository; previous logic sometimes ran generation/validation unconditionally.
insights.root_cause: Missing input and conditional commit step; action relied on inline logic rather than exposing a commit option.
insights.solution: Added inputs.commit-lockfile (default true) and a conditional EndBug/add-and-commit step. Added better changed-files detection and set a POM_CHANGED env var, plus printing of changed-files for debugging.
insights.lesson_learned: Expose CI behavior as explicit inputs (like commit-lockfile) instead of hardcoding; always surface the changed-files result for easier debugging. Ensure conditional commit steps check both the input and the changed-files output.

insights.title: Checkout PR HEAD ref for correct source when running action
insights.problem: Checkout step did not specify ref for pull request head, which could lead to wrong branch being checked out
insights.root_cause: actions/checkout default behavior without specifying ref can check out the merge commit instead of the PR head; the action needed the PR head ref to run correctly against user changes.
insights.solution: Set the checkout 'ref' to ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }} for PR runs.
insights.lesson_learned: When running composite actions against PRs, explicitly specify the ref in checkout if you need the PR branch contents (not the merge commit).

insights.title: Switch to tj-actions/changed-files to detect changed poms and branch behavior
insights.problem: Previously the composite action used in-Java/JGit detection, or always executed the same jbang invocation. This made the action heavier and required more environment setup.
insights.root_cause: In-process detection using JGit required checkout depth, complicated setup, and maintenance of Git logic inside the action.
insights.solution: Use the popular tj-actions/changed-files GH Action to provide a changed-files step (outputs.any_changed) and conditionally run jbang with action=generate or action=validate accordingly.
insights.lesson_learned: Offload repository-introspection responsibilities to dedicated GH Actions; this simplifies the action code, avoids native dependencies and reduces maintenance.

insights.title: Changed jbang coordinate invocations and conditional behavior
insights.problem: Action's jbang invocation command changed coordinates (artifact id and versions) across commits; also behavior regarding when to run generate vs validate changed based on the changed-files output. This changes the action's runtime semantics.
insights.root_cause: Ongoing release/versioning work and refactors to detection logic.
insights.solution: Updated the composite action to call the right artifact (maven-lockfile-github-action) and to conditionalize based on the new changed-files step.
insights.lesson_learned: When changing conditional behavior in composite actions, ensure documentation and examples are updated and that consumers of the action are aware of any semantic changes.

insights.title: Checkout the pull request head ref instead of base ref
insights.problem: Composite action default checkout used the repository default ref; when running in PR context the step needed to check out the PR head ref to run tests against PR changes.
insights.root_cause: The actions/checkout step was not configured with the PR head ref and composite action YAML indentation/structure was wrong in one patch causing potential syntax issues.
insights.solution: Set the checkout step to use ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}.
insights.lesson_learned: When writing composite actions used in PR contexts, explicitly check out the PR head ref. Also validate YAML indentation (incorrect indentation in commit diffs hints at problems).

pom.xml.short_description: Parent POM that centralizes build configuration, plugins, and reproducibility settings for the project
pom.xml.description:
This file is the root parent POM for the maven-lockfile project. It exists to centralize project metadata (groupId, artifactId, version, name, description, scm, licenses, developers), declare modules (maven_plugin), and to define build-time behavior via plugin configuration and global properties. The POM encodes reproducibility settings (project.build.outputTimestamp), CI/packaging behavior (cyclonedx SBOM generation during package phase), resource templating (maven-resources-plugin to copy and filter template/action.yml into the repository root), automated embedding of action content (gmavenplus-plugin executing a Groovy script in generate-resources), code-style/format enforcement (spotless-maven-plugin), artifact signing control (sigstore-maven-plugin toggled via property), and enforcement of plugin versioning policy (maven-enforcer-plugin requirePluginVersions rule).

Because this is the parent POM, it is authoritative for module coordinates and many build-related behaviors: plugin versions, execution phases, and resource filtering are specified here and will affect all modules that inherit from this POM. Several plugins are executed in specific Maven phases (e.g., cyclonedx in package, gmavenplus in generate-resources, cyclonedx makeBom execution) and their configuration influences outputs that downstream tooling (CI, lockfile generator, release automation) expects to exist. The POM intentionally sets deterministic output metadata via project.build.outputTimestamp to support reproducible builds and checksum stability; changes to that property or the listed plugin versions will affect produced artifact checksums and may break validations elsewhere (lockfile checks, SBOM comparisons).

key_constructs.name: project.build.outputTimestamp
key_constructs.purpose: Provides a fixed timestamp used by the build to make artifact outputs reproducible.
key_constructs.reasoning: This property is used by Maven and plugins to normalize timestamps; altering it changes produced artifact checksums and should be treated as authoritative for reproducibility.

key_constructs.name: sigstore.skip
key_constructs.purpose: Property used to conditionally skip sigstore-maven-plugin signing executions.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to disable signing in CI or local builds; toggling this affects whether artifacts are signed during the build lifecycle.

key_constructs.name: modules
key_constructs.purpose: Declares child modules of this multi-module Maven project (maven_plugin).
key_constructs.reasoning: Module list is authoritative for reactor builds; adding/removing modules changes build scope.

key_constructs.name: cyclonedx-maven-plugin
key_constructs.purpose: Plugin configured to produce SBOM (BOM) during package phase.
key_constructs.reasoning: Invocation produces BOM files that are expected by CI and release processes; its configuration (includeTestScope) matters for what gets recorded.

key_constructs.name: gmavenplus-plugin
key_constructs.purpose: Executes a Groovy script in generate-resources to read .github/workflows/Lockfile.yml into project properties.
key_constructs.reasoning: This execution is used to prepare repository files (action content) and must run in generate-resources; altering its script or phase changes generated resources.

key_constructs.name: maven-resources-plugin
key_constructs.purpose: Copies and filters template files (action.yml) from template/ to project root using filtering.
key_constructs.reasoning: This expands templates into repository root: filtering placeholders are applied; file paths and filtering variables are significant and can overwrite repository files.

key_constructs.name: spotless-maven-plugin
key_constructs.purpose: Enforces/auto-applies formatting rules (including pom sorting) for code and the POM itself.
key_constructs.reasoning: Spotless can reformat files and sort the POM; changes to formatting settings will alter committed files and may be enforced in CI.

key_constructs.name: maven-enforcer-plugin
key_constructs.purpose: Enforces best-practices about plugin versions (requirePluginVersions rule in specific phases).
key_constructs.reasoning: The enforcer is configured with rules that will fail builds if plugin versions are missing or are snapshots/latest in specific phases; edits that remove explicit plugin versions may cause CI or release builds to fail.

key_behaviors: centralizes plugin versions and build settings for child modules
produces SBOM during package phase
embeds action content by copying and filtering templates into repository root
controls artifact signing via sigstore.skip

pitfalls.mistake: Changing project.build.outputTimestamp without coordinating dependent systems
pitfalls.consequence: Build artifacts and checksums will change, potentially invalidating lockfiles, CI validations, and reproducibility guarantees.
pitfalls.prevention: Treat outputTimestamp as authoritative; update downstream lockfiles and CI expectations if this value must change.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or omitting explicit plugin versions referenced by enforcer
pitfalls.consequence: maven-enforcer-plugin may fail builds in enforce phases (clean, deploy, site) causing CI failures.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve explicit plugin versions or update enforcer configuration if changes are intended.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying the gmavenplus script or maven-resources filtering without checking outputs
pitfalls.consequence: Generated action.yml or other generated resources may be incorrect or overwritten, affecting CI workflows or action behavior.
pitfalls.prevention: Validate generate-resources outputs locally (inspect action.yml and generated files) after any change to scripts or resource templates.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering cyclonedx plugin configuration (phase or includeTestScope) without updating consumers
pitfalls.consequence: Produced SBOM contents will differ (e.g., include/exclude test-scope artifacts) causing mismatch in security scans or release artifacts.
pitfalls.prevention: Coordinate SBOM consumers and CI when changing cyclonedx configuration; run package to verify BOM outputs.

pitfalls.mistake: Allowing maven-resources-plugin filtering to overwrite repository files unintentionally
pitfalls.consequence: Important files at repository root (like action.yml) may be replaced with unexpected content.
pitfalls.prevention: Check the outputDirectory configuration and resource include patterns before running resource copy; run copy-resources in a safe environment first.

reading_guide.start_here: properties: review project.build.outputTimestamp and sigstore.skip to understand reproducibility and signing behavior
key_sections: modules: which submodules are part of reactor builds (maven_plugin)
build -> plugins -> cyclonedx-maven-plugin: SBOM generation configuration and execution phase
build -> plugins -> gmavenplus-plugin: Groovy script that reads .github/workflows/Lockfile.yml into properties
build -> plugins -> maven-resources-plugin: template filtering settings and outputDirectory
build -> plugins -> spotless-maven-plugin and maven-enforcer-plugin: formatting and enforcement rules that can break CI
skip_unless_needed: plugin entries for maven-jar-plugin, maven-install-plugin, maven-clean-plugin, maven-surefire-plugin when not debugging packaging or test execution details

related_files.relationship: child module declared by modules section; inherits plugin configuration and properties from parent
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to parent plugins or properties (e.g., outputTimestamp, sigstore.skip, plugin versions) will affect how the maven_plugin module builds and may require coordinated updates.

related_files.relationship: Referenced and read by gmavenplus-plugin script (Groovy) to produce action content property; influences generated action.yml
related_files.reason_to_check: Modifying the workflow file or Groovy script can change the generated action content embedded by the build; ensure consistency if either is edited.

related_files.relationship: Template resource copied and filtered into repository root by maven-resources-plugin
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to template placeholders or filtering variables require updates to resource filtering configuration and may change action.yml contents placed at project root.

tests_to_run: mvn -DskipTests package (to exercise cyclonedx and resource generation)
mvn test (to ensure tests in modules still pass)
mvn spotless:apply (if formatting changes are made) and verify no CI formatting failures

insights.title: Add cyclonedx plugin and change mavenVersion property
insights.problem: Need to generate SBOMs (BOM) during package phase and ensure plugin list recorded.
insights.root_cause: SBOM generation not configured in project build.
insights.solution: Added cyclonedx-maven-plugin configuration to pom.xml and adjusted mavenVersion property (note: in one commit changed mavenVersion to 3.2.5 which may be surprising).
insights.lesson_learned: When adding build tooling that produces additional outputs (SBOMs), ensure lockfiles or release configs account for the new artifacts. Verify that mavenVersion changes are intentional as they affect metadata recorded in lockfiles.

insights.title: Make builds reproducible by fixing output timestamps
insights.problem: Build artifacts contained non-deterministic timestamps which prevented reproducible outputs and deterministic checksums.
insights.root_cause: No project.build.outputTimestamp was set; build outputs get current timestamps by default.
insights.solution: Set <project.build.outputTimestamp>2023-01-01T00:00:00Z</project.build.outputTimestamp> and add the maven-artifact-plugin to stabilize artifact metadata.
insights.lesson_learned: Set project.build.outputTimestamp (and other reproducibility settings) if deterministic artifacts and checksums are required for validation in CI or lockfiles.

insights.title: Use maven-resources-plugin to generate README and action.yml from templates
insights.problem: Previously documentation generation used ad-hoc sed commands which were brittle and platform dependent.
insights.root_cause: Doc generation relied on shell commands inside workflows; these were error-prone and harder to maintain.
insights.solution: Added maven-resources-plugin configuration to copy & filter files from template/ into repository root, making doc generation controllable via mvn resources:copy-resources.
insights.lesson_learned: Prefer build-tool-native templating (maven-resources-plugin) over shell sed invocations for cross-platform, reproducible doc/resource generation.

insights.title: Improve parent POM metadata and formatting sanity fixes
insights.problem: Parent POM previously used deprecated/malformed schema location and lacked common project metadata (scm, developers, licenses) used for releases.
insights.root_cause: Initial quick scaffold left out standard fields.
insights.solution: Update xsi:schemaLocation to Maven XSD https URL, add scm/developers/licenses, bump version to 1.0.0, and normalize formatting for plugins (spotless config).
insights.lesson_learned: Keep parent POMs complete to support release tooling (jreleaser) and ensure generated artifacts contain expected metadata.

insights.title: Parent POM metadata updated and project rebrand
insights.problem: Parent pom contained legacy project name and missing SCM/license metadata after rebrand.
insights.root_cause: Rebrand and release preparation required updating parent POM coordinates and metadata.
insights.solution: Set parent version to 1.0.0, update project name to maven-lockfile, add licenses, developers, and SCM blocks as needed.
insights.lesson_learned: Parent POM is authoritative for module coordinates and metadata — ensure changes are kept consistent to avoid release issues.

insights.title: Add provided-scoped Maven dependencies required for plugin compilation
insights.problem: Plugin compilation may fail without providing maven-model and maven-artifact on provided scope.
insights.root_cause: Maven runtime classes used by the plugin need to be available for compilation but should not be packaged.
insights.solution: Add org.apache.maven:maven-model and org.apache.maven:maven-artifact with scope provided to the root pom dependencies.
insights.lesson_learned: When building Maven plugins, mark core Maven APIs as provided to avoid bundling them and to allow compilation.

maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.short_description: High-level orchestration to build a LockFile: dependency graph, plugin metadata, POM chain and BOMs.
maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.description:
LockFileFacade is the central orchestration component used by the Mojos to construct the LockFile JSON model for a Maven project. It exists to collect the full dependency graph (using Maven's DependencyCollectorBuilder), transform that into the project's DependencyGraph (with checksums attached via an AbstractChecksumCalculator), optionally collect build plugin metadata (including plugin dependency sub-graphs and resolved repository information), build the chain of parent POMs (local or external) with checksums or resolved URLs, and resolve BOMs for the project and dependency nodes.

This class purposefully keeps high-level assembly logic and delegates low-level resolution and I/O to helper classes: ProjectBuilder (build plugin MavenProject from coordinates), BomResolver (resolve BOM POMs and attach them to nodes), and AbstractChecksumCalculator implementations (calculate checksums and provide resolved repository information). The design separates graph construction (minimal visitor that only wires edges) from node resolution and checksum calculation which occurs inside DependencyGraph.of(...). The facade is used by GenerateLockFileMojo and other components to serialize a deterministic LockFile instance.

Key design decisions visible in this file: (1) deterministic ordering via TreeSet for roots/plugins using comparators on DependencyNode comparator strings; (2) optional inclusion of plugins controlled by metadata.getConfig().isIncludeMavenPlugins(); (3) several failure-tolerant catch-all blocks that log warnings and return empty graphs/sets rather than throwing; (4) external POMs are handled by constructing a synthetic 'pom' Artifact (DefaultArtifact) so checksum/resolved-url can be obtained consistently via checksumCalculator. The file also uses PluginLogManager (set by Mojos) for logging so that messages integrate with Maven's build output.

key_constructs.name: LockFileFacade
key_constructs.purpose: High-level static facade that constructs a LockFile from a MavenProject and runtime inputs.
key_constructs.reasoning: Any edits must preserve its role as the single orchestration point that glues together dependency graph creation, plugin resolution, POM chain construction, checksum calculation, and BOM resolution. Call sites (Mojos) expect a static API that returns a fully-populated LockFile instance.

key_constructs.name: GraphBuildingNodeVisitor
key_constructs.purpose: DependencyNodeVisitor implementation that adds directed edges between dependency graph nodes into a Guava MutableGraph.
key_constructs.reasoning: This visitor intentionally does minimal work (only adding edges) to decouple graph building from heavy resolution. Modifying it to perform resolution or I/O would violate the separation of concerns relied upon elsewhere (DependencyGraph.of handles checksum/resolution).

key_constructs.name: getLockFilePath
key_constructs.purpose: Return filesystem Path for a lockfile name relative to project base directory.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by Mojos to determine where to write/read lockfile JSON. It uses project.getBasedir() and lockfileName; callers assume the result is on disk and writable by Maven process.

key_constructs.name: generateLockFileFromProject
key_constructs.purpose: Main entrypoint: create the LockFile model for the supplied MavenProject using the given dependency collector, checksum calculator and metadata.
key_constructs.reasoning: This method orchestrates plugin collection, dependency graph building (including reduced mode), POM chain construction and BOM resolution, and assembles the final LockFile object. Changes here must preserve metadata.getConfig() semantics, deterministic ordering for sets, and exception-tolerant behavior expected by Mojos and tests.

key_constructs.name: getAllPlugins
key_constructs.purpose: Collect build plugins declared/used by the project and produce a Set<MavenPlugin> with resolved checksum and dependency roots.
key_constructs.reasoning: This function merges project-level user-declared plugin dependency overrides into the plugin POM's dependencies, uses ProjectBuilder to build the plugin MavenProject, and then resolves plugin dependency graphs. It relies on checksumCalculator to provide repository information and plugin checksum. Maintain consistent merging behavior (user overrides) and scope filtering semantics for plugin dependencies.

key_constructs.name: resolvePluginDependencies
key_constructs.purpose: Build a temporary MavenProject for a plugin artifact, merge user overrides, collect its dependency graph (compile+runtime scopes), and return root DependencyNode wrappers.
key_constructs.reasoning: This routine calls ProjectBuilder.buildFromGav, sets up a ProjectBuildingRequest, and uses DependencyCollectorBuilder.collectDependencyGraph with a ScopeArtifactFilter("compile+runtime"). It returns roots from DependencyGraph.of(...). The method swallows exceptions and logs warnings; tests depend on no-throw semantics and recorded dependency scopes.

key_constructs.name: graph
key_constructs.purpose: Collect the project's dependency graph via DependencyCollectorBuilder and convert it into a DependencyGraph instance using the provided checksumCalculator and reduced flag.
key_constructs.reasoning: Graph builds a Guava MutableGraph of org.apache.maven.shared.dependency.graph.DependencyNode via GraphBuildingNodeVisitor and passes it into DependencyGraph.of(...). It logs node count and returns an empty graph on exceptions — this behavior is relied upon for graceful failure in tests and CI.

key_constructs.name: constructRecursivePom
key_constructs.purpose: Create a Pom data structure representing the project and its parent chain (local relative paths for local parents; resolved URL/repoId and checksum for external POMs).
key_constructs.reasoning: This function walks parent chain from child to root, reverses the list and creates nested Pom objects. For external parents (project.getFile() == null) it synthesizes a 'pom' Artifact and uses checksumCalculator.calculateArtifactChecksum and getArtifactResolvedField to fill resolved/repo fields. For local POM files it uses checksumCalculator.calculatePomChecksum. The code carefully constructs relative paths relative to the initialProject base directory.

key_constructs.name: resolveBoms
key_constructs.purpose: Delegate BOM resolution to BomResolver, mutating dependency graph nodes with BOM information and returning project's BOM list.
key_constructs.reasoning: This method instantiates BomResolver with the project's remote artifact repositories and checksumCalculator and calls resolver.resolveBomsForDependencies(graph) and resolver.resolveForProject(project). Because BomResolver mutates graph nodes, callers must expect side-effects on the DependencyGraph passed in.

key_behaviors: generates a LockFile model from a MavenProject
collects the project's dependency graph and converts it to DependencyGraph
constructs recursive parent POM chain with checksums/resolved urls
optionally collects maven build plugin metadata and plugin dependency roots
delegates BOM resolution and attaches BOMs to graph and project

pitfalls.mistake: Assume graph construction does resolution or performs checksum calculation inside GraphBuildingNodeVisitor.
pitfalls.consequence: Introducing resolution or I/O into the visitor breaks the intended separation and may duplicate work or cause ordering/parallelism assumptions to fail.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the visitor minimal; rely on DependencyGraph.of(...)/checksumCalculator for resolution.

pitfalls.mistake: Throw exceptions instead of logging and returning empty collections when the dependency collector or plugin resolution fails.
pitfalls.consequence: Mojos and tests expect failure-tolerant behavior; changing this will make builds error out unexpectedly (tests assert success/failure semantics).
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve existing try/catch behavior that logs warnings and returns empty graphs/sets.

pitfalls.mistake: Modify how external POMs are represented (e.g., change the synthesized DefaultArtifact packaging or classifier).
pitfalls.consequence: ChecksumCalculator implementations and downstream serialization expect a consistent artifact type for external POM checksums; altering may break checksum lookup/resolution.
pitfalls.prevention: When constructing the synthetic Pom artifact for external POMs, keep packaging 'pom' and maintain use of DefaultArtifact with artifact handler.

pitfalls.mistake: Change deterministic ordering logic for roots/plugins (TreeSet with comparator on comparatorString).
pitfalls.consequence: Lockfile determinism and equality tests (LockfileTest.shouldLockFilesEqualWhenOrderIsChanged) will break and consumers of serialized lockfiles may see non-deterministic diffs.
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain sorted set construction and comparators used to build roots and plugin sets.

pitfalls.mistake: Assume resolveBoms is pure and does not mutate the DependencyGraph.
pitfalls.consequence: Code that expects graph to be immutable will be surprised; tests and downstream serialization rely on resolver having attached BOM data to nodes.
pitfalls.prevention: Treat BomResolver.resolveBomsForDependencies(graph) as side-effecting; preserve calls and expectations.

key_sections: getAllPlugins: collects plugin artifacts, merges user-declared plugin dependency overrides, and attaches plugin checksum and repo info
graph: collects project dependency graph and converts it to DependencyGraph.of(...), note reduced flag usage
constructRecursivePom: builds nested Pom objects for the project and parents, handles external vs local POMs
skip_unless_needed: GraphBuildingNodeVisitor: simple edge-building visitor (boilerplate)
getLockFilePath: trivial path helper

relevant_snippets.description: Asserts lockfile equality is order-insensitive and validates deterministic ordering semantics (uses Set of DependencyNode and MavenPlugin).
Integration tests that exercise plugin collection and plugin dependency resolution (pluginProject, pluginUserDependency), and basic graph/checksum behaviors.

related_files.relationship: DependencyGraph.of is the consumer of the Guava graph produced here and is responsible for creating richer DependencyNode models with attached checksums. Changes in graph construction must be compatible with DependencyGraph.of.
related_files.reason_to_check: If the graph building visitor or node types are changed, DependencyGraph.of may need updates to maintain checksum attachment and reduced-mode filtering.

related_files.relationship: LockFileFacade delegates all checksum work to implementations of this interface; it also calls getPluginResolvedField/getArtifactResolvedField/calculatePluginChecksum/calculatePomChecksum.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any changes to how checksums or resolved fields are requested must be compatible with the calculator API; tests assume deterministic algorithm strings and behavior.

related_files.relationship: Used to build plugin MavenProject from gav coordinates in resolvePluginDependencies; critical for plugin metadata resolution.
related_files.reason_to_check: If plugin-project-building behavior changes (search order, fallbacks), plugin dependency resolution semantics and tests (pluginProject) will be impacted.

related_files.relationship: resolveBoms delegates BOM resolution to BomResolver which mutates the dependency graph nodes and returns project-level BOMs.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any changes to BOM handling need alignment with LockFileFacade.resolveBoms and the expectation that graph nodes are mutated with BOM info.

related_files.relationship: LockFileFacade uses PluginLogManager.getLog() for all logging; Mojos set this log to integrate with Maven.
related_files.reason_to_check: Modifying logging approach here requires keeping compatibility with how Mojos inject Maven's Log into PluginLogManager so build output remains consistent.

related_files.relationship: Final LockFile object is assembled here (LockFile constructor invoked). Serialization consumers/tests depend on the structure produced by LockFileFacade.
related_files.reason_to_check: If LockFile structure changes, tests and Mojos that read/write lockfile.json must be updated accordingly.

tests_to_run: Run the LockfileTest unit tests class
data_constants_to_check: MetaData.Config checks for includeMavenPlugins, reduced, checksumAlgorithm and checksumMode
Lockfile name used by getLockFilePath in Mojos (lockfile.json default)

insights.title: Extracted BOM and project-building logic into resolvers/builders
insights.problem: LockFileFacade contained bespoke, duplicated, and fragile logic to locate plugin and POM artifacts (local repo path heuristics, manual artifact resolution via container lookups). BOMs were not resolved/included previously.
insights.root_cause: Mixing high-level orchestration with low-level artifact resolution and repository lookups made the code hard to maintain and reuse.
insights.solution: Introduce BomResolver and ProjectBuilder classes and delegate plugin/POM resolution to them; replace inline plugin POM resolution code with ProjectBuilder.buildFromGav; call BomResolver to resolve BOMs and attach them to the graph and project-level boms.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep high-level orchestration (LockFileFacade) focused on assembly; push artifact/PO M resolution and repo logic into small, testable helpers. When adding new data (BOMs), add a dedicated resolver to avoid scattered logic.

insights.title: Pre-warm checksum cache for remote calculator
insights.problem: Remote checksum download is network-bound and could be serialized causing slow lockfile generation.
insights.root_cause: Checksum calculation ran per-node without a chance to parallelize or prefetch remote metadata.
insights.solution: Collect unique artifacts in DependencyGraph and call checksumCalculator.prewarmArtifactCache(uniqueArtifacts) to let RemoteChecksumCalculator fetch checksums in parallel.
insights.lesson_learned: Provide a prewarm hook on heavy I/O components and call it from the graph assembly path to increase throughput.

insights.title: Record Maven plugin metadata and (later) plugin dependency trees
insights.problem: Lockfile initially didn't include plugin dependency trees or full resolved repository info for plugins.
insights.root_cause: Lack of plugin POM resolution and traversal logic; plugin artifacts and their POMs require dedicated resolution using the Maven session and project repositories.
insights.solution: Added getAllPlugins and resolvePluginDependencies to build plugin MavenProject from plugin POMs (attempt jar-local POM, local repo, then resolve via ArtifactResolver), used DependencyCollectorBuilder to collect plugin dependency graph; register plugin dependency roots into MavenPlugin model.
insights.lesson_learned: Resolving plugin dependencies requires using the session, proper ProjectBuildingRequest (plugin repositories), and fallbacks; watch out for multiple ways POM can be located (jar sibling .pom, local repo path, resolver).

insights.title: Switch LockFileFacade logging to PluginLogManager
insights.problem: LockFileFacade used log4j Logger which doesn't integrate with Mojo getLog() context.
insights.root_cause: Mixed logging APIs produce logs not attached to Maven's build output.
insights.solution: Replace Logger calls with PluginLogManager.getLog() and ensure Mojos set the log via PluginLogManager.setLog(getLog()).
insights.lesson_learned: Ensure long-lived utility classes used by Mojos obtain the Maven-provided Log before logging to keep helpful context and consistent output in CI.

insights.title: LockFileFacade now includes plugin dependency nodes and repository info
insights.problem: Lockfile JSON structure and MavenPlugin model were extended with additional fields (resolved url, repository id, plugin dependencies), changing serialized output and equality semantics.
insights.root_cause: Feature additions (record repository id & plugin dependency graph) required adding extra fields to MavenPlugin and dependency nodes.
insights.solution: Updated MavenPlugin constructor/fields and LockFile assembly to include new data. Tests updated to reflect new serialized fields.
insights.lesson_learned: Adding fields to lockfile JSON is a breaking change for consumers that parse lockfile; when evolving the model, ensure backward compatibility (e.g. default empty values) and update JsonUtils adapters accordingly.

insights.title: Switch dependencies/plugins collections to Sets and use checksum-based ordering
insights.problem: LockFile equality and diffs were order-sensitive and fragile.
insights.root_cause: Lists represented dependencies and plugins — equality/serialization depended on insertion order.
insights.solution: Return Set<DependencyNode> and Set<MavenPlugin> for LockFile; collect roots/plugins into TreeSet using checksum comparator to achieve deterministic ordering and order-insensitive equality.
insights.lesson_learned: Use sorted sets for serialized configuration artifacts to make lockfiles deterministic and order-independent.

insights.title: Use MetaData.getConfig rather than separate Config param
insights.problem: Different code paths passed Config separately; metadata/config coupling caused inconsistencies.
insights.root_cause: Earlier API passed Config and Metadata separately; later refactor introduced MetaData wrapper but some callsites still used old signatures.
insights.solution: Update generateLockFileFromProject to accept MetaData (environment+config) and use metadata.getConfig() inside; adjust callsites accordingly.
insights.lesson_learned: When introducing grouped metadata types, update all callsites and centralize usage to avoid mismatch between config and metadata.

insights.title: Switch generateLockFileFromProject to accept Config and reduced flag; use DependencyNode-based graph
insights.problem: Generation API used multiple booleans and raw artifact types making it harder to evolve (e.g., add reduced mode).
insights.root_cause: Tightly coupled arguments (includeMavenPlugins, reduced) were passed individually and the graph used raw Artifact nodes.
insights.solution: Change generateLockFileFromProject signature to accept a Config object; change internal graph representation to MutableGraph<DependencyNode> and thread reduced flag into DependencyGraph.of().
insights.lesson_learned: Use a configuration object for extensible APIs; prefer strongly-typed graph node types over raw Artifact objects to carry richer metadata (scope, selectedVersion). Note: this is a breaking API change for internal callers.

insights.title: Graph building now uses DependencyNode objects and propagates reduced flag
insights.problem: Previously graph visitor added edges between raw Artifact objects. That limited the metadata available during node construction.
insights.root_cause: Artifact-only graphs can't carry computed info (selectedVersion, scope) needed by downstream lockfile nodes.
insights.solution: Graph building now uses DependencyNode nodes and visitors add edges between DependencyNode objects; the reduced flag is applied in DependencyGraph.of to filter nodes.
insights.lesson_learned: When changing a central data structure (graph node type), multiple files (graph, DependencyGraph, visitors) must be updated together — watch for shotgun changes.

insights.title: Decouple graph building from dependency resolution and add checksum integration
insights.problem: Previous visitor resolved artifacts (via DependencyResolver) while building the graph, coupling graph creation and resolution logic.
insights.root_cause: Resolving artifacts inside the visitor added complexity and unnecessary coupling.
insights.solution: Replaced ResolvingDependencyNodeVisitor with GraphBuildingNodeVisitor that simply builds edges using the artifacts in the dependency tree. Flow now passes an AbstractChecksumCalculator into DependencyGraph.of so checksum calculation happens during node creation, not during graph traversal.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep graph construction responsibilities minimal — don't mix heavy resolution with traversal; pass required services to the next layer for explicit control.

insights.title: Optional inclusion of Maven build plugins into lockfile
insights.problem: Lock files previously contained only runtime/compile dependencies and missed build plugin information which can influence reproducibility.
insights.root_cause: Plugins used in build are separate from dependency tree and were not collected.
insights.solution: Added optional parameter includeMavenPlugins and getAllPlugins() to collect project.getBuildPlugins(), convert to MavenPlugin objects and include them in LockFile.
insights.lesson_learned: Build plugins are part of reproducible builds; make plugin inclusion optional because it may introduce more variability. Tests should cover both modes.

template/action.yml.short_description: A GitHub composite action template that runs the Maven Lockfile plugin to generate/validate and optionally commit lockfiles.
template/action.yml.description:
This YAML file is a template for a GitHub composite action that orchestrates running the Maven Lockfile Maven plugin inside CI. It exposes inputs that control token, commit behavior, whether to include Maven plugins in lockfile computation, lockfile filename, the workflow filename to watch, and whether to skip regeneration when an existing lockfile was produced by a newer plugin. The action uses actions/checkout and actions/setup-java, inspects git diffs to detect changes to pom.xml or the workflow file, then either invokes the plugin's generate goal or validate goal depending on inputs and state, and finally can commit updated lockfiles with an auto-commit action.

The file exists as a reusable template to embed the Maven Lockfile plugin behavior inside other repository workflows or to be used as a published action. It centralizes decision logic (detect changed pom/workflow, decide generate vs validate, avoid overwriting lockfiles produced by newer plugin versions) so downstream consumers can rely on consistent behavior. The run step contains a substantial bash script block (including a helper execute_maven_command function and version-checking logic) which is the main runtime behavior; this ties the template closely to runtime environment expectations (availability of jq, git history in the runner, Maven installed via setup-java, and substitution of a PLUGIN_VERSION placeholder).

key_constructs.name: inputs
key_constructs.purpose: Defines the action inputs (github-token, commit-lockfile, commit-message, include-maven-plugins, lockfile-name, workflow-filename, skip-older-regeneration).
key_constructs.reasoning: Inputs define the public configuration surface that consumers of the action expect and must remain stable when the action is used by external workflows.

key_constructs.name: changed-files
key_constructs.purpose: Step id that computes whether pom.xml or workflow file changed and sets POM_CHANGED in $GITHUB_ENV.
key_constructs.reasoning: This step determines the central condition (POM_CHANGED) used later to decide whether to run generation or validation; edits must preserve its semantics and environment export behavior.

key_constructs.name: POM_CHANGED
key_constructs.purpose: Environment variable populated by the changed-files step indicating whether pom.xml or workflow-filename changed in the diff.
key_constructs.reasoning: This env var is heavily relied upon by the main script to decide the action; its exact true/false string semantics are used in string comparisons in bash.

key_constructs.name: COMMIT_UPDATED_LOCKFILE
key_constructs.purpose: Environment variable set from the commit-lockfile input to indicate whether the action should commit generated lockfiles.
key_constructs.reasoning: Controls whether the action tries to commit a regenerated lockfile — affects whether commit-lockfile step runs and how the script chooses generate vs validate.

key_constructs.name: execute_maven_command
key_constructs.purpose: Bash function that runs mvn with provided goal, writes notices to GitHub step summary and logs, and returns success/failure status.
key_constructs.reasoning: Encapsulates the pattern for invoking Maven and formatting success/failure messaging; tests and consumers rely on the exact output behavior to populate GitHub step summary content.

key_constructs.name: PLUGIN_VERSION
key_constructs.purpose: Holds the Maven plugin version to invoke; in the template it is assigned from the placeholder ${released.version}.
key_constructs.reasoning: This must be substituted at template rendering time with a release version. Any change to how this value is injected affects which plugin version is executed and has broad effects on lockfile format/version comparisons.

key_constructs.name: SKIP_OLDER_REGENERATION
key_constructs.purpose: Input that controls whether regeneration is skipped when an existing lockfile was generated by a newer plugin version.
key_constructs.reasoning: Part of the safety logic; editing its semantics changes whether the action prefers not to overwrite newer lockfiles generated by a release pipeline.

key_constructs.name: LOCKFILE_NAME
key_constructs.purpose: Variable holding the lockfile file name (default lockfile.json) used to read existing metadata and to pass into the plugin.
key_constructs.reasoning: Name consistency is required across the script, the plugin CLI arg (-DlockfileName), and the commit step; mismatches will cause the wrong file to be inspected or committed.

key_constructs.name: commit-lockfile
key_constructs.purpose: Step id for the final commit step which is conditional on the inputs.commit-lockfile input; uses an external git-auto-commit action.
key_constructs.reasoning: Enacts repository writes; changes here affect commit behavior and the external action configuration (e.g. author/message).

key_behaviors: detects changes to pom or workflow files via git diff and sets POM_CHANGED
decides between running mvn <plugin>:generate and mvn <plugin>:validate
checks lockfile metadata to avoid regenerating a lockfile made by a newer plugin version
invokes Maven (quiet) and writes step summary entries for success/failure
optionally commits updated lockfile using a git auto-commit action

pitfalls.mistake: Assume the checkout step will always have the pull_request context and that GITHUB_BASE_REF is set
pitfalls.consequence: The changed-files diff logic uses GITHUB_BASE_REF and the explicit ref/repository checkout inputs; in non-PR contexts the ref logic or git diff may behave unexpectedly, causing POM_CHANGED to be incorrect.
pitfalls.prevention: When editing, preserve the conditional branch that sets BASE_REF and the checkout inputs (fetch-depth: 0, explicit ref/repo/token) or ensure equivalent logic remains.

pitfalls.mistake: Remove or alter the PLUGIN_VERSION placeholder substitution (${released.version}) without providing an alternate substitution mechanism
pitfalls.consequence: The script will attempt to run a literal plugin coordinate with an unresolved placeholder, causing mvn to fail or invoke the wrong plugin version.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep PLUGIN_VERSION assignment semantics or ensure any templating altering it will always produce a concrete Maven coordinate.

pitfalls.mistake: Assume utilities like jq and sort -V are always available or behave identically across runner environments
pitfalls.consequence: jq is used to extract metaData.config.mavenLockfileVersion and sort -V is used for version comparisons; if these are absent or behave differently on some runners, skip-generation logic or version detection can fail.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the jq usage or ensure any alternative also extracts the expected JSON field; avoid changing the version-compare implementation without validating across OS runners.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing environment boolean string comparisons (e.g., 'true' vs 'True')
pitfalls.consequence: The script performs string equality checks (== "true"); altering expected casing or value shape will break branching (generate vs validate, skipping logic, etc.).
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the current exact string values used for truth checks when modifying inputs or logic.

Maven Lockfile Action run block: contains environment mapping, PLUGIN_VERSION placeholder, execute_maven_command function, and decision logic for generate vs validate

commit-lockfile step: conditional use of git-auto-commit action driven by inputs.commit-lockfile

the exact action pins (uses: ... hashes) unless you are auditing pinned versions

related_files.reason_to_check: Edits to input names, default values, or skip-generation logic in template/action.yml should be reconciled with any example workflow using the action.

related_files.relationship: the Maven plugin code that the action invokes (generate/validate goals) — the plugin version selection (PLUGIN_VERSION) determines which code path and lockfile schema will be used
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to expected lockfile metaData.config.mavenLockfileVersion, CLI property names (e.g., -DlockfileName) or plugin behavior should be harmonized between the template and the actual plugin implementation.

related_files.reason_to_check: Inputs added/renamed in the published action (action.yml) should be mirrored in this template to avoid divergence (commit history shows prior refactorings where these fell out of sync).

pinned action commit hashes used in uses: actions/checkout@..., actions/setup-java@..., stefanzweifel/git-auto-commit-action@...

insights.title: Template action updated in sync with action.yml
insights.problem: Template file and actual action file diverged; both need same inputs.
insights.root_cause: Template files were not updated when action.yml changed.
insights.solution: Add workflow-filename input to template/action.yml and include it in the files watched.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep template files synchronized with working action definitions to avoid surprises for consumers scaffolding new actions.

insights.title: Template action input updated to include-maven-plugins
insights.problem: Template for action.yml needed to reflect new input.
insights.root_cause: Action input added downstream but template was not updated.
insights.solution: Added include-maven-plugins to template/action.yml.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep templates in sync with runtime action changes to avoid confusion when generating new actions from template.

insights.title: Make action template support configurable commit author/message and explicit checkout repo
insights.problem: The action template duplicated hard-coded behavior from action.yml (commit defaults, implicit checkout) causing same limitations in generated templates.
insights.root_cause: Template inputs mirrored previous static defaults and didn't include explicit repository handling for checkout.
insights.solution: Added commit-message and commit-author inputs, wired them to EndBug/add-and-commit; added explicit repository to checkout context.
insights.lesson_learned: Templates used to generate action.yml should expose the same configurability and robustness as the canonical action config to avoid propagating brittle defaults to downstream users.

maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.short_description: Checksum resolver that fetches artifact checksums and resolved URLs from remote repositories over HTTP with caching and parallel pre-warm.
maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.description:
RemoteChecksumCalculator implements the AbstractChecksumCalculator API to obtain checksum and resolved-url information by querying remote Maven repositories over HTTP. It exists so the plugin can produce authoritative checksums (when available as repository-provided files like .sha1/.sha256) or fall back to downloading artifacts remotely and computing requested checksums locally. The class centralizes network access (single HttpClient), adds in-memory caches for both checksum values and repository resolution information, and exposes a pre-warm facility that can compute many lookups in parallel to reduce latency when generating a lockfile for many artifacts.

Design decisions visible in the implementation: allowed checksum algorithms are whitelisted (MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-512) and enforced in the constructor; the code builds canonical repository URLs using artifact.getBaseVersion() for the directory portion and the artifact's full version for the filename (to correctly handle SNAPSHOT timestamped filenames in the repository layout). Network interactions: it first attempts to fetch the repository-provided checksum file (artifact.<algorithm>), and if that returns 404 it downloads the artifact and attempts to verify the download with the repository-provided SHA-1 (.sha1) when available before computing the requested checksum locally. Caching uses ConcurrentHashMap keyed by a string composed from group:artifact:version:classifier:type and (for checksums) the checksum algorithm; resolved URL cache stores RepositoryInformation objects and uses RepositoryInformation.Unresolved() sentinel. There's a prewarmArtifactCache method that runs calculateArtifactChecksum and getArtifactResolvedField in parallel worker threads and populates these caches ahead of main processing.

key_constructs.name: RemoteChecksumCalculator
key_constructs.purpose: Provides remote-based implementations for checksum and resolved-url resolution; coordinates HTTP requests and caching.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the main class other modules instantiate for 'remote' checksum mode; edits must respect the constructor contract (allowed algorithms) and the caching behavior used by callers (prewarm/populated caches).

key_constructs.name: getCacheKey
key_constructs.purpose: Generate a stable string key for caching based on artifact coordinates and classifier/type.
key_constructs.reasoning: Cache key format is relied on by checksumCache and resolvedCache; any change will affect cache hits; the key must match the semantics used in tests/other code.

key_constructs.name: calculateChecksumInternal
key_constructs.purpose: Core implementation that attempts to obtain a checksum from remote checksum files or by downloading and locally hashing the artifact, with caching.
key_constructs.reasoning: This contains the HTTP GET flow, fallback to artifact download, SHA-1 verification fallback, and insertion into checksumCache. It must preserve algorithm string handling, filename construction, and exception-to-empty-cache behavior.

key_constructs.name: getResolvedFieldInternal
key_constructs.purpose: Core implementation that performs HTTP HEAD requests across configured remote repositories to find the artifact's resolved URL and repository id, with caching.
key_constructs.reasoning: This method constructs the canonical artifact URL (using baseVersion for directories), issues HEAD requests, and populates resolvedCache; callers expect RepositoryInformation.Unresolved() for misses.

key_constructs.name: prewarmArtifactCache
key_constructs.purpose: Populate checksum and resolved-url caches in parallel for a collection of artifacts using a fixed thread pool.
key_constructs.reasoning: Pre-warming uses an executor sized based on available processors; it creates futures and waits on them. Edits must respect concurrency, logging, and not assume immediate termination beyond Future.get() semantics already present.

key_constructs.name: calculateArtifactChecksum
key_constructs.purpose: Public API wrapper calling calculateChecksumInternal with artifact-building request and returning an empty string when unavailable.
key_constructs.reasoning: Called by external code (lockfile generation) to fetch artifact checksums; return-empty string semantics are relied upon by serializers and validators.

key_constructs.name: calculatePluginChecksum
key_constructs.purpose: Public API wrapper to compute checksums for plugin artifacts using plugin-specific building request.
key_constructs.reasoning: Similar to calculateArtifactChecksum but uses pluginBuildingRequest; plugin resolution and cache usage are distinct and must be preserved.

key_constructs.name: getDefaultChecksumAlgorithm
key_constructs.purpose: Return the default checksum algorithm for this calculator.
key_constructs.reasoning: Consumers may call the default algorithm; the current implementation returns "SHA-256" and changes here affect defaults elsewhere.

callers.context: this.checksumAlgorithm = new FileSystemChecksumCalculator(null, null, null, null).getDefaultChecksum

key_constructs.name: getArtifactResolvedField
key_constructs.purpose: Public API wrapper to obtain a RepositoryInformation describing resolved URL and repository id for artifacts.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used when assembling lockfile data for artifacts; must return RepositoryInformation.Unresolved() when not found.

key_constructs.name: getPluginResolvedField
key_constructs.purpose: Public API wrapper to obtain repository resolution information for plugin artifacts.
key_constructs.reasoning: Parallel of getArtifactResolvedField but with pluginBuildingRequest; must preserve behavior for lockfile plugin entries.

key_behaviors: fetches artifact checksum files from remote repositories
downloads artifact and verifies it against remote SHA-1 when checksum absent
computes requested checksum locally if needed
resolves artifact URL via HTTP HEAD requests
caches checksum and resolution results in-memory
pre-warms caches in parallel using a thread pool

pitfalls.mistake: Assume constructor accepts any checksum algorithm string.
pitfalls.consequence: Constructor throws IllegalArgumentException for unsupported algorithms (only MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-512 allowed).
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the allowed algorithms when instantiating or configuring this class.

pitfalls.mistake: Change cache key composition (getCacheKey) or omit checksumAlgorithm suffix when interacting with checksumCache.
pitfalls.consequence: Cache collisions or misses may occur, causing redundant network requests and inconsistent behavior between checksum types.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the exact key format: group:artifactId:version:classifier:type plus ":" + checksumAlgorithm for checksum cache keys.

pitfalls.mistake: Assume remote checksum responses are always present and successful.
pitfalls.consequence: If remote checksum files are missing (404), code will download the artifact; if download cannot be verified it may throw RuntimeException or return empty, affecting lockfile generation.
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure network availability and repository structure, and be prepared to handle Optional.empty() results as the code currently does.

pitfalls.mistake: Modify prewarmArtifactCache to shutdown the executor without waiting on futures.
pitfalls.consequence: In-flight prewarm tasks might be interrupted or not completed; although the method waits on futures currently, altering this could cause partial cache population and intermittent behavior.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the current pattern of collecting futures and calling future.get() before shutting down the executor, or otherwise ensure tasks complete.

pitfalls.mistake: Assume artifact verification (.sha1) string casing or format is normalized.
pitfalls.consequence: Comparison between calculated SHA-1 (lowercased) and repository-provided SHA-1 may fail if repository uses uppercase or extra formatting; the current code extracts the first token but does not normalize case for the extracted token.
pitfalls.prevention: Be careful when changing verification logic; maintain token extraction and consider case-insensitive comparison if modifying behavior (but do not change now without adjusting dependent tests/consumers).

pitfalls.mistake: Change URL construction to use artifact.getVersion() for the directory in SNAPSHOT cases.
pitfalls.consequence: SNAPSHOT artifacts will be looked up under timestamped directories, which mismatches repository layout and will cause failed HEAD/GET checks and unresolved fields.
pitfalls.prevention: Continue using artifact.getBaseVersion() for the directory portion and full artifact.getVersion() for the filename, as tests depend on this.

key_sections: calculateChecksumInternal: contains HTTP fetch, 404 fallback to artifact download, SHA-1 verification and caching
getResolvedFieldInternal: constructs HEAD requests to determine resolved URL and repository id
prewarmArtifactCache: concurrency and caching invocation for performance
skip_unless_needed: constructor argument validation (boilerplate)
small wrapper methods calculateArtifactChecksum/calculatePluginChecksum/getArtifactResolvedField/getPluginResolvedField

relevant_snippets.description: Integration tests that exercise lockfile generation covering plugin checksum resolution and dependency checks (includes scenarios that hit remote resolution code paths).

related_files.relationship: Alternative checksum calculator implementation used when artifacts are present on the local filesystem; RemoteChecksumCalculator mirrors many behaviors but over HTTP and both share AbstractChecksumCalculator API.
related_files.reason_to_check: When changing URL/filename construction, algorithm handling, or caching semantics both implementations must remain consistent (especially around baseVersion usage for SNAPSHOTs).

related_files.relationship: Defines public API that RemoteChecksumCalculator implements; caller code depends on the contract (method names and empty-string semantics).
related_files.reason_to_check: Any changes to method contracts (returning Optional vs empty string, default algorithm naming) require corresponding changes here and in callers.

insights.title: Add caching and parallel pre-warm to RemoteChecksumCalculator
insights.problem: Remote checksum and resolved URL resolution performed repeated network requests; earlier code built an HttpClient per request.
insights.root_cause: Uncached and sequential network requests caused slow lockfile generation and redundant remote work.
insights.solution: Introduce checksumCache and resolvedCache (ConcurrentHashMap) keyed by artifact G:A:V:classifier:type and include checksumAlgorithm; reuse a single HttpClient instance; add prewarmArtifactCache which spins a fixed thread pool and submits tasks to calculateArtifactChecksum/getArtifactResolvedField in parallel; use cache to return results quickly.
insights.lesson_learned: Caching remote lookups reduces network load and improves performance, but cache key must include classifier/type/version semantics. Prewarming introduces concurrency; ensure proper logging and avoid silent failures. Executor is shutdown but not awaited — callers should be aware of potential in-flight operations if process interrupts occur.

insights.title: Use baseVersion for remote artifact path resolution
insights.problem: SNAPSHOT artifacts were resolved using timestamped version directories which do not match the repository layout for directory paths used by checksums and remote POMs.
insights.root_cause: Code used artifact.getVersion() rather than artifact.getBaseVersion() when constructing repository paths.
insights.solution: Use artifact.getBaseVersion() when building remote URLs (artifact directory), ensuring SNAPSHOT artifacts map to their base snapshot directory.
insights.lesson_learned: When computing canonical repository URLs, use baseVersion for directory paths (directories use the base snapshot string); filename still uses the full version (possibly timestamped). Tests were added to validate this behavior.

insights.title: Handle classifier and plugin type mapping when requesting remote checksums or resolved urls
insights.problem: Remote checksum/resolved-URL lookup ignored classifiers and treated maven-plugin type incorrectly, causing HEAD/GET checks to to miss artifacts with classifiers or maven-plugin extension.
insights.root_cause: Filename built as artifactId-version.extension without classifier; maven-plugin extension expected 'jar' on repo.
insights.solution: Include classifier when constructing filename (prepend '-') and normalize maven-plugin to jar for remote paths; use HEAD for resolved URL checks; use full artifact URL when trying checksum fallback.
insights.lesson_learned: Remote repository paths must match real stored filenames; include classifier and handle packaging-to-extension conversions (maven-plugin->jar).

insights.title: Support SHA-256 and SHA-512 remote checksums and verify downloaded artifacts using SHA-1 fallback
insights.problem: Remote checksum mode only supported MD5/SHA-1 previously; need stronger algorithms and robust fallback verification.
