insights.root_cause: Maven repositories provide various checksum files; stronger algorithms are preferred. When remote checksum missing, downloaded artifact must be verified where possible.
insights.solution: Allowed checksumAlgorithm values md5/sha1/sha256/sha512; when remote checksum missing, download artifact and verify it against a remote SHA-1 file if available before calculating requested checksum locally.
insights.lesson_learned: When supporting multiple checksum algorithms, implement safe fallback: if remote checksum isn't present, download artifact and verify against an available trusted checksum (sha1 here) before computing alternate checksums locally.

insights.title: Switch RemoteChecksumCalculator logging to PluginLogManager and improve messages
insights.problem: Log messages used log4j and had less informative formatting.
insights.root_cause: Need consistent Maven logging and clearer messages when remote lookups or fallbacks occur.
insights.solution: Use PluginLogManager.getLog() .info/debug/warn/error and more descriptive messages (include artifact.toString()).
insights.lesson_learned: Clear logging is essential for diagnosing remote lookup failures (which repository was checked, when downloads happen and when verifications fail).

insights.title: Remote checksum calculator default algorithm (sha1)
insights.problem: When using remote checksum mode, a different default algorithm (sha1) is expected by Maven's remote metadata.
insights.root_cause: Different trust sources imply different canonical checksum algorithms; previously no way to express per-mode default.
insights.solution: Implement getDefaultChecksumAlgorithm() returning 'sha1' in RemoteChecksumCalculator.
insights.lesson_learned: Checksum mode semantics should be explicit and enforced at calculator implementation level to avoid confusion.

insights.title: Remote checksum mode fetches checksum from Maven Central
insights.problem: Need a mode that uses repository-provided checksum files instead of computing locally.
insights.root_cause: Some environments may not have local artifacts; remote fetch can provide authoritative checksum (sha1/md5) from central.
insights.solution: RemoteChecksumCalculator fetches <artifact>.{sha1|md5} from repo1.maven.org using Java HttpClient; validates algorithm only supports sha1 or md5.
insights.lesson_learned: Remote mode depends on repository structure and only supports limited algorithms; be explicit about acceptable algorithms and fail-fast for unsupported ones.

insights.title: Network and artifact URL construction can fail
insights.problem: If artifact coordinates or type mismatch expected layout or network fails, calculation throws and propagates RuntimeException.
insights.root_cause: Assumes artifacts follow Central layout and that network is available.
insights.solution: Logs warn and throws RuntimeException on failure. Consider returning empty and handling equivalently to local mode to avoid failing entire generation.
insights.lesson_learned: Remote calls must consider network reliability, repository mirrors, authentication, and rate limits; design graceful fallback behaviors.

maven_plugin/src/test/java/io/github/cha.short_description: Unit tests verifying resolved URL construction for SNAPSHOT and non-SNAPSHOT artifacts in local repo layout.
maven_plugin/src/test/java/io/github/cha.description:
This JUnit test class verifies the behavior of FileSystemChecksumCalculator.getArtifactResolvedField with respect to how it composes the resolved URL for artifacts saved in the local Maven repository layout. The tests simulate local repository files and an accompanying _remote.repositories file that maps artifact files to repository IDs. They assert that for SNAPSHOT artifacts the directory portion of the resolved URL uses the artifact.getBaseVersion() (e.g., "1.0-SNAPSHOT") while the filename uses the timestamped version, and for non-SNAPSHOT artifacts the directory and filename use the same version string.

The tests create temporary directories and files (using JUnit @TempDir), build DefaultArtifact objects with specific baseVersion or version values, set up a DefaultProjectBuildingRequest with one remote repository (MavenArtifactRepository), instantiate FileSystemChecksumCalculator with a null resolver (so resolveDependency is a no-op), and then call getArtifactResolvedField and assert that ResolvedUrl and RepositoryId match expectations. These tests codify the contract that directory path = baseVersion for SNAPSHOTs and = version for non-SNAPSHOTs, and exercise edge cases such as non-semantic version suffixes (e.g., redhat qualifiers).

key_constructs.name: FileSystemChecksumCalculatorTest
key_constructs.purpose: Container for unit tests that validate URL resolution behavior for artifacts in the local repo layout.
key_constructs.reasoning: This test class defines the expectations that FileSystemChecksumCalculator must meet; agents editing checksum-related code must ensure tests remain green.

key_constructs.name: snapshotArtifactResolvedUrlUsesBaseVersion
key_constructs.purpose: Test that SNAPSHOT artifact resolved URL uses baseVersion for the directory portion.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the key regression test for using artifact.getBaseVersion() in URL construction for snapshot handling.

key_constructs.name: nonSnapshotArtifactResolvedUrlUsesVersion
key_constructs.purpose: Test that non-SNAPSHOT artifact resolved URL uses artifact.getVersion() for both directory and filename.
key_constructs.reasoning: Validates normal version behavior and ensures changes do not break release artifact handling.

key_constructs.name: versionSuffixArtifactResolvedUrlUsesVersion
key_constructs.purpose: Test that non-SNAPSHOT artifacts with qualifier suffixes (non-semantic) still use the version in both directory and filename.
key_constructs.reasoning: Covers real-world versions that include vendor suffixes, ensuring consistent URL composition for such versions.

key_behaviors: simulate local maven repository layout with _remote.repositories
ensure SNAPSHOT directory path uses baseVersion
verify resolved URL and repository id extraction logic

pitfalls.mistake: Change FileSystemChecksumCalculator to use artifact.getVersion() for the directory of SNAPSHOT artifacts.
pitfalls.consequence: These tests will fail and SNAPSHOT artifacts will be resolved incorrectly (timestamped directory mismatch).
pitfalls.prevention: Respect test contract: directory portion should use baseVersion; filename uses the artifact's full version.

pitfalls.mistake: Rely on resolver behavior in these tests (resolver is passed as null).
pitfalls.consequence: Tests purposely bypass dependency resolution; altering the test to expect resolver resolution will change semantics and may mask real behavior.
pitfalls.prevention: When editing tests or FileSystemChecksumCalculator, remember these specific tests assume resolver is null and that resolveDependency will not alter artifact coordinates.

key_sections: test setup: creation of artifact directory, artifact file, and _remote.repositories contents (establishes local repo layout)
artifact creation and baseVersion assignment (shows difference between baseVersion and timestamped version)
assertions: expected ResolvedUrl and RepositoryId
skip_unless_needed: duplicate setup code across tests (similar pattern repeated for each test case)

related_files.relationship: This test file directly tests getArtifactResolvedField from FileSystemChecksumCalculator and encodes the expected behavior for snapshot and non-snapshot layout resolution.
related_files.reason_to_check: Modifying URL construction, baseVersion/filename logic, or handling of _remote.repositories in the implementation requires updating these tests.

insights.title: Test baseVersion vs timestamped SNAPSHOT resolution
insights.problem: Without tests, baseVersion vs timestamped artifact handling could regress.
insights.root_cause: Repository layout differences between base snapshot directory and timestamped filenames are easy to get wrong.
insights.solution: Add unit tests snapshotArtifactResolvedUrlUsesBaseVersion and nonSnapshotArtifactResolvedUrlUsesVersion that create a fake local repository structure and assert resolved URL uses baseVersion directory.
insights.lesson_learned: Edge cases around SNAPSHOTs must be tested by simulating local repo layout and setting artifact.baseVersion explicitly. These tests exercise FileSystemChecksumCalculator.getArtifactResolvedField.

maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.short_description: Abstract base Mojo centralizing injected Maven context and checksum/config factories
maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.description:
This abstract class is the shared base for concrete Mojos in the maven-lockfile plugin. It centralizes common injected fields (MavenProject, MavenSession, DependencyCollectorBuilder, DependencyResolver, MojoExecution) and provides utility/factory methods used across goals: generation, validation and freeze. The class exists to avoid duplication of session/project injection, metadata generation, and checksum calculator construction across multiple Mojo implementations.

Key responsibilities include: generating minimal environment metadata (generateMetaInformation), producing a Config instance that captures the effective plugin configuration (getConfig), constructing appropriate AbstractChecksumCalculator implementations based on configuration or CLI/parameter values (getChecksumCalculator overloads), and creating ProjectBuildingRequest instances targeted at either artifact or plugin remote repositories. The class maps plugin parameters (many declared as String to be bound from Maven properties) into typed domain objects (Config and ChecksumModes) and uses MojoExecution to expose the plugin version into persisted configuration.

Design decisions visible here: parameters that are logically booleans are declared as String and parsed when building Config (Boolean.parseBoolean). Checksum behavior is encapsulated by ChecksumModes and two concrete calculator implementations: FileSystemChecksumCalculator (LOCAL) and RemoteChecksumCalculator (REMOTE). ProjectBuildingRequest builders copy the session's building request and replace remote repositories with either project artifacts or plugin artifact repositories to ensure resolution happens against intended repository sets. The class' protected members and factory methods are intended to be used by subclasses implementing specific Mojo goals.

key_constructs.name: AbstractLockfileMojo
key_constructs.purpose: Provides shared injected Maven context and factories for concrete Mojos in the plugin
key_constructs.reasoning: Subclasses (GenerateLockFileMojo, ValidateChecksumMojo, FreezeDependencyMojo) rely on consistent construction of Config, checksum calculators and project-building requests; centralizing these avoids divergence and keeps plugin behavior consistent across goals.

key_constructs.name: generateMetaInformation
key_constructs.purpose: Builds an Environment object containing runtime metadata (OS, Maven and Java versions)
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to populate lockfile metadata; it reads System properties and injected mojo parameters so changes to how environment metadata is recorded must preserve these sources.

key_constructs.name: getChecksumCalculator
key_constructs.purpose: Factory for AbstractChecksumCalculator chosen by checksumMode/runtime parameters
key_constructs.reasoning: Instantiates either FileSystemChecksumCalculator or RemoteChecksumCalculator after constructing ProjectBuildingRequest contexts; callers depend on this to perform artifact and POM checksum resolution consistently.

key_constructs.name: getChecksumCalculator
key_constructs.purpose: Overloaded factory that accepts a Config and optional forceLocalChecksumMode flag
key_constructs.reasoning: When a persisted or programmatic Config drives behavior (e.g., validating from an existing lockfile), this overload ensures the checksum calculator reflects the desired checksum mode and algorithm from the persisted config while still allowing an explicit override to force local mode.

key_constructs.name: getConfig
key_constructs.purpose: Converts Mojo parameters into a typed Config instance used by the rest of the system
key_constructs.reasoning: All Mojo configuration parsing logic is centralized here; it interprets String parameters, boolean-like flags, and injects the plugin version via MojoExecution to create a self-contained Config object that is used across generation and validation flows.

callers.context: Config config = lockFileFromFile.getConfig() == null ? getConfig() : lockFileFromFile.getConfig();

key_constructs.name: newResolveArtifactProjectBuildingRequest
key_constructs.purpose: Creates a ProjectBuildingRequest for resolving artifacts using the project's remote artifact repositories
key_constructs.reasoning: Artifact checksum and resolution must use the project's artifact repositories; this function ensures the correct repositories are set on a copy of the session's building request.

key_constructs.name: newResolvePluginProjectBuildingRequest
key_constructs.purpose: Creates a ProjectBuildingRequest for resolving plugins using the project's plugin artifact repositories
key_constructs.reasoning: Plugin resolution may require a different set of remote repositories; this function ensures plugin resolution uses the plugin artifact repositories from the project rather than the general artifact repositories.

key_behaviors: constructs Config from Mojo parameters
creates checksum calculator (local or remote) based on mode
generates minimal environment metadata for lockfiles
constructs ProjectBuildingRequest for artifact/plugin resolution

pitfalls.mistake: Treating @Parameter fields as normal internal fields (changing types or names without preserving @Parameter annotations)
pitfalls.consequence: Maven will no longer bind plugin configuration correctly; CLI properties and pom-configured values may stop working or produce runtime errors.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep @Parameter annotations intact, preserve declared property names and default values when editing these fields.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering the boolean-like fields from String to boolean or changing parsing logic without updating callers
pitfalls.consequence: Mojo configuration behavior may change; values set via -D flags or pom properties might be interpreted differently, leading to different Config outputs and unexpected runtime behavior.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect current representation where fields are Strings parsed by Boolean.parseBoolean in getConfig; if changing types, ensure Maven binding and all call sites are updated together.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying repository selection logic in newResolveArtifactProjectBuildingRequest/newResolvePluginProjectBuildingRequest
pitfalls.consequence: Artifact or plugin resolution might use wrong remote repositories, causing checksum calculation or dependency resolution failures in CI or user builds.
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure building requests still copy session.getProjectBuildingRequest() and set remote repositories from project.getRemoteArtifactRepositories() or project.getPluginArtifactRepositories() respectively.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming getConfig and getChecksumCalculator share the same defaults for checksumMode and algorithm
pitfalls.consequence: Inconsistencies may occur between a Config created from Mojo params and a directly created checksum calculator (different default modes/algorithms), leading to surprising behavior across plugin operations.
pitfalls.prevention: When reading or modifying defaults, verify both getConfig and getChecksumCalculator behavior together; be aware of the existing defaults (checksumMode defaultValue is "remote" in field declaration but getConfig chooses LOCAL if empty).

key_sections: getConfig: central place where String parameters are parsed into typed Config and where MojoExecution is used to capture plugin version
getChecksumCalculator (both overloads): factory logic that selects FileSystemChecksumCalculator or RemoteChecksumCalculator and constructs ProjectBuildingRequest contexts
newResolveArtifactProjectBuildingRequest / newResolvePluginProjectBuildingRequest: how repository sets are chosen for resolution
skip_unless_needed: Field declarations for injected Maven objects (boilerplate injection) unless you're changing injection or parameter bindings

related_files.relationship: Concrete Mojo subclass that uses AbstractLockfileMojo facilities to generate lockfiles
related_files.reason_to_check: Any changes to Config shape, checksum calculator construction, or ProjectBuildingRequest behavior must be compatible with GenerateLockFileMojo usage.

related_files.relationship: Concrete Mojo subclass that performs validation and relies on getChecksumCalculator and getConfig
related_files.reason_to_check: Validation semantics depend on how getConfig maps parameters and how checksum calculators are created.

related_files.relationship: Config is the typed model created by getConfig and consumed throughout the plugin
related_files.reason_to_check: Changing the fields or enum values in Config requires updating getConfig and any code that reads Config.

related_files.relationship: Enumerates checksum modes used by getChecksumCalculator
related_files.reason_to_check: Checksum mode names and semantics affect the switch logic converting modes to concrete calculator types.

related_files.relationship: Concrete LOCAL checksum implementation instantiated by getChecksumCalculator
related_files.reason_to_check: Factory instantiation parameters (constructor args) must be preserved if FileSystemChecksumCalculator signature changes.

related_files.relationship: Concrete REMOTE checksum implementation instantiated by getChecksumCalculator
related_files.reason_to_check: Factory instantiation parameters (constructor args) must be preserved if RemoteChecksumCalculator signature changes.

data_constants_to_check: plugin configuration properties (field @Parameter names and defaultValue strings)
ChecksumModes enum values and any default checksumAlgorithm constants

insights.title: Unify common mojo fields and checksum factory
insights.problem: Generate and Validate mojos duplicated boilerplate: session/project injection, checksum factory logic and metadata generation.
insights.root_cause: Common functionality was implemented separately in multiple Mojos increasing maintenance burden and risk of divergence.
insights.solution: Introduce AbstractLockfileMojo which centralizes injected fields (project, session, dependencyCollectorBuilder, dependencyResolver), metadata generation, skip handling and provides getChecksumCalculator() and getChecksumCalculator(Config) factory methods.
insights.lesson_learned: Consolidate shared Mojo responsibilities into an abstract base class. Provide factory methods for environment-specific objects (checksums) so all Mojos behave consistently.

insights.title: Expose plugin version via MojoExecution for config persistence
insights.problem: The plugin version needed to be included in saved Config but wasn't readily available in a consistent place.
insights.root_cause: Earlier code used ad-hoc ways to obtain plugin version; centralizing required access to MojoExecution.
insights.solution: Inject MojoExecution and use mojo.getPlugin().getVersion() inside getConfig() when building the Config object.
insights.lesson_learned: When you need plugin runtime metadata (e.g. version), inject MojoExecution into the abstract base so all Mojos can access consistent runtime context.

maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.short_description: Abstract base class defining checksum calculation API and common pom checksum implementation
maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.description:
This file defines AbstractChecksumCalculator, the core abstraction for all checksum resolution strategies used by the Maven lockfile plugin. It exists to decouple how checksums and resolved repository information are obtained from the rest of the plugin logic (LockFileFacade, graph builders, Mojos). Concrete implementations (e.g., file-system based and remote-repository based calculators) implement the abstract methods to provide artifact checksums, plugin checksums, and details about where artifacts/poms were resolved from.

The class centralizes common behavior: it normalizes the configured checksum algorithm (falls back to a concrete implementation's default via getDefaultChecksumAlgorithm()), exposes the selected algorithm via getChecksumAlgorithm(), and provides a shared implementation of calculatePomChecksum(Path) that computes a hex (base16) digest in lowercase. It also introduces a prewarmArtifactCache(Collection<Artifact>) no-op hook intended for implementations that can fetch data in parallel (commit history shows this was added to support RemoteChecksumCalculator optimizations). Logging of exceptional conditions in the shared method uses PluginLogManager so messages appear in Maven logs consistently.

key_constructs.name: AbstractChecksumCalculator
key_constructs.purpose: Defines the API surface for checksum calculation strategies and holds the selected checksum algorithm.
key_constructs.reasoning: This base class exists so consumers (graph assembly, lockfile generation/validation) can use checksum services via a common interface and switch implementations (file system vs remote) without changing orchestration logic. It centralizes algorithm defaulting behavior and implements POM checksum logic shared across implementations.

key_constructs.name: getChecksumAlgorithm
key_constructs.purpose: Accessor for the normalized checksum algorithm name used by this calculator.
key_constructs.reasoning: Other components rely on the algorithm string to produce lockfile metadata and to compare checksums. The constructor enforces a default when null/empty; callers should use this accessor rather than re-deriving the algorithm.

key_constructs.name: prewarmArtifactCache
key_constructs.purpose: Hook method to let implementations pre-fetch or parallelize expensive resolution work for a collection of Artifacts.
key_constructs.reasoning: Default is no-op; RemoteChecksumCalculator overrides it to perform prefetching. Orchestration code may call this before per-artifact operations to optimize latency. The method signature and presence in the abstract class are part of the API contract implemented by concrete calculators.

key_constructs.name: calculatePomChecksum
key_constructs.purpose: Concrete helper that reads a Path and returns a lowercase hex digest using the configured algorithm.
key_constructs.reasoning: Many parts of lockfile construction need pom checksums (for project parents and BOMs). Providing a single implementation ensures consistent encoding (Base16 lowercase) and consistent logging behavior on failures via PluginLogManager.

key_behaviors: exposes checksum algorithm for use in lockfile metadata
computes pom file checksums in a consistent hex lowercase format
provides abstract methods for artifact/plugin checksum retrieval and resolved-source info
offers a prewarm hook for implementations to parallelize or prefetch resolution

pitfalls.mistake: Changing the defaulting behavior in the constructor or removing getDefaultChecksumAlgorithm usage.
pitfalls.consequence: Different implementations may end up using inconsistent algorithms or null/empty values, causing mismatches between generated lockfiles and validation runs.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the constructor's fallback-to-getDefaultChecksumAlgorithm behavior when modifying initialization.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering the calculatePomChecksum encoding (e.g., changing BaseEncoding.base16 or case conversion).
pitfalls.consequence: Lockfile JSON will contain checksums with a different encoding/case and validation will fail across runs and CI matrices.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep Base16 encoding and lowercase conversion; callers expect lowercase hex strings.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or renaming prewarmArtifactCache or changing its signature/semantics.
pitfalls.consequence: Remote or other implementations that rely on the hook for parallel prefetching will break or degrade performance; orchestration code may call this method expecting it to exist.
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain the no-arg default no-op semantics and the same signature so implementations can override safely.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing exception swallowing behavior in calculatePomChecksum (e.g., throwing instead of logging and returning empty string).
pitfalls.consequence: Higher-level callers may currently depend on empty-string behavior for missing/unreadable POMs; throwing changes control flow and may break error handling.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve current behavior of catching exceptions, logging with PluginLogManager.getLog().warn, and returning an empty string unless you update all call sites accordingly.

key_sections: constructor: shows how checksumAlgorithm is normalized and defaulted
getChecksumAlgorithm: trivial accessor but important for metadata consumers
prewarmArtifactCache: the hook added for implementations that need prefetching
calculatePomChecksum: concrete implementation to compute pom checksums consistently
skip_unless_needed: imports and trivial getters if you only need to understand high-level behavior

related_files.relationship: Concrete implementation that likely overrides prewarmArtifactCache and implements remote retrieval of checksums and RepositoryInformation.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change to AbstractChecksumCalculator (signatures, behavior, defaulting, logging) must remain compatible with RemoteChecksumCalculator's overrides and expectations.

related_files.relationship: Concrete implementation that computes checksums from local filesystem artifacts.
related_files.reason_to_check: Modifications to calculatePomChecksum behavior or algorithm normalization could affect how FileSystemChecksumCalculator produces values; ensure encoding/case stays consistent.

related_files.relationship: Orchestration code that consumes checksum calculators to build lockfile checksums, pom resolved fields, and may call prewarmArtifactCache.
related_files.reason_to_check: LockFileFacade controls end-to-end generation/validation and depends on the abstract API; changes here can impact lockfile output and validation behavior.

insights.title: Add prewarmArtifactCache hook to checksum calculators
insights.problem: No generic way for checksum implementations to prefetch or parallelize expensive operations.
insights.root_cause: Remote checksum calculation can be optimized but callers had no uniform hook.
insights.solution: Add prewarmArtifactCache(Collection<Artifact>) default no-op method to AbstractChecksumCalculator so implementations (Remote) can override to prefetch in parallel.
insights.lesson_learned: When a subsystem has multiple implementations that may benefit from precomputation, add a hook at the abstraction level and call it from orchestration code (DependencyGraph).

insights.title: Checksum calculator API refined and supports pom checksum and resolved data
insights.problem: Original API mixed artifact & plugin checksum methods and didn't expose resolved fields and pom checksum.
insights.root_cause: Evolving requirements: need to compute artifact/plugin checksums separately, return resolved field info (url+repo), and compute pom checksums.
insights.solution: Introduce methods: calculateArtifactChecksum, calculatePluginChecksum, getArtifactResolvedField, getPluginResolvedField (returning RepositoryInformation), and calculatePomChecksum. Move logging to PluginLogManager.
insights.lesson_learned: Define clear separation of responsibilities in the checksum API: artifact vs plugin vs pom computations and resolved-location queries. Use typed return values (RepositoryInformation) to avoid ambiguity.

insights.title: Switch AbstractChecksumCalculator logging to PluginLogManager
insights.problem: Used log4j logger for warnings in checksum calculations; inconsistent with Maven log.
insights.root_cause: Non-Mojo classes logged via log4j instead of Maven Log.
insights.solution: Replace log calls with PluginLogManager.getLog() where possible.
insights.lesson_learned: Even internal utilities should use the central logging hook so messages appear in Maven build logs correctly.

insights.title: Checksum calculator default algorithm behavior extracted
insights.problem: Default checksum algorithm selection was ad-hoc and duplicated; null/empty algorithm values weren't consistently handled.
insights.root_cause: Constructors took checksumAlgorithm raw; no centralized defaulting behavior.
insights.solution: AbstractChecksumCalculator now accepts checksumAlgorithm and if null/empty uses getDefaultChecksumAlgorithm(); add abstract getDefaultChecksumAlgorithm() for concrete classes to implement.
insights.lesson_learned: Centralizing default selection logic in base classes reduces duplication and makes different checksum modes pluggable.

insights.title: Introduce checksum abstraction
insights.problem: Checksum calculation was previously inline and tied to a particular strategy.
insights.root_cause: Single implementation prevented pluggable checksum sources (local file vs remote repo).
insights.solution: Added AbstractChecksumCalculator with getChecksumAlgorithm and abstract calculateChecksum(Artifact) to allow multiple modes.
insights.lesson_learned: Introduce small abstraction to allow multiple checksum strategies and pass it through graph construction to decouple checksum retrieval from graph traversal.

maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.short_description: Maven Mojo that validates the project's resolved dependencies against an existing lockfile.
maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.description:
ValidateChecksumMojo is a Maven plugin goal (Mojo) implementing the "validate" goal. Its purpose is to read an existing lockfile.json from the project, reconstruct the project's current resolution using the same configuration stored in the lockfile (or defaulting to the mojo config if missing), compute checksums locally, and compare the generated LockFile object against the lockfile on disk. The class exists to enable deterministic CI/in-repo validation: it can fail builds or emit warnings according to the stored Config policies in the lockfile. This class coordinates reading the lockfile, constructing a checksum calculator (explicitly forcing local checksum mode for deterministic validation), calling LockFileFacade.generateLockFileFromProject to produce a lockfile from the current project state, and then comparing environment, pom checksum, and the whole lockfile structure.

This Mojo is carefully designed to reproduce the original generation environment by using the Config serialized in the existing lockfile. It distinguishes three distinct validation checks with potentially different policies: environmental validation (Environment objects), pom checksum validation (Pom object), and the full lockfile equality (detailed diff via LockFileDifference). Each of these checks uses the config enum fields (getOnEnvironmentalValidationFailure, getOnPomValidationFailure, getOnValidationFailure) to decide whether to warn or to fail the build by throwing MojoExecutionException. The class also initializes PluginLogManager with the Maven log so downstream non-Mojo classes can log consistently.

Because validation must be deterministic and avoid network dependencies, the class uses getChecksumCalculator(config, true) (note: getChecksumCalculator defined in the AbstractLockfileMojo parent) to force local checksum resolution. Error handling: IOExceptions while reading the on-disk lockfile are translated into MojoExecutionException. When full lockfile equality fails, the code computes a LockFileDifference and assembles a multi-section diagnostic string (missing deps/plugins and group:artifact:version summaries) which is either logged or embedded in the thrown MojoExecutionException depending on the configured policy.

key_constructs.name: ValidateChecksumMojo
key_constructs.purpose: Maven Mojo that implements the 'validate' goal to verify a project's resolved artifacts against an existing lockfile.
key_constructs.reasoning: Agents editing this class must preserve the orchestration and policy-driven behavior (read lockfile config, force local checksum mode, compare environment/pom/entire lockfile, honor the Config enums for warn/error) because these are central to CI validation semantics and to avoid network-dependent or non-deterministic comparisons.

key_constructs.name: execute
key_constructs.purpose: Main entry point invoked by Maven to run the validate goal; reads lockfile, generates project lockfile, and applies configured validation policies.
key_constructs.reasoning: Most control flow (initialization, reading lockfile, building checksum calculator, comparing objects, and throwing MojoExecutionException on configured failures) happens here. Any edit must respect exception translation, logging via PluginLogManager, and use of lockfile-stored Config to reconstruct the generation context.

key_behaviors: reads lockfile.json from project
reproduces project lockfile using stored Config
forces local checksum resolution for validation
compares environment, pom checksum, and complete lockfile and enforces configured policies

pitfalls.mistake: Constructing a checksum calculator that uses remote/network resolves for validation (i.e., not forcing local mode).
pitfalls.consequence: Validation becomes network-dependent, non-deterministic, and may produce false negatives or cause CI flakiness.
pitfalls.prevention: Use the stored Config and call getChecksumCalculator(config, true) as this class currently does to enforce deterministic local checksums.

pitfalls.mistake: Ignoring the Config stored in the existing lockfile and using current mojo runtime flags when regenerating the lockfile for comparison.
pitfalls.consequence: Spurious mismatches due to configuration differences (checksum algorithm/mode, includeMavenPlugins etc.), causing false validation failures in CI.
pitfalls.prevention: Always read the stored Config from the lockfile and use it to construct MetaData/checksum calculator; fall back only when config is absent and log a warning.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying validation behavior such that environment or pom mismatches always throw without honoring the Config policy.
pitfalls.consequence: Breaks backward compatibility and CI flows that rely on warn-or-error policies stored in repository lockfiles.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect config.getOnEnvironmentalValidationFailure(), config.getOnPomValidationFailure(), and config.getOnValidationFailure() branches for Warn vs Error actions.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or altering PluginLogManager.setLog(getLog()) initialization.
pitfalls.consequence: Other library classes that call PluginLogManager.getLog() may log to a null logger or have inconsistent logging output in Maven.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep initializing PluginLogManager with the Mojo's log early in execute().

key_sections: reading lockfile and determining effective Config: LockFile.readLockFile(...) and Config config = lockFileFromFile.getConfig() == null ? getConfig() : lockFileFromFile.getConfig()
constructing checksum calculator: getChecksumCalculator(config, true) (note 'true' forces local mode)
regenerating lockfile: LockFileFacade.generateLockFileFromProject(...)
comparisons and policy handling: environment compare, pom compare, full equality compare with LockFileDifference
skip_unless_needed: import section and javadoc comments

relevant_snippets.description: Integration test class containing tests that exercise lockfile generation and validate behaviors (many tests assert lockfile contents and failure modes).

related_files.relationship: ValidateChecksumMojo calls LockFileFacade.generateLockFileFromProject to recreate a LockFile from the current project resolution for comparison.
related_files.reason_to_check: If generation behavior or the LockFile structure changes, validation comparisons and diff messages here must be updated accordingly.

related_files.relationship: ValidateChecksumMojo inherits utility methods (getChecksumCalculator, generateMetaInformation, getConfig, lockfileName etc.) from AbstractLockfileMojo.
related_files.reason_to_check: Edits to how checksums or configuration are constructed in the abstract mojo affect validation determinism and must be synchronized.

related_files.relationship: ValidateChecksumMojo constructs an AbstractChecksumCalculator to compute artifact/pom/plugin checksums for comparison.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to checksum semantics (algorithm names, output formats, or local/remote modes) change validation results and stored lockfile fields.

related_files.relationship: Used to produce structured diffs when generated and on-disk lockfiles differ; its output is embedded in thrown errors or warnings.
related_files.reason_to_check: If diff format or API changes, the diagnostic strings and test expectations must be kept in sync.

related_files.relationship: ValidateChecksumMojo initializes the PluginLogManager so library classes log to Maven's logger during validation.
related_files.reason_to_check: Logging initialization/contract changes can affect visibility of warnings/errors in CI output.

tests_to_run: Run integration fixtures under maven_plugin/src/test/resources-its (these are used by the Maven IT framework)
data_constants_to_check: LockFile serialization fields (LockFile, MetaData, Config) to preserve stored config and checksum algorithm names

insights.title: Validation behavior made configurable and environment/pom mismatches can fail the build
insights.problem: Validate mojo initially only emitted warnings for environment or pom mismatches regardless of user's desired policy.
insights.root_cause: Configuration options were limited (later improved), and validation path didn't have separate handling for environment vs pom.
insights.solution: Use Config.getOnEnvironmentalValidationFailure/getOnPomValidationFailure/getOnValidationFailure enums to choose between warn vs throw MojoExecutionException; also added detailed messages with both lockfile and project environment/pom values.
insights.lesson_learned: Provide clear, configurable validation policies and emit helpful messages (including both expected and actual) to aid diagnosis. Prefer throwing MojoExecutionException to signal build failure.
constructs: validate logic for environment and pom mismatches

insights.title: Force local checksum mode when validating lockfile
insights.problem: Validation sometimes used remote checksum mode resulting in network calls and inconsistent verification when validating against an existing lockfile.
insights.root_cause: Validating should compare against locally-resolved artifacts to avoid network-dep problems; previous getChecksumCalculator didn't enforce local mode for validate path.
insights.solution: Call getChecksumCalculator(config, true) forcing local checksum mode for ValidateChecksumMojo.
insights.lesson_learned: Validation paths should prefer deterministic local checks (avoid remote network-dependent behaviors). Provide a way to force local mode for predictable validation.

insights.title: Ensure Maven Log is available to plugin via PluginLogManager
insights.problem: ValidateChecksumMojo and other Mojos now set PluginLogManager.setLog(getLog()) to ensure non-Mojo classes can log via PluginLogManager.
insights.root_cause: Non-Mojo classes use PluginLogManager.getLog(); Mojos must set it early.
insights.solution: Set PluginLogManager.setLog(getLog()) in Mojo execute methods.
insights.lesson_learned: Mojos must initialize shared logging hooks early in execution so library classes can write to Maven log.

insights.title: Compare environment objects instead of older metadata equality; build MetaData for validation
insights.problem: Validation compared wrong fields and used inconsistent metadata representation; warnings about metadata mismatch were unclear.
insights.root_cause: Earlier code used different metadata representations and compared fields with inconsistent names.
insights.solution: Generate Environment, create MetaData with lockfile config or defaults, generate lockfile-from-project using MetaData, and compare lockfile environment fields; warn on environment mismatch.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep environment and config representations consistent between reading and generating lockfiles to produce useful validation warnings.

insights.title: Validation uses lockfile's stored Config to regenerate project lockfile
insights.problem: Validate mojo previously recreated a lockfile using current mojo properties which could differ from the values used to originally generate the existing lockfile (e.g., checksum mode or includeMavenPlugins).
insights.root_cause: Lack of persisted configuration in lockfile forced validate to assume current runtime options; this could cause spurious mismatches.
insights.solution: Read Config from existing lockfile and use it to construct the checksum calculator and to pass into LockFileFacade.generateLockFileFromProject; warn and fall back to defaults if config missing.
insights.lesson_learned: Validation should reproduce the original generation environment by using stored configuration; if config is absent, log a warning and fallback deterministically.

insights.title: Validate mojo uses same checksum configuration as generate
insights.problem: Validation must use same checksum mode/algorithm as generation to compare lockfile contents accurately.
insights.root_cause: Validation previously constructed checksum differently or used same hard-coded approach.
insights.solution: Added checksumMode and checksumAlgorithm parameters and construct same AbstractChecksumCalculator; pass into LockFileFacade.generateLockFileFromProject.
insights.lesson_learned: For comparison tasks ensure both sides of comparison use identical strategies and parameters (algorithm/mode) to avoid false mismatches.

insights.title: Improve error message content on failure
insights.problem: Previous implementation logged message without appending JSON diffs in exception text.
insights.root_cause: Diagnostics were written to log but not included in thrown exception message.
insights.solution: Now builds a detailed string with sections for missing dependencies and plugins and includes it in MojoExecutionException message.
insights.lesson_learned: Include helpful diffs in exceptions to reduce debugging time when CI fails validation.

maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.short_description: Calculates checksums and resolves repository/URL info for Maven artifacts using local filesystem state
maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.description:
This class implements filesystem-based checksum and resolution logic used by the Maven lockfile plugin. It extends AbstractChecksumCalculator and provides implementations that (1) resolve an Artifact via a DependencyResolver and a ProjectBuildingRequest, (2) compute a hex checksum over the artifact file contents using a MessageDigest with a configurable algorithm, and (3) infer the repository id and resolved URL for artifacts installed in the local repository by parsing the _remote.repositories file sitting alongside the artifact in the local repo layout. FileSystemChecksumCalculator accepts separate ProjectBuildingRequest instances for normal artifacts and plugin artifacts so the correct repository lists (remote vs plugin repositories) are used during resolution.

Why it exists: lockfile generation and validation require a deterministic, reproducible checksum and provenance (resolved-repo/url) for every artifact recorded. This implementation aims to derive those values from the local repository layout and downloaded files, enabling generation of lockfile.json entries that include checksums and the resolved repository origin for artifacts fetched into the local cache.

How it fits: LockFileFacade (and the Mojos) call into a checksum calculator to produce the per-node checksums and resolved fields stored in LockFile data models. The class is used during both generate and validate phases (and by tests). Its behavior is exercised by unit tests that validate resolved URL formation and by integration tests that assert produced SHA-256 checksums in generated lockfiles.

Design notes / constraints: The implementation resolves artifacts before reading files (resolveDependency). It reads entire artifact files via Files.readAllBytes and encodes the digest as lowercase hex using Guava BaseEncoding (preserves leading zeros). It derives directory path components using artifact.getBaseVersion() when constructing resolved URLs so that snapshot artifacts map to their SNAPSHOT directory (commit history and tests enforce this behavior). The class logs warnings and returns empty/Unresolved values when IO/resolution fails instead of throwing, to avoid breaking Mojo flows outright.

key_constructs.name: FileSystemChecksumCalculator
key_constructs.purpose: Implements checksum calculation and resolved-field extraction for artifacts using the local filesystem and ProjectBuildingRequest-based resolution.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the concrete checksum mode used when the plugin is configured to compute checksums from local files; callers (Mojos/LockFileFacade) depend on predictable behavior around artifact resolution and resolved URL formatting (including snapshot handling).

callers.context: this.checksumAlgorithm = new FileSystemChecksumCalculator(null, null, null, null).getDefaultChecksum

key_constructs.name: createDependency
key_constructs.purpose: Builds an org.apache.maven.model.Dependency object from an Artifact instance to feed into the DependencyResolver API.
key_constructs.reasoning: DependencyResolver.resolveDependencies expects model Dependency instances; preserving scope, type, classifier and optionality ensures resolver behavior mirrors the original artifact when resolving the on-disk file.

key_constructs.name: resolveDependency
key_constructs.purpose: Invokes the DependencyResolver with a constructed Dependency and returns the resolved Artifact (falls back to input artifact on error).
key_constructs.reasoning: Resolution populates Artifact.getFile() and canonicalizes coordinates. It must be used before performing file IO. The method logs warnings and returns the original artifact if resolution fails, a behavior tests and integration flows rely on.

key_constructs.name: calculateChecksumInternal
key_constructs.purpose: Reads the artifact file bytes, computes a MessageDigest with the configured algorithm, encodes hex with Guava BaseEncoding, and returns the lowercase hex string wrapped in Optional.
key_constructs.reasoning: This encapsulates the digest computation and encoding used for both artifacts and plugins. It preserves leading zeros and normalizes case, matching the checksums asserted by integration tests.

key_constructs.name: getResolvedFieldInternal
key_constructs.purpose: Determines repository id and constructs a resolved URL by reading the _remote.repositories file adjacent to the artifact and mapping the referenced repository id to a URL from the ProjectBuildingRequest.
key_constructs.reasoning: This logic is responsible for returning the LockFile's ResolvedUrl/RepositoryId pair for artifacts present in the local repo. It intentionally uses artifact.getArtifactHandler().getExtension() and artifact.getBaseVersion() to match Maven's local layout (addresses snapshot and classifier edge cases).

key_constructs.name: calculateArtifactChecksum
key_constructs.purpose: Public override that resolves the artifact with the artifactBuildingRequest then computes its checksum (returns empty string on failure).
key_constructs.reasoning: Exposed API used by LockFileFacade when computing ordinary artifact checksums; it delegates to resolution and the internal checksum routine.

key_constructs.name: calculatePluginChecksum
key_constructs.purpose: Public override that resolves the artifact with the pluginBuildingRequest then computes its checksum (returns empty string on failure).
key_constructs.reasoning: Separate behavior for plugin artifacts that must be resolved against plugin repositories. Callers rely on distinct repository contexts for plugins.

key_constructs.name: getDefaultChecksumAlgorithm
key_constructs.purpose: Returns the default checksum algorithm for the filesystem calculator ("SHA-256").
key_constructs.reasoning: Default algorithm choice is part of the public calculator contract—tests and lockfile resources expect SHA-256 by default for filesystem mode.

callers.context: this.checksumAlgorithm = new FileSystemChecksumCalculator(null, null, null, null).getDefaultChecksum

key_constructs.name: getArtifactResolvedField
key_constructs.purpose: Public override that returns RepositoryInformation for artifacts after resolving with artifactBuildingRequest, or Unresolved when unavailable.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by LockFileFacade to populate LockFile.Pom/DependencyNode resolved fields; must match unit test expectations around snapshot vs non-snapshot directories.

key_constructs.name: getPluginResolvedField
key_constructs.purpose: Public override that returns RepositoryInformation for plugin artifacts using pluginBuildingRequest or Unresolved when unavailable.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used for populating MavenPlugin entries in the lockfile; behavior differs from artifact resolution because plugin repositories are separate.

key_behaviors: resolves artifacts via DependencyResolver and a ProjectBuildingRequest
computes hex-encoded checksums of artifact files using a MessageDigest
derives repository id and resolved URL by parsing _remote.repositories in local repo
returns empty or Unresolved values and logs warnings on resolution/IO errors
uses artifact.getBaseVersion() for snapshot directory resolution and artifact handler extension for file extension

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming Artifact.getFile() is always non-null and directly reading it
pitfalls.consequence: NullPointerException or incorrect behavior; class logs a warning and returns empty/Unresolved when file is missing.
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure artifacts are resolved via resolveDependency with the correct ProjectBuildingRequest before calling checksum or resolved-field methods; respect null/empty returns.

pitfalls.mistake: Using artifact.getVersion() instead of artifact.getBaseVersion() when building local repository directory paths
pitfalls.consequence: Snapshot artifacts with timestamped versions will produce incorrect resolved URLs (would use timestamped directory instead of SNAPSHOT directory).
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain use of artifact.getBaseVersion() for directory components (filename still uses full/timestamped version).

pitfalls.mistake: Ignoring classifier when constructing target filename or parsing _remote.repositories
pitfalls.consequence: Artifacts with classifiers will not match entries in _remote.repositories and resolved URLs may fail to be detected.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve and prepend '-' + classifier when non-null to build the expected filename and match _remote.repositories entries.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming _remote.repositories files have a fully stable or comprehensive format
pitfalls.consequence: Parsing may fail for unexpected formats leading to missing resolved repository info.
pitfalls.prevention: Handle parsing failures gracefully (the code logs a warn and returns empty) and ensure tests cover different possible _remote.repositories lines.

pitfalls.mistake: Reading entire artifact with Files.readAllBytes() for very large artifacts
pitfalls.consequence: High memory usage in CI or on large artifacts (performance/memory issue).
pitfalls.prevention: Be aware that the implementation reads entire files; if altering, preserve behavior that tests expect and consider streaming alternatives carefully.

pitfalls.mistake: Mixing repository lists for plugins vs artifacts by reusing a single ProjectBuildingRequest
pitfalls.consequence: Plugin resolution may use the wrong repository list causing incorrect resolved fields or failures.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the separate artifactBuildingRequest and pluginBuildingRequest parameters; use the appropriate one in plugin vs artifact methods.

key_sections: constructor: accepts resolver, artifactBuildingRequest, pluginBuildingRequest, checksumAlgorithm — establishes the context used for resolution and checksumming
resolveDependency: performs resolver.resolveDependencies(...) and contains the failure fallback behavior
calculateChecksumInternal: IO + MessageDigest logic and BaseEncoding usage (hex lowercase)
getResolvedFieldInternal: parsing of _remote.repositories and URL construction logic including baseVersion and classifier handling
skip_unless_needed: createDependency: small helper to create a model Dependency from an Artifact (boilerplate mapping)
overrides at bottom (simple delegating methods that call internals)

relevant_snippets.description: Unit tests that set up a temp local repo layout, create _remote.repositories entries and assert getArtifactResolvedField returns ResolvedUrl using baseVersion for SNAPSHOTs and version for non-SNAPSHOTs.
Integration tests that validate generated lockfile entries (checksums and plugin entries) and rely on FileSystem checksum mode as part of full lockfile generation.

related_files.relationship: Base class that defines the public API (methods overridden here) and shared behavior for checksum calculators.
related_files.reason_to_check: Constructor and overridden method signatures (calculateArtifactChecksum, calculatePluginChecksum, getArtifactResolvedField, getPluginResolvedField) must remain compatible with the abstract API.

related_files.relationship: Alternative checksum mode (remote) with different behavior for computing checksums and resolved fields.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to algorithms, returned formats, or default algorithm selections may need to be consistent across modes and reflected in tests and lockfile resources.

related_files.relationship: Unit tests that assert snapshot and non-snapshot resolved URL behavior and exercise classifier/baseVersion logic.
related_files.reason_to_check: If modifying resolved URL logic or encoding, update these tests accordingly.

related_files.relationship: Provides logging used by this class; logging messages and levels are part of expected diagnostic output.
related_files.reason_to_check: If changing logging behavior here, ensure messages remain informative for users and tests that inspect logs (if any) are updated.

tests_to_run: mvn test (to run unit + integration tests; integration suite may be extensive)
data_constants_to_check: encoding: BaseEncoding.base16() usage and lowercase normalization

insights.title: Use baseVersion for local path resolution of snapshot artifacts
insights.problem: Resolved URL generation and local repo path lookups used artifact.getVersion() which could be a timestamped version for SNAPSHOTs, leading to incorrect path calculations.
insights.root_cause: Using version instead of baseVersion when building the path segment for the artifact directory.
insights.solution: Switch to artifact.getBaseVersion() for directory path construction so snapshot artifacts use their base SNAPSHOT directory (filename continues to use full/timestamped version).
insights.lesson_learned: Be explicit about baseVersion vs version when mapping artifacts to repository layouts; add tests that create timestamped artifacts in local repo and assert expected resolved URLs.

insights.title: Use artifact handler extension rather than type string for extension
insights.problem: Code special-cased artifact type 'maven-plugin' to map to jar; this was fragile and incomplete.
insights.root_cause: Using artifact.getType() is less reliable than the artifact handler extension, which represents packaging/extension.
insights.solution: Use artifact.getArtifactHandler().getExtension() to derive file extension/packaging consistently.
insights.lesson_learned: Use the Maven API's artifact handler to derive file extension/packaging instead of ad-hoc type checks.

insights.title: Handle artifact classifier when computing resolved url and filenames
insights.problem: Filename and _remote.repositories parsing ignored artifact classifier, leading to incorrect resolved URL detection for artifacts with classifiers.
insights.root_cause: Filename construction used artifactId-version.type without classifier; _remote.repositories entries reference classifier-qualified filenames.
insights.solution: Include classifier when building filename (prepend '-' + classifier if present) and use that when scanning _remote.repositories and constructing target paths.
insights.lesson_learned: Artifact filenames must include classifier when present. Always test for artifacts with classifiers (e.g., noaop classifier) when resolving local repository paths.

insights.title: FileSystemChecksumCalculator uses separate ProjectBuildingRequest for artifacts and plugins
insights.problem: One single ProjectBuildingRequest prevented using the appropriate remote repositories for artifact vs plugin resolution.
insights.root_cause: Plugin and artifact repositories differ (pluginRepositories vs remoteArtifactRepositories).
insights.solution: Accept artifactBuildingRequest and pluginBuildingRequest in constructor and use correct one during resolutions.
insights.lesson_learned: When resolving different kinds of artifacts (plugins vs normal artifacts), pass the appropriate building request configured with the correct repository lists.

insights.title: Migrate logging to PluginLogManager
insights.problem: Previously logged via log4j; replaced with PluginLogManager.
insights.root_cause: Need to integrate with Maven's logging.
insights.solution: Use PluginLogManager.getLog() for warn/error messages.
insights.lesson_learned: Consistent logging across components is essential for actionable messages in CI runs.

insights.title: Preserve leading zeros in hex checksums (use base16 encoding)
insights.problem: Previously using BigInteger(1, bytes).toString(16) dropped leading zeros in hex representation, altering expected checksum strings and failing validations.
insights.root_cause: Using BigInteger to convert bytes to hex omits leading zeros.
insights.solution: Switch to Guava BaseEncoding.base16().encode(artifactHash).toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT) to produce full-length hex strings with leading zeros preserved.
insights.lesson_learned: Checksum encoding must preserve byte length — prefer explicit hex encoding routines over BigInteger-based conversions.

insights.title: Provide default checksum algorithm
insights.problem: Different checksum modes require different default algorithms; FileSystem checksum should default to SHA-256.
insights.root_cause: Defaulting previously lived in Config; behavior moved into calculator implementations.
insights.solution: Implement getDefaultChecksumAlgorithm() returning 'SHA-256' in FileSystemChecksumCalculator.
insights.lesson_learned: Provide per-mode defaults in the calculator implementation and use base class fallback for null/empty input.

insights.title: Calculate checksum from local artifact file and resolve artifact first
insights.problem: Artifact files may be null if not resolved; checksum calculation must resolve artifacts via DependencyResolver.
insights.root_cause: Checksum calculation requires the actual file on disk; unresolved Artifact.getFile() would be null.
insights.solution: FileSystemChecksumCalculator resolves artifact via DependencyResolver.resolveDependencies(buildingRequest, ...) and computes MessageDigest with configured algorithm. Returns empty string on failure.
insights.lesson_learned: Resolutions and IO can fail; return empty or handle gracefully and log warnings. Use resolver to obtain local file before reading bytes. Consider streaming large files rather than reading fully into memory.

insights.title: Reads entire artifact file into memory to compute digest
insights.problem: Uses Files.readAllBytes to compute checksum, which loads whole file into memory.
insights.root_cause: Simple implementation used readAllBytes for ease.
insights.solution: Works but may be memory heavy for large artifacts; consider streaming with DigestInputStream to compute digest incrementally.
insights.lesson_learned: For potentially large binary files prefer streaming digest computation.

maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.short_description: Builds an ordered DependencyNode graph from Maven's dependency graph and prepares checksums/resolution metadata.
maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.description:
DependencyGraph is the adaptor that converts Maven's dependency-graph representation (org.apache.maven.shared.dependency.graph.DependencyNode graph) into the project's immutable, deterministic data model (Set<DependencyNode>). Its responsibilities are: determine roots, traverse the Maven graph to create DependencyNode instances enriched with checksum and repository information (using an injected AbstractChecksumCalculator), produce a flattened dependency set via BFS, and support a 'reduced' mode that omits non-selected (conflicted) versions. The class centralizes ordering decisions (TreeSet with comparator on DependencyNode::getComparatorString) so serialized lockfiles are stable and comparable across runs.

This file exists because lockfile generation requires a stable, fully-resolved representation of every dependency node with checksum and resolved URL/repository id metadata. The design pushes IO and resolution work into the AbstractChecksumCalculator implementation (injected into DependencyGraph.of), keeping graph traversal and node assembly logic here. createDependencyNode handles nuanced behavior: root vs non-root checksum suppression, winner-version detection via SpyingDependencyNodeUtils.getWinnerVersion(node) to set the included/selectedVersion fields, and recursive child creation. The of(...) factory pre-warms the checksum calculator by collecting unique non-root artifact coordinates and calling calc.prewarmArtifactCache(uniqueArtifacts) to enable parallel/responsible remote fetching.

This class interacts closely with DependencyNode (the model it constructs), AbstractChecksumCalculator (checksum + resolved-field provider), RepositoryInformation, and higher-level orchestrators like LockFileFacade (which calls DependencyGraph.of). Several tests exercise behavior around deterministic ordering, reduction mode, resolved-field population, and checksum modes (see LockfileTest and IntegrationTestsIT). The commit history shows this file evolved to address deterministic ordering, pre-warming caches for performance, adding resolved-url/repository-id to nodes, and careful handling of conflict/winner resolution; these are important constraints to respect when editing it.

key_constructs.name: DependencyGraph
key_constructs.purpose: Encapsulates the converted dependency graph as a Set<DependencyNode> with helpers for roots and full dependency traversal.
key_constructs.reasoning: Primary entrypoint and immutable holder for the project dependency graph used when assembling the final LockFile; maintain deterministic ordering and expose traversal utilities.

key_constructs.name: getRoots
key_constructs.purpose: Return the top-level nodes (those without a parent) as a deterministic TreeSet ordered by DependencyNode::getComparatorString.
key_constructs.reasoning: Lockfile JSON and equality comparisons rely on a stable ordering of root nodes; changing ordering semantics will break deterministic lockfile equality.

key_constructs.name: getDependencySet
key_constructs.purpose: Breadth-first traversal (BFS) to flatten the dependency tree into a deterministic TreeSet of DependencyNode, avoiding cycles via a visited set.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to gather all transitive dependencies in a stable order and introduced historically to allow pre-warming of checksum caches (performance optimization).

key_constructs.name: of
key_constructs.purpose: Factory that converts a Guava MutableGraph of Maven dependency nodes into the project's DependencyGraph, invoking checksum prewarming and assembling roots.
key_constructs.reasoning: Central orchestration point: determines Maven graph roots, collects unique non-root artifacts for calc.prewarmArtifactCache, builds DependencyNode trees, removes the project root node, and returns a graph of real dependency roots.

key_constructs.name: createDependencyNode
key_constructs.purpose: Recursive helper to create a single DependencyNode from a Maven dependency node, computing checksum/resolved fields and recursing to successors.
key_constructs.reasoning: Handles subtle decisions: suppressing checksum for top-level project root (isRoot), setting included/selectedVersion using SpyingDependencyNodeUtils.getWinnerVersion(node), honoring the reduce flag (skip non-selected nodes), and adding children deterministically.

key_constructs.name: getParentForNode
key_constructs.purpose: Find and return the parent DependencyNode for a given DependencyNode based on stored parent id.
key_constructs.reasoning: Utility used when mapping parent relationships back into the internal Set representation; relies on DependencyNode.id equality and the internal graph Set.

key_behaviors: converts Maven dependency graph into project DependencyNode set
pre-warms checksum calculation cache for unique artifacts
constructs child-parent relationships and flattens graph via BFS
respects 'reduced' mode by omitting non-selected (conflicted) nodes

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or changing the TreeSet ordering / comparator used to collect nodes (DependencyNode::getComparatorString).
pitfalls.consequence: Lockfile ordering becomes non-deterministic; tests that assert lockfile equality or stable diffs will fail and CI could report spurious changes.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the existing deterministic collection usage (TreeSet with the comparator) and ensure any new comparator continues to produce a stable ordering across runs and JVMs.

pitfalls.mistake: Performing checksum IO or network calls directly inside DependencyGraph without using the AbstractChecksumCalculator or bypassing prewarmArtifactCache.
pitfalls.consequence: Degraded performance and loss of centralized control over checksum strategies (local vs remote). Remote calculators rely on pre-warming to parallelize; bypassing that may introduce serial network calls and test flakiness.
pitfalls.prevention: Always use calc.calculateArtifactChecksum(...) and calc.getArtifactResolvedField(...) and preserve the prewarmArtifactCache(uniqueArtifacts) call in of(...).

pitfalls.mistake: Altering root handling logic (isRoot flag) so the top-level project node gets a checksum or behaves like normal dependencies.
pitfalls.consequence: Lockfiles may contain spurious project checksums and mismatch validation semantics; validation logic and tests assume root has no checksum.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the isRoot parameter behavior: root checksum = empty string and RepositoryInformation.Unresolved for root nodes.

pitfalls.mistake: Relying on or changing SpyingDependencyNodeUtils.getWinnerVersion usage without understanding its fragility.
pitfalls.consequence: If Maven internals change (this util reads private/internal data), winner-version detection could break silently, producing incorrect included/selectedVersion fields and wrong reduced-mode behavior.
pitfalls.prevention: Recognize that winner detection is brittle across Maven upgrades. If editing around that code, ensure tests exercise conflict resolution and expect Optional.empty fallback behavior remains safe.

key_sections: createDependencyNode: maps a single Maven node to DependencyNode and contains the core logic for checksum, resolved fields, inclusion flags, and recursion
getDependencySet: BFS flattening used for cache pre-warming and stable enumeration
getRoots: how top-level nodes are determined and ordered
skip_unless_needed: equals/hashCode: standard implementations; only touch if changing graph internal representation
getGraph/getParentForNode: small utilities used by consumers; check only when debugging parent lookups

relevant_snippets.description: shouldLockFilesEqualWhenOrderIsChanged constructs LockFile objects with swapped orders to assert deterministic equality (relies on DependencyGraph ordering).
Integration tests that validate generated lockfile contents (checksums, resolved urls, plugin dependency scopes) which depend on correct graph construction and checksum pre-warming.

related_files.relationship: DependencyNode is the model produced by DependencyGraph; changes to its fields or comparator will impact ordering, serialization and equality.
related_files.reason_to_check: If you change what data is stored in nodes (resolved url, repository id, checksum algorithm, selectedVersion, included) or change comparator semantics, update both files and tests that depend on serialization order.

related_files.relationship: DependencyGraph delegates checksum computation and resolved-field resolution to this interface; it must be called correctly to preserve behavior and performance.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change to how prewarmArtifactCache or calculateArtifactChecksum behave affects graph construction performance and test reproducibility.

related_files.relationship: Higher-level orchestrator that invokes DependencyGraph.of as part of lockfile generation.
related_files.reason_to_check: If you change the API or required behaviors of DependencyGraph.of (parameters, reduced flag semantics), update the callers in LockFileFacade.

related_files.relationship: Unit test that asserts deterministic ordering and equality of LockFile/DependencyNode sets; indirectly validates DependencyGraph ordering semantics.
related_files.reason_to_check: Run these tests after edits to ensure ordering and equality invariants hold.

tests_to_run: mvn -pl maven_plugin verify (to run integration tests like IntegrationTestsIT)

insights.title: Add getDependencySet and pre-warm checksum cache
insights.problem: Dependency traversal used only for roots; there was no easy way to gather all unique transitive artifacts to parallelize heavy I/O operations such as remote checksum retrieval.
insights.root_cause: Calculators (RemoteChecksumCalculator) performed network calls per artifact in arbitrary order, leading to suboptimal throughput.
insights.solution: Add getDependencySet (BFS) to collect flattened dependency set and then compute unique artifact list (using a key comprising groupId:artifactId:version:classifier:type). Call calc.prewarmArtifactCache(uniqueArtifacts) so checksum calculators can parallelize network fetches.
insights.lesson_learned: Provide coarse-grained access to all nodes for performance optimizations; ensure key is stable (include classifier/type/baseVersion) to avoid redundant downloads.

insights.title: DependencyNode now includes resolved url and repository id; more debug logging
insights.problem: Dependency nodes lacked resolved URL and repository id which are useful for validation and debugging.
insights.root_cause: New checksum/resolution features need to expose where an artifact was fetched from as part of lockfile.
insights.solution: DependencyGraph.of now queries calc.getArtifactResolvedField and injects ResolvedUrl and RepositoryId into DependencyNode constructor; added PluginLogManager debug logs for node creation and checksum/resolved lookups.
insights.lesson_learned: Expanding node metadata increases checking/validation capabilities but requires coordinated changes across graph, node, JSON adapters and tests.

insights.title: Migrate debug output to PluginLogManager (Maven Log)
insights.problem: Graph creation logging used other logger and lacked Maven context.
insights.root_cause: Earlier log4j usage.
insights.solution: Use PluginLogManager.getLog().debug/info in graph creation to produce Maven-consistent logs.
insights.lesson_learned: All graph-building/traversal code should log via the central Maven log to be visible in Maven runs.

insights.title: Respect conflict resolution markers and 'reduced' option when building nodes
insights.problem: Reduced lockfile generation and conflict resolution markers were mishandled; nodes selection and 'included' flag were incorrect.
insights.root_cause: Previously the code used winner version logic inconsistently and based 'reduce' on version mismatch checks; did not set included flag properly.
insights.solution: Use SpyingDependencyNodeUtils.getWinnerVersion(node) to decide 'included' flag; set baseVersion accordingly; set included boolean in DependencyNode and return Optional.empty() when reduced and node is not included.
insights.lesson_learned: Use the dependency graph utilities provided by Maven (SpyingDependencyNodeUtils) to determine resolution winners; propagate inclusion state into nodes for downstream consumers.

insights.title: Change representation of graph and roots to Set (TreeSet) for deterministic ordering
insights.problem: Graph collections used mutable/unordered Lists leading to non-deterministic lockfile ordering.
insights.root_cause: List-based collections preserved discovery order from Maven dependency graph, which can vary.
insights.solution: Switch graph and root collections to Set implementations and collect them into TreeSet using Comparator.comparing(DependencyNode::getChecksum) to get deterministic order.
insights.lesson_learned: When serializing dependency graphs, ensure deterministic ordering via sorted sets to make lockfiles comparable and stable across runs.

insights.title: Support 'reduced' lockfile (selected version) and include scope
insights.problem: Lockfile needed an option to only persist the final selected versions after dependency resolution (smaller, easier to review), and to include Maven scopes.
insights.root_cause: Previous implementation serialized the whole resolved tree including version candidates; no explicit selectedVersion tracking or scope presence existed.
insights.solution: Change DependencyGraph.of to accept a MutableGraph<DependencyNode>, an AbstractChecksumCalculator, and a boolean reduced; createDependencyNode now reads SpyingDependencyNodeUtils.getWinnerVersion(node) to set selectedVersion and, when reduced is true, omit nodes whose base version differs from the node's version (i.e., non-selected ones). Also include MavenScope in node construction.
insights.lesson_learned: Implementing reduced views of resolved dependency graphs requires understanding resolution winners (conflict resolution). Because upstream APIs may not expose winners, consider the maintenance cost of using internal/reflection-based access and provide fallback behavior. Also ensure scope is part of the node identity when relevant.

insights.title: Use of reflection to obtain conflict winner version is fragile
insights.problem: There's no public API to get the 'winner' version from the dependency graph's verbose nodes; code uses reflection to access private fields.
insights.root_cause: Apache Maven's dependency graph internals (VerboseDependencyNode / ConflictData) are not part of public contract.
insights.solution: Reflectively read the private 'data' field and extract winner version with SpyingDependencyNodeUtils.getWinnerVersion(); code returns Optional.empty() on failure.
insights.lesson_learned: Accessing internal/private fields via reflection will work short-term but is brittle across dependency upgrades — include tests that exercise conflict resolution and be prepared to update or replace this mechanism when Maven internals change.

insights.title: Move checksum computation into graph node creation and make graph immutable-safe
insights.problem: Earlier checksum computation lived in DependencyGraph using MessageDigest and file IO; constructor allowed null and graph list was mutable.
insights.root_cause: Mixing responsibilities (checksum IO + graph structure) and using mutable fields that could be null.
insights.solution: Constructor now defensively converts null graph to empty List.of(), graph list is final. DependencyGraph.of now accepts AbstractChecksumCalculator to compute checksums when creating DependencyNode; root node checksum left blank and children computed with provided calculator. Removed inline MessageDigest code.
insights.lesson_learned: Separate IO/side-effect concerns from structural construction; inject services (checksum calc) and make internal state immutable where possible.

insights.title: Root node checksum suppression
insights.problem: Project root artifact should not have a checksum computed (it represents the project POM, not a dependency artifact).
insights.root_cause: Previous implementation may compute checksum for roots incorrectly.
insights.solution: createDependencyNode receives isRoot flag; if isRoot true, checksum is empty string.
insights.lesson_learned: Explicitly handle root vs dependency nodes to avoid spurious checksums in lockfile which would cause mismatches.

maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.short_description: Model of a single dependency node used in the lockfile dependency graph.
maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.description:
DependencyNode is the domain model representing a node in the dependency graph used to build and serialize the project's lockfile. It exists so the lockfile can capture an artifact's coordinates (groupId, artifactId, version), metadata required for validation (checksum and algorithm), repository/resolution details (ResolvedUrl and RepositoryId), packaging/classifier (ArtifactType, Classifier), Maven scope, and derived bookkeeping state (selectedVersion, included). DependencyNode also models hierarchical relationships via parent/children and supports attaching BOM POMs to a node.

The class is designed for deterministic ordering and stable equality: children are held in a TreeSet with a comparator based on a comparator string (group#artifact#version#checksum) to remove insertion-order nondeterminism, and DependencyNode implements Comparable for ordering nodes in other contexts. It participates in lockfile equality and serialization flows: equals/hashCode include most fields (including children and boms) so that LockFile equality can be stable even when sets/lists are iterated in different orders. Some internal fields (parent) are annotated to avoid serialization; the id NodeId is derived from coordinates and used as the authoritative internal identity. The class contains defensive checks; e.g., addChild assigns the child's parent to this node's id and verifies it, throwing an IllegalStateException if inconsistent.

Because this class is central to graph construction, checksum validation, and deterministic serialization, it must remain stable in its equality, comparison, and ordering semantics. The commit history shows multiple breaking and feature changes (added classifier, artifact type, resolved URL, repositoryId, BOMs, scope, and selectedVersion); those changes required updating compareTo/equals/hashCode/toString and switching children from List to TreeSet to ensure deterministic outputs. Other components that create or mutate DependencyNode instances (LockFileFacade, DependencyGraph, BomResolver, checksum calculators, and code that resolves/attaches plugin dependencies) depend on the constructor signature and exposed getters/setters.

key_constructs.name: DependencyNode
key_constructs.purpose: Encapsulates artifact coordinates, checksum and metadata, parent/children relationships and BOM attachments for a dependency graph node.
