key_constructs.reasoning: An agent modifying graph construction or serialization must understand that this class is the canonical in-memory representation of every artifact recorded in the lockfile. Constructor parameters map directly to lockfile fields; changes to these fields impact equality, ordering, and JSON output consumed by validation code and tests.

key_constructs.name: addChild
key_constructs.purpose: Adds a child node to this node's children set, sets the child's parent to this node's id, and verifies the parent assignment.
key_constructs.reasoning: When building the dependency tree, callers must use addChild so parent linkage is correctly set and the child is inserted into the ordered TreeSet. Replacing addChild callers or mutating children directly can break invariants and throw IllegalStateException if parent is inconsistent.

key_constructs.name: compareTo
key_constructs.purpose: Defines natural ordering for DependencyNode based on groupId, artifactId, version and classifier (null-safe).
key_constructs.reasoning: This Comparable implementation is used for ordering nodes (e.g., in TreeSets, sorted collections). Any change to ordering fields or their null handling will affect deterministic behavior and could break TreeSets or ordering expectations in tests that assert equality across permutations.

key_constructs.name: getComparatorString
key_constructs.purpose: Produces a deterministic string (group#artifact#version#checksum) used by the children TreeSet comparator to order children.
key_constructs.reasoning: This string is relied upon to generate a stable ordering of children regardless of insertion order. If fields used to build this string change after insertion (notably checksum), TreeSet invariants will be violated; callers must ensure these fields are stable before insertion.

key_constructs.name: getChildren
key_constructs.purpose: Returns an unmodifiable view of the children set.
key_constructs.reasoning: Consumers should rely on this read-only view to traverse the graph. Direct modification of the underlying children set is prevented by design; agents should use addChild and setBoms for controlled mutations.

key_constructs.name: getBoms
key_constructs.purpose: Returns the set of BOM Pom objects attached to the node.
key_constructs.reasoning: BOMs were introduced to record BOM POMs per node. The boms set is part of equals/hashCode, so attaching or mutating BOMs has equality and serialization implications.

key_constructs.name: setBoms
key_constructs.purpose: Replaces the BOMs set attached to the node.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by BomResolver to attach resolved BOM POMs to nodes. Because boms participate in equality and toString, setting them after insertion into ordered collections must be done carefully to avoid changing equality or ordering semantics unexpectedly.

key_constructs.name: equals
key_constructs.purpose: Value equality across many fields: coordinates, classifier, artifact type, checksum and algorithm, scope, selectedVersion, id, parent, children and boms.
key_constructs.reasoning: LockFile equality in tests relies on DependencyNode.equals. Any modification of which fields are included here requires synchronizing hashCode and attention in tests and serialization consumers.

key_constructs.name: hashCode
key_constructs.purpose: Generates a hash based on the same fields used in equals.
key_constructs.reasoning: Consistency between equals and hashCode is mandatory. Mutating fields included in hashCode while the object is used as a member of hashed collections may violate collection invariants.

key_behaviors: represents artifact coordinates and metadata for lockfile entries
orders children deterministically via comparator string
enforces parent-child linkage invariants on addChild
participates in equality/hash used by lockfile comparisons

pitfalls.mistake: Mutating fields (e.g., checksum) that are used by the comparator or by equals/hashCode after the node has been inserted into ordered or hashed collections (TreeSet, HashSet, HashMap).
pitfalls.consequence: Violates collection invariants leading to unpredictable ordering, duplicate entries, or lost elements; can break deterministic serialization and tests relying on stable equality.
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure fields that affect comparator/getComparatorString and equals/hashCode are fixed before insertion; if mutation is required, remove and reinsert node into collections or perform mutations prior to adding.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming parent is serialized or persisted because there is a public getParent method.
pitfalls.consequence: Parent is annotated with @Expose(serialize = false, deserialize = false) and is not part of JSON representation; relying on serialized parent will lead to missing parent relationships when reloading from lockfile.
pitfalls.prevention: Treat parent as runtime-only metadata. When reconstructing graphs from serialized data, rely on NodeId or other reconstructing logic performed elsewhere (LockFileFacade / DependencyGraph).

pitfalls.mistake: Changing comparison ordering rules (compareTo or getComparatorString) without updating tests and other modules.
pitfalls.consequence: Breaks deterministic output and equality checks (e.g., LockfileTest.shouldLockFilesEqualWhenOrderIsChanged), causes diffs in serialized lockfile and may make CI fail.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve existing comparison semantics or update all consumers/tests and re-run the full test suite including integration tests.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering constructor signature or dropping fields such as resolved/ repositoryId/ classifier/ type without updating builders and callers.
pitfalls.consequence: Compile-time breakages across codebase and incorrect/missing information in generated lockfiles (affecting validation and plugin checks).
pitfalls.prevention: When changing constructor/fields, update all callers (LockFileFacade, DependencyGraph builders), serialization/deserialization logic, and tests.

key_sections: constructor: maps incoming resolved artifact data to lockfile fields and constructs internal NodeId
addChild: establishes parent/child linkage and enforces the parent invariant
getComparatorString: central to deterministic ordering of children
compareTo/equals/hashCode: used for ordering and equality and must remain consistent
skip_unless_needed: toString: debugging representation
simple getters/setters that just return fields

test_functions: Integration tests: many tests read lockfile and assert node fields (e.g., simpleProject, singleDependency, pluginProject)
tests.example_command:
mvn -pl maven_plugin test
# or run entire project tests from repo root:
# mvn test

relevant_snippets.description: Defines dependency nodes with different children and permutations to assert two LockFile instances are equal when order of sets is changed. Exercises children TreeSet ordering, equals and hashCode.

Integration tests that read serialized lockfile and assert DependencyNode fields like artifactId, groupId, version, checksum and that plugin dependencies have non-null scopes; exercises serialization and field expectations.

related_files.relationship: DependencyNode instances are constructed/populated by graph-building logic in LockFileFacade and consumers rely on its constructor signature and getters.
related_files.reason_to_check: If constructor parameters or fields are changed here (e.g., adding/removing resolved or repositoryId), update callers in LockFileFacade.

related_files.relationship: DependencyGraph wraps/consumes DependencyNode instances and exposes traversal utilities; ordering and equality of DependencyNode affect graph behavior and serialization.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to ordering or equals semantics will affect graph creation and deterministic export; update graph utilities/tests accordingly.

related_files.relationship: BomResolver mutates nodes by calling setBoms to attach BOM POMs to nodes.
related_files.reason_to_check: BOM attachment is part of equals/hashCode; ensure setBoms usage remains compatible and done in safe phases (e.g., before insertion into ordered sets or with reinsert).

related_files.relationship: Unit test that asserts LockFile equality when node order changes; uses dependency nodes built with this class.
related_files.reason_to_check: If ordering/equality semantics change, tests will need update; this test documents expected deterministic behavior.

tests_to_run: mvn test (root) to run integration tests; CI will run workflows that include ITs
data_constants_to_check: lockfile field names in LockFile and JSON adapters (lockfile.json format)

insights.title: Attach BOMs to DependencyNode and record artifact type
insights.problem: Lockfile lacked per-node BOM metadata and did not distinguish non-default artifact types.
insights.root_cause: DependencyNode model did not have fields for BOMs or artifact type (previously defaulted implicitly to jar or omitted).
insights.solution: Add boms field with getter and setter, include boms in equals/hashCode/toString; add ArtifactType type field with accessor and include in compare/equals and toString.
insights.lesson_learned: When extending model objects used in ordering and equality, update compareTo/equals/hashCode consistently to avoid unstable ordering. Also be cautious: BomResolver currently mutates nodes (setBoms) — consider immutability.

insights.title: DependencyNode constructor signature and stored fields expanded
insights.problem: DependencyNode gained resolved URL and repositoryId fields; constructors updated and old compatibility constructor removed or altered.
insights.root_cause: Resolved URL/RepositoryId must be stored per node for lockfile validation and serialization.
insights.solution: Add new constructor overloads that accept ResolvedUrl and RepositoryId, update equals/hashCode/toString to include repositoryId and resolved where appropriate, and provide getRepositoryId/getResolved accessors.
insights.lesson_learned: Changing a widely used model class constructor is a breaking change across the codebase; update all builders, JSON adapters, and comparators and ensure tests cover serialization and comparisons.

insights.title: Add classifier field and include it in comparisons
insights.problem: Lockfile had no representation for artifact classifiers; different artifacts that only differ by classifier could collide.
insights.root_cause: DependencyNode identity and comparison used only groupId/artifact/version; classifier was ignored.
insights.solution: Introduce a Classifier field, extend constructors, include classifier in equals/hashCode/compareTo, and update toString. Keep legacy constructor for backward compatibility.
insights.lesson_learned: Adding subtle identifying attributes to graph nodes requires updating identity, ordering, and backward compatibility constructors to avoid breaking serialization/equality.

insights.title: Change children collection from List to ordered Set (TreeSet)
insights.problem: Ordering-sensitive Collections (List) made LockFile equality and diffs depend on insertion order.
insights.root_cause: Lists of children required post-add sorting to ensure deterministic output; comparisons across different insertion orders could fail.
insights.solution: Switch children to a TreeSet with Comparator.comparing(DependencyNode::getChecksum); remove manual sort calls and expose unmodifiableSet.
insights.lesson_learned: Use ordered Set structures for graph children where deterministic ordering is required for stable serialization and equality. Update all methods that assumed List semantics.

insights.title: Ensure compareTo considers classifier nulls correctly
insights.problem: compareTo previously compared only version; when classifier added, comparing null vs non-null needed explicit ordering.
insights.root_cause: Introducing classifier without ordering semantics could cause inconsistent ordering in TreeSet.
insights.solution: Extend compareTo to compare version first, then classifier with null-handling: null < non-null; otherwise delegate to classifier.compareTo.
insights.lesson_learned: When adding new comparison criteria, carefully define null-handling to keep Comparator/Comparable consistent for TreeSet usage.

insights.title: DependencyNode extended with scope and selectedVersion
insights.problem: DependencyNode lacked explicit Maven scope and a field to record which version was selected by the resolver (winner).
insights.root_cause: Initial model only stored artifact coordinates and checksums; new features (reduced lockfile) require selectedVersion and scope to be persisted and compared.
insights.solution: Add MavenScope scope field, nullable selectedVersion with setter/getter, include scope and checksumAlgorithm into equals/hash or equality checks, and add toString for easier debugging.
insights.lesson_learned: When extending domain objects that are part of an equality contract, update equality/hashCode/toString accordingly and ensure consumers (serialization, comparisons) are updated to include the new fields.

maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.short_description: Maven Mojo that reads a lockfile and writes a frozen POM with pinned dependency and plugin versions.
maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.description:
FreezeDependencyMojo is a Maven Mojo (plugin goal) that implements the 'freeze' functionality for the project: it reads an existing JSON lockfile produced by the plugin, computes the set of resolved (selected) dependency versions, and writes a new POM file (default pom.lockfile.xml) where direct dependencies are pinned and transitive dependencies are added into dependencyManagement. The Mojo also optionally merges plugin entries and writes plugin dependency lists into the build/plugins section so plugin classpath dependencies are reproducible. It is intended to be run in the GENERATE_RESOURCES phase and requires resolution/online access because it reads lockfiles and plugin artifacts.

The class exists to provide reproducible builds by transforming dynamically resolved/soft-declared versions into explicit, freezeable forms consumed by subsequent Maven builds (or CI). It fits into the overall project flow where LockFile generation is a separate goal; freeze reads that authoritative lockfile (LockFile.readLockFile) and applies its recorded resolution to a project's POM model. The implementation uses MavenXpp3Reader/Writer to read and write POM XML safely rather than text processing. It also uses DependencyNode (lockfile graph nodes) and MavenPlugin representations recorded in the lockfile to rehydrate dependencies into org.apache.maven.model.Dependency and org.apache.maven.model.Plugin entries.

Design decisions visible in the code: BFS traversal of the lockfile dependency graph to find the 'nearest/selected' node (uses depNode.isIncluded()) rather than a highest-version heuristic; conversion of plain version strings into 'exact' ranges (e.g. "1.2.3" -> "[1.2.3]") is configurable via the exactVersionStrings parameter (default "true"); plugin dependency scopes are filtered to compile/runtime/system before being written into the POM because plugin dependencies are constrained. The code purposefully avoids in-place modification of the original pom.xml by writing a new file named by pomLockfileOutput. Error handling wraps IO and XML parsing in MojoExecutionException so failures fail the build.

Because this Mojo is part of a multi-goal plugin, it expects the lockfile format and DependencyNode semantics produced by LockFileFacade and LockFile classes (selected/winner marked via isIncluded()). Integration tests exercise freeze behavior (see IntegrationTestsIT in tests), so changes must preserve the exact semantics expected by those tests.

key_constructs.name: FreezeDependencyMojo
key_constructs.purpose: The Mojo class that implements the 'freeze' goal for Maven.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the entrypoint when the plugin runs the freeze goal. Any change must preserve its lifecycle annotations, required resolution/online flags, parameter bindings, and the sequence of reading the lockfile -> computing pinned dependencies -> updating POM model -> writing output.

key_constructs.name: execute
key_constructs.purpose: Main entrypoint that orchestrates reading the lockfile, transforming the pom Model, and writing pomLockfileOutput.
key_constructs.reasoning: execute is invoked by Maven. It initializes PluginLogManager, reads lockfile (via LockFile.readLockFile and LockFileFacade.getLockFilePath), calls helper methods to compute dependencies and plugins, and writes the frozen POM. Changes here affect the overall behavior of the Mojo and build failures reported to Maven.

key_constructs.name: readPomFile
key_constructs.purpose: Reads a POM file into an org.apache.maven.model.Model using MavenXpp3Reader.
key_constructs.reasoning: I/O and XML parsing behavior (resource closing, exceptions) is concentrated here. Any modification to POM reading should maintain try-with-resources semantics and throw XmlPullParserException/IOException through execute as currently handled.

key_constructs.name: updateDependencies
key_constructs.purpose: Applies pinned versions and dependencyManagement additions to the POM Model using a list of Maven model Dependency objects.
key_constructs.reasoning: This method merges pinned direct dependencies into existing POM dependencies and adds transitive entries into dependencyManagement. It constructs mapping keys for matching; changes affect how dependencies are matched/overwritten or added and thus directly affect POM output.

key_constructs.name: createDependencyMap
key_constructs.purpose: Creates a Map keyed by groupId:artifactId to quickly find existing Dependency instances in a list.
key_constructs.reasoning: The current mapping key is groupId:artifactId (scope is not included). This mapping governs which existing dependencies are considered the same when versions are replaced; editing this key must consider tests and previous bug history around scope differences.

key_constructs.name: writePomLockFile
key_constructs.purpose: Writes the modified Model back to an XML file using MavenXpp3Writer.
key_constructs.reasoning: Handles file creation and writer closing; changes here affect output formatting and file encoding if modified.

key_constructs.name: getNearestVersionDependency
key_constructs.purpose: Traverses the lockfile dependency graph breadth-first and collects Dependency objects for nodes marked as included (the selected/winner nodes).
key_constructs.reasoning: This method implements the semantics of selecting the resolved (winner) version from the lockfile rather than choosing highest-version. It relies on DependencyNode.isIncluded() and BFS traversal of children; changing traversal or selection rules will change pinned versions.

key_constructs.name: toMavenDependency
key_constructs.purpose: Converts a graph DependencyNode into an org.apache.maven.model.Dependency instance, copying groupId/artifactId/version/classifier/type/scope.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the canonical transformer from lockfile domain objects to Maven model objects. It also honors exactVersionStrings and may alter version strings. Any change must preserve mapping of fields and how nulls are handled.

key_constructs.name: convertSoftToExactVersionString
key_constructs.purpose: Transforms a non-range version string '1.2.3' into an exact range '[1.2.3]' unless it already appears to be a range.
key_constructs.reasoning: This encapsulates the exactVersionStrings behavior and logs a warning when the supplied version already looks like a range; behaviour is sensitive because frozen POM consumers expect either plain versions or bracketed ranges depending on configuration.

key_constructs.name: updatePlugins
key_constructs.purpose: Merges MavenPlugin entries from the lockfile into the POM's build.plugins: adds missing plugin entries, and writes plugin dependency lists filtered to compile/runtime/system.
key_constructs.reasoning: This method populates plugin dependencies in the frozen POM so plugin classpaths are reproducible. It warns when adding a plugin not present in the original POM. It also enforces allowed scopes for plugin dependencies; modifying the filtering or plugin merge behavior requires cross-checking integration tests that assert plugin dependency scopes and presence.

key_behaviors: reads lockfile.json from project base directory
computes selected (nearest) dependency versions from lockfile graph
writes a new POM file with pinned direct dependency versions
adds transitive dependencies into dependencyManagement
merges plugin entries and writes plugin dependencies with allowed scopes
converts soft version strings into exact ranges when configured

pitfalls.mistake: Assume identity of dependencies is only groupId:artifactId across all contexts.
pitfalls.consequence: Dependencies that differ only by scope (e.g., same G:A used in compile and test) may collide and be incorrectly replaced or deduplicated; frozen POM may incorrectly pin or overwrite entries.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect that createDependencyMap currently keys by groupId:artifactId; if changing identity semantics, consider tests and the historical bug noted where scope needed to be included and update all matching logic and tests accordingly.

pitfalls.mistake: Treat exactVersionStrings as a boolean independent of its String type and direct string equality.
pitfalls.consequence: The code compares exactVersionStrings.equals("true"); if parameter binding or POM sets true/false differently (e.g., Boolean), behavior may change unexpectedly or NPE occur if parameter value is null.
pitfalls.prevention: When editing, preserve parameter wiring and either keep current string comparison semantics or adapt parameter type consistently across Mojo and tests to avoid changing runtime semantics.

pitfalls.mistake: Modify selection algorithm in getNearestVersionDependency to pick highest-version lexicographically or semver-unaware comparisons.
pitfalls.consequence: Frozen POM may no longer reproduce the same winner selection recorded in the lockfile; this will break reproducibility and integration tests that assert specific pinned versions.
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain usage of DependencyNode.isIncluded() semantics and BFS traversal that picks selected nodes; if changing selection logic, update lockfile generation and tests accordingly.

pitfalls.mistake: Change updatePlugins scope filtering without consulting integration tests.
pitfalls.consequence: Plugin dependencies may be written with disallowed scopes (e.g., test) or missing expected overrides (test asserts that certain scopes are not TEST and one override to COMPILE is expected), causing integration tests to fail.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the current allowed scopes for plugin dependencies (compile, runtime, system) and the special-case handling used by tests for the maven-lockfile plugin dependency scope override.

pitfalls.mistake: Modify read/write behavior (MavenXpp3Reader/Writer) to change output encoding or formatting.
pitfalls.consequence: Generated pom.lockfile.xml may not be compatible with downstream tooling or tests that assert exact XML fragments (IntegrationTestsIT searches for groupId/artifactId strings).
pitfalls.prevention: Keep using MavenXpp3Writer and preserve field ordering where integration tests expect textual presence of elements; validate with integration tests.

key_sections: getNearestVersionDependency: core selection logic for pinned versions (relies on DependencyNode.isIncluded())
toMavenDependency: field mapping from lockfile graph to Maven model Dependency (includes classifier/type/scope)
updateDependencies: merging pinned deps and adding dependencyManagement entries
updatePlugins: merging plugin entries and writing plugin dependencies with scope filtering
skip_unless_needed: readPomFile and writePomLockFile: boilerplate I/O using MavenXpp3Reader/Writer
createDependencyMap: small helper for map creation (unless changing matching semantics)

test_functions: freezePluginDependencies (integration tests referenced in commit history)

relevant_snippets.lines: truncated in provided context, contains freezeJunit assertions
relevant_snippets.description: Integration tests assert that the generated or transformed POM contains expected groupId/artifactId entries and that plugin dependencies have acceptable scopes; these are the primary behavioral checks for the freeze mojo.

related_files.relationship: Freeze reads the lockfile data structure produced by this class; it depends on the JSON shape and semantics (DependencyNode.isIncluded()).
related_files.reason_to_check: If lockfile schema or semantics change (selected node marking, dependency shapes), freeze must be updated accordingly.

related_files.relationship: Freeze traverses DependencyNode graphs and calls isIncluded(), getChildren(), and field accessors used by toMavenDependency.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to node children representation or isIncluded semantics will alter which versions are selected by freeze.

related_files.relationship: updatePlugins consumes MavenPlugin entries from the lockfile and reads their dependencies; structure and field names must match what freeze expects.
related_files.reason_to_check: If plugin dependency representation or checksum meta changes, freeze's plugin merge logic may need adjustments.

related_files.relationship: LockFileFacade produces and locates lockfiles; Freeze uses LockFileFacade.getLockFilePath(project, lockfileName) to find the file.
related_files.reason_to_check: If lockfile path resolution or naming conventions change, Freeze's use of getLockFilePath must be consistent.

related_files.relationship: Integration tests validate freeze behavior (POM content and plugin dependency scope).
related_files.reason_to_check: When modifying freeze semantics (mapping, keys, scope handling), these tests must be reviewed and updated to reflect expected outcomes.

tests_to_run: Run full integration test suite referenced under maven_plugin/src/test/resources-its/* if present

insights.title: Include plugin dependencies when freezing POM
insights.problem: Freeze goal previously only pinned project dependencies, not plugin dependencies, which could lead to builds that still fetch transitive plugin deps at build time.
insights.root_cause: The frozen POM did not incorporate plugin dependency trees from the lockfile.
insights.solution: Add updatePlugins(Model pomModel, Set<MavenPlugin>) that merges plugin entries and writes plugin dependencies (with scope filtering to compile/runtime/system) into the frozen POM.
insights.lesson_learned: Freezing must consider both project and plugin dependency graphs; plugin dependency scopes are restricted for plugin classpath. When merging plugins, warn when adding a plugin that doesn't exist in original POM.

insights.title: Add exactVersionStrings option to freeze goal
insights.problem: freeze previously always wrote plain version strings; some consumers want exact pinned ranges (e.g., [1.2.3]).
insights.root_cause: Users need the ability to freeze to exact ranges to ensure strict dependency selection.
insights.solution: Add exactVersionStrings (default true) parameter; if true convert soft requirement like '1.2.3' to '[1.2.3]'; skip transformation if version already looks like a range (starting with [ or ()).
insights.lesson_learned: Small flags on goals that control output format should be explicit and default to safe behavior; log a warning when input seems already a range.

insights.title: Set PluginLogManager log from FreezeDependencyMojo
insights.problem: Other classes used PluginLogManager but Mojos not initializing it led to inconsistent log placement.
insights.root_cause: Same as other Mojos; added setLog(getLog()).
insights.solution: Call PluginLogManager.setLog(getLog()) at run start.
insights.lesson_learned: Apply consistent initialization across all plugin entrypoints.

insights.title: Refactor freeze to create a new pom.lockfile.xml and to populate dependencyManagement
insights.problem: Freeze goal previously replaced versions in-place and had brittle resolution logic; writing back to the same pom risked changing original pom unexpectedly.
insights.root_cause: Original freeze implementation edited pom in place and used highest-version selection heuristics.
insights.solution: Introduce pomLockfileOutput parameter (default pom.lockfile.xml), read lockfile, compute nearest-version dependencies, update pom model (replace direct dependency versions and add transitive dependencies in dependencyManagement), and write new pom file. Create helper methods: readPomFile, updateDependencies, writePomLockFile, createDependencyMap.
insights.lesson_learned: Freeze should write a new output pom to avoid modifying original pom; dependencyManagement is the right place to pin transitive dependencies; use nearest/included selected version (not highest) to mirror resolution decisions.

insights.title: Fix dependency selection from lockfile to nearest (selected) versions
insights.problem: Previously freeze collected the highest version among occurrences; this could pin a higher version than the one actually selected by Maven resolution.
insights.root_cause: Using highest-version strategy didn't reflect winner selection recorded in lockfile (which may come from nearest declaration).
insights.solution: Change algorithm to getNearestVersionDependency: traverse lockfile nodes breadth-first, pick nodes where depNode.isIncluded() (selected/winner) and use those versions; return list of Maven Model Dependency to apply to pom.
insights.lesson_learned: When freezing, use the selected (winner) version recorded in the lockfile rather than naive highest-version heuristics so freeze reproduces exact resolution used for build.

insights.title: Preserve classifier when translating lockfile DependencyNode -> Maven Dependency
insights.problem: Freeze previously dropped classifier when converting packaged DependencyNodes to Maven model Dependency entries.
insights.root_cause: toMavenDependency conversion didn't set classifier field on created Maven Dependency.
insights.solution: In FreezeDependencyMojo.toMavenDependency, set mavenDep.setClassifier(dep.getClassifier().getValue()) when dep.getClassifier()!=null.
insights.lesson_learned: When adding new identifying fields (like classifier), update all transformation paths to carry them across (lockfile -> Maven model -> pom output).

insights.title: Include scope when matching and writing frozen dependencies
insights.problem: When freezing dependencies into a new POM, matching depended-on entries by groupId:artifactId alone could incorrectly merge or mismatch dependencies across different scopes (e.g., test vs compile).
insights.root_cause: Key used to map POM dependencies omitted scope so similarly named artifacts with different scopes could be misbound.
insights.solution: Include dep.getScope() / pomDep.getScope() in the key used to map entries; when writing the frozen dependency, also set the mavenDep.setScope from the resolved DependencyNode.
insights.lesson_learned: When matching and transforming dependencies across representations, include scope as part of the identity when it influences dependency selection and behavior.

insights.title: Add freeze mojo to pin versions from lockfile into pom
insights.problem: Need ability to rebuild older versions reproducibly by pinning dependency versions from a lockfile into a project's pom.
insights.root_cause: No mechanism existed to apply locked dependency versions from the generated lockfile back into a project's pom.
insights.solution: Added FreezeDependencyMojo that reads LockFile, traverses dependency tree, selects highest version per groupId:artifactId and writes versions (and missing deps) into the project's pom via MavenXpp3Reader/MavenXpp3Writer.
insights.lesson_learned: When mutating a pom file programmatically prefer using MavenXpp3Reader/MavenXpp3Writer and ensure uniqueness/selection logic (highest version) across transitive deps. Consider scopes and optional flags when adding dependencies.

insights.title: Selecting highest version by lexicographic compare
insights.problem: The implemented selection for highest version uses String.compareTo which is lexicographic, not semver-aware.
insights.root_cause: Simple compareTo on version strings was used to prefer the 'highest' version.
insights.solution: Current implementation uses dep.getVersion().compareTo(highestVersionDep.getVersion()) > 0; callers should replace with proper VersionNumber comparison or Maven version comparator.
insights.lesson_learned: When choosing between multiple versions use semantic/version-aware comparators (Maven's VersionRange/ComparableVersion) rather than lexicographic string compare to avoid incorrect picks like '10' < '2'.

maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.short_description: Maven Mojo that generates the project's lockfile.json by collecting dependencies and checksums
maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.description:
This Mojo is the entrypoint for the 'generate' goal of the maven-lockfile plugin. Its responsibility is to produce a LockFile (JSON) representing the project's POM chain, resolved dependency graph, plugin metadata (optionally), BOMs and checksums. It exists to be invoked by Maven (default lifecycle phase GENERATE_RESOURCES) and to orchestrate reading any existing lockfile, selecting configuration, preparing metadata/environment, selecting the checksum calculation strategy and delegating the heavy lifting to LockFileFacade.generateLockFileFromProject(...). After obtaining the LockFile object it serializes it to disk at the path determined by LockFileFacade.getLockFilePath(...).

Design-wise the class is a thin orchestration/adapter layer: it handles Mojo-specific wiring (parameters, logging, skip flag, reading previous lockfile) and defers graph construction, checksum calculation and serialization responsibilities to other modules (LockFileFacade, AbstractChecksumCalculator implementations, JsonUtils, and the LockFile data model). It also centralizes how configuration is selected when an existing lockfile is present via the getConfig(LockFile) helper, supporting the getConfigFromFile toggle. The GenerateLockFileMojo is tightly coupled to AbstractLockfileMojo for shared fields and helper methods (e.g., getChecksumCalculator(Config), mojo reference, session, project, dependencyCollectorBuilder, lockfileName and skip flags).

Behavioral flow: the Mojo sets a static PluginLogManager (important for non-Mojo classes that fetch a logger), optionally reads an existing lockfile, chooses which Config to use (honoring getConfigFromFile), optionally builds Environment metadata, creates a MetaData object (Environment + Config), instantiates an AbstractChecksumCalculator via inherited factory, calls LockFileFacade.generateLockFileFromProject(...) with session/project/collector/calculator/meta, and writes the produced JSON to disk. The class purposefully isolates the Mojo-level concerns (I/O, plugin params, logging) from core generation logic implemented elsewhere.

key_constructs.name: GenerateLockFileMojo
key_constructs.purpose: Mojo class implementing the 'generate' lifecycle goal to create the lockfile.json for a Maven project
key_constructs.reasoning: This class exists as the plugin's public entrypoint invoked by Maven; changes here affect plugin behavior and CLI flags. It should remain focused on orchestration and use AbstractLockfileMojo helpers for shared concerns (checksum selection, session/project access).

key_constructs.name: execute
key_constructs.purpose: Main method invoked by Maven when the 'generate' goal runs; coordinates reading prior lockfile, config selection, checksum calculator creation, calling the facade and writing out JSON
key_constructs.reasoning: All call-sites and lifecycle wiring funnel through this method. Any edits that change invocation order, logging initialization, exception handling, or file write semantics will change runtime behavior observed by users and integration tests.

key_constructs.name: getConfig
key_constructs.purpose: Helper that merges configuration from an existing LockFile with current mojo runtime metadata (notably updating plugin version) when getConfigFromFile is used
key_constructs.reasoning: This function encodes the precedence rules for config when regenerating from an existing lockfile. It preserves user-configurable fields in the stored config while injecting current plugin runtime values (mojo.getPlugin().getVersion()). Edits must preserve the merge semantics and the fields copied.

callers.context: Config config = lockFileFromFile.getConfig() == null ? getConfig() : lockFileFromFile.getConfig();

key_behaviors: reads an existing lockfile when present
selects and merges configuration (optionally from existing lockfile)
generates environment metadata if configured
delegates dependency graph and checksum calculation to LockFileFacade and checksum calculators
writes lockfile.json to the project filesystem

pitfalls.mistake: Remove or move the PluginLogManager.setLog(getLog()) call or initialize logging late
pitfalls.consequence: Non-Mojo library classes expecting PluginLogManager.getLog() may log to null or lose log context; tests and runtime logging will be incorrect
pitfalls.prevention: Keep PluginLogManager.setLog(getLog()) as one of the first actions in execute before delegations.

pitfalls.mistake: Ignore or change the getConfigFromFile semantics or the getConfig merging behaviour
pitfalls.consequence: Regenerating lockfiles could lose user-specified config persisted in the existing lockfile or fail to update runtime metadata like plugin version; this may break CI reproducibility expectations
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the getConfigFromFile flag and maintain the merge behavior implemented in getConfig(LockFile) where plugin version is updated but other fields are preserved.

pitfalls.mistake: Assume IOExceptions during write are fatal if code only logs them
pitfalls.consequence: If an IOException occurs (e.g., permissions, disk full), the current code logs the error but does not rethrow MojoExecutionException; calling contexts or CI may not fail as expected leading to silent failures
pitfalls.prevention: When editing, be aware that this code path logs IOException and continues; any changes that alter exception handling will change observed behavior in CI/tests.

pitfalls.mistake: Change how the checksum calculator is created or what config fields are passed without updating Validate/Freeze mojos
pitfalls.consequence: Inconsistent checksum selection across Mojos could cause mismatches between generated and validated lockfiles
pitfalls.prevention: Edits must respect that AbstractLockfileMojo.getChecksumCalculator(Config) is the single factory for calculator selection and that Config is the authoritative object describing checksum mode/algorithm.

key_sections: PluginLogManager.setLog(getLog()): ensures non-Mojo classes have a logger
reading existing lockfile block (Files.exists -> LockFile.readLockFile): where config may be loaded from disk
Config selection (getConfigFromFile and getConfig(LockFile)): encodes precedence/merge rules
getChecksumCalculator(config) call: selects the checksum strategy used downstream
LockFileFacade.generateLockFileFromProject(...) and Files.writeString(...): main delegation and persistence
skip_unless_needed: import block
parameter declaration lines and javadoc

exercised_by: maven_plugin/src/test/resources-its/** (integration test projects invoked by the IT harness)

relevant_snippets.description: The pluginProject integration test asserts the generated lockfile contains mavenPlugins with checksums and dependency graphs; directly exercises GenerateLockFileMojo behavior when include-maven-plugins is enabled.
freezeJunit test inspects the produced pom.xml after freeze behavior; the generate goal participates in building metadata used by freezing workflows.

related_files.relationship: Superclass providing shared Mojo fields, helpers (getChecksumCalculator(Config)), mojo reference and common configuration; changes here affect GenerateLockFileMojo behavior
related_files.reason_to_check: getChecksumCalculator(Config), shared parameters (lockfileName, skip), and mojo metadata are defined here; any change to config semantics or factory must be kept consistent.

related_files.relationship: Core orchestration that builds dependency graph, resolves POM parents and plugin BOMs, and returns the LockFile model; GenerateLockFileMojo delegates generation to it
related_files.reason_to_check: If the way MetaData or Config is handed to the facade changes, or if the facade API signature changes, both files must be updated in lockstep.

related_files.relationship: Static logging helper which must be initialized by Mojos via setLog(getLog()); non-Mojo classes call PluginLogManager.getLog() for logging
related_files.reason_to_check: Ensure logging semantics and expectations (null checks, concurrency) align with how the Mojo initializes the log.

related_files.relationship: Interface/abstraction for checksum calculation chosen by this Mojo (via getChecksumCalculator(Config)); implementations perform actual checksum/resolution
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change to the config fields or how calculators are chosen should be reconciled with the AbstractChecksumCalculator contract.

data_constants_to_check: Config fields persisted in LockFile (checksumMode, checksumAlgorithm, includeEnvironment, includeMavenPlugins)

insights.title: Set PluginLogManager log from Mojo
insights.problem: Non-Mojo code expects PluginLogManager.getLog(); the Mojo must initialize it.
insights.root_cause: Centralized logging requires Mojos set the Log in PluginLogManager.
insights.solution: Call PluginLogManager.setLog(getLog()) at start of Mojo execution.
insights.lesson_learned: When introducing a static logging helper used by library classes, ensure all entrypoints (Mojos) set the logging instance early.

insights.title: When regenerating lockfile, preserve and update config properly
insights.problem: Regenerating from an existing lockfile lost existing config values or didn't update lockfile metadata (plugin version) properly.
insights.root_cause: getConfigFromFile previously returned lockfileFromFile.getConfig() directly; this didn't allow updating the plugin version.
insights.solution: When lockfile exists, copy config and create a new Config preserving fields but replacing the plugin version (mojo.getPlugin().getVersion()).
insights.lesson_learned: When regenerating persisted artifacts, merge existing user config with current runtime metadata rather than wholesale replacement to avoid losing intent and to update only intended fields.

insights.title: Use MetaData built from Environment + Config; support getConfigFromFile
insights.problem: Configuration building was fragmented: sometimes use defaults, sometimes use lockfile-supplied config; previously ambiguous.
insights.root_cause: GenerateLockFileMojo created Metadata and Config in multiple ways and passed config separately to facade.
insights.solution: Generate Environment from generateMetaInformation(), build MetaData(environment, config) where config is chosen depending on getConfigFromFile flag; pass MetaData to LockFileFacade.
insights.lesson_learned: Centralize metadata creation and prefer explicit flags (getConfigFromFile) so behavior is predictable in CI.

insights.title: Centralize config selection & support reading config from existing lockfile
insights.problem: Configuration for generation (includeMavenPlugins, reduced, checksumMode/Algorithm) was computed ad-hoc inside the mojo leading to duplication and inconsistent precedence when an existing lockfile had configuration.
insights.root_cause: Config values were scattered across mojo fields and logic; there was no single Config object persisted/consumed causing complex conditional logic to choose between CLI/mojo properties and lockfile values.
insights.solution: Introduce a Config data object, pass it into LockFileFacade.generateLockFileFromProject and use a helper getConfig(LockFile) to prefer the lockfile's config when present (controlled by getConfigFromFile flag).
insights.lesson_learned: Centralize plugin configuration into a single serializable object persisted in the lockfile. When reading a previous lockfile, explicitly define precedence (lockfile vs current mojo) and encapsulate that logic in one place to avoid scattered conditionals.

insights.title: Make checksum calculator selection configurable
insights.problem: Checksum calculator selection logic was duplicated across Generate and Validate mojos and used raw mojo fields.
insights.root_cause: Each mojo assembled the checksum calculator from individual properties; no single API consumed a config object.
insights.solution: Construct an AbstractChecksumCalculator via AbstractLockfileMojo.getChecksumCalculator(Config) using the Config object, and pass that into the facade.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep environment/strategy selection (checksum mode/algorithm) behind a single factory method that accepts a configuration object to ensure consistent behavior between generate and validate workflows.

insights.title: Introduce checksumMode and checksumAlgorithm parameters
insights.problem: Checksum calculation strategy and algorithm should be configurable.
insights.root_cause: Hard-coded checksum algorithm and strategy limited flexibility across environments.
insights.solution: Added mojo parameters checksumAlgorithm and checksumMode; construct appropriate AbstractChecksumCalculator (FileSystem or Remote) and pass into LockFileFacade.
insights.lesson_learned: Expose behavior-affecting options as Mojo parameters and validate values (throw MojoExecutionException for invalid modes). Ensure default values are sensible (sha1, maven_local).

insights.title: Build request cloning before resolving artifacts
insights.problem: Resolver APIs require a ProjectBuildingRequest; ensure using a copy of session request to avoid mutating shared state.
insights.root_cause: Session's ProjectBuildingRequest may be reused elsewhere.
insights.solution: Creates DefaultProjectBuildingRequest(session.getProjectBuildingRequest()) and passes to FileSystemChecksumCalculator.
insights.lesson_learned: When passing build context into resolvers create a dedicated ProjectBuildingRequest copy to avoid surprising shared-state side-effects.

maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.short_description: Serializable configuration model capturing generation/validation options persisted into the lockfile.
maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.description:
Config is a small, centralized value-object that captures the options used when generating or validating a lockfile and is intended to be serialized into the LockFile model. It exists to provide a canonical, persisted representation of runtime flags (include plugins, reduced output, checksum mode & algorithm, and validation severity) so that later validation runs can compare current state to how the lockfile was produced. The class consolidates multiple boolean flags into clearer enum-based inputs (constructor accepts enums) and exposes both boolean getters (is*) and enum getters (get* returning the corresponding enum), preserving ergonomics for consumers that expect either representation.

The class provides two constructors: a parameterized constructor which accepts enums to set internal boolean fields, and a no-argument default constructor which sets backward-compatible defaults. Notably, the default checksumMode is taken from the ChecksumModes enum and the default checksumAlgorithm is obtained by invoking FileSystemChecksumCalculator.getDefaultChecksumAlgorithm() via a newly constructed FileSystemChecksumCalculator (constructed with nulls). The class also defines inner enums that represent the allowed states for plugin inclusion, validation behavior, environment inclusion, and reduction state. Because Config is persisted into the lockfile, its shape and defaults are part of the project's stability and compatibility contract.

Design decisions visible here: booleans are stored internally for compactness and for serialization convenience, but enum-typed constructor arguments and enum-returning getters were introduced (per commit history) to improve type safety at call sites and to make intent explicit. The default constructor ties default choices to implementation-provided defaults (ChecksumModes and FileSystemChecksumCalculator) to avoid duplicating literal default strings in multiple places. This file therefore functions as the authoritative single-source-of-truth for generation-time settings written into and read from lockfile.json, and any edits must preserve serialization compatibility and the assumptions other parts of the plugin make about defaults and null-handling.

key_constructs.name: Config
key_constructs.purpose: Holds lockfile generation/validation options and provides default values; serialized into LockFile.
key_constructs.reasoning: Agents editing code that constructs or reads Config must respect that it is the persisted representation of generation options and that it exposes both boolean and enum views. Default constructor ties to other components (ChecksumModes, FileSystemChecksumCalculator), so changes may affect lockfile compatibility and tests.

key_constructs.name: MavenPluginsInclusion
key_constructs.purpose: Enum representing whether to include Maven plugins in the lockfile (Include/Exclude).
key_constructs.reasoning: Constructor maps this enum to an internal boolean; callers and serializers may rely on exact enum names and mapping behavior.

key_constructs.name: OnValidationFailure
key_constructs.purpose: Enum representing overall validation failure behavior (Warn/Error).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to translate mojo/string inputs into internal semantics; persisted in lockfile via boolean mapping.

key_constructs.name: OnPomValidationFailure
key_constructs.purpose: Enum for whether POM validation failures should warn or error.
key_constructs.reasoning: Added to allow POM validation to be configured independently; mapping to boolean is used by validation code paths.

key_constructs.name: OnEnvironmentalValidationFailure
key_constructs.purpose: Enum for whether environment mismatches should warn or error.
key_constructs.reasoning: Newer enum used by the ValidateChecksumMojo and related logic; important for CI behavior around environment differences.

key_constructs.name: EnvironmentInclusion
key_constructs.purpose: Enum representing whether environment data is included in the lockfile (Include/Exclude).
key_constructs.reasoning: Controls inclusion of environment metadata into persisted lockfiles; consumers check getEnvironmentInclusion/isIncludeEnvironment.

key_constructs.name: ReductionState
key_constructs.purpose: Enum indicating whether the generated lockfile is reduced or full.
key_constructs.reasoning: Affects graph and metadata emitted into the lockfile and how downstream validation/regen should behave.

key_behaviors: captures generation-time options for lockfile serialization
exposes both boolean and enum views of options
provides backward-compatible defaults tied to checksum implementations

pitfalls.mistake: Changing default mavenLockfileVersion, checksumMode, or checksumAlgorithm without coordinating lockfile schema handling.
pitfalls.consequence: Existing lockfiles may no longer be considered equivalent to newly generated ones, causing validation failures or broken CI flows.
pitfalls.prevention: When editing, preserve prior semantics or ensure readers/validators understand version/algorithm changes.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering the internal boolean-to-enum mapping logic or renaming enums/fields used during serialization.
pitfalls.consequence: Breaks serialization compatibility with existing lockfiles and may cause deserialization or validation errors.
pitfalls.prevention: Treat enum names and boolean mapping as part of the persisted contract; update consumers and migration paths together if any change is necessary.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying or removing the dependency on FileSystemChecksumCalculator.getDefaultChecksumAlgorithm() (default constructor creates a new FileSystemChecksumCalculator with nulls).
pitfalls.consequence: If FileSystemChecksumCalculator's constructor signature or null-handling changes, the default constructor here could throw or return unexpected defaults, affecting default checksumAlgorithm values in new lockfiles.
pitfalls.prevention: If the FileSystemChecksumCalculator constructor or behavior is changed, coordinate updates so Config's default constructor remains valid and deterministic.

pitfalls.mistake: Passing null enum arguments into the parameterized constructor or assuming it tolerates nulls.
pitfalls.consequence: Null enum arguments will cause a NullPointerException because the constructor uses equals(...) on the enum params.
pitfalls.prevention: Callers should supply non-null enums; if changes are made, preserve or document non-null expectations and add validation where appropriate.

Config(): default constructor - shows canonical defaults (checksumMode, checksumAlgorithm) that are used when nothing is supplied
enum definitions at end of file - authoritative allowed states for each option

insights.title: Config constructor refactored to enums for strong typing
insights.problem: Many boolean/string flags made code less explicit and caused deprecation/compatibility complexity for checksum modes and validation behaviors.
insights.root_cause: Configuration was represented as booleans/strings, leading to confusing checks and deprecation handling.
insights.solution: Introduce enums (MavenPluginsInclusion, OnValidationFailure, OnPomValidationFailure, EnvironmentInclusion, ReductionState) and store checksumMode as ChecksumModes enum, expose getter methods returning enums, keep deprecated backward-compatible defaults.
insights.lesson_learned: Using enums for config states reduces ambiguity, makes switch statements safer and centralizes allowed values; but it requires migrations where strings were previously used (AbstractLockfileMojo translation).

insights.title: Add environmental validation failure handling
insights.problem: There was no dedicated config to decide whether environment mismatches should warn or fail.
insights.root_cause: Need to allow environment mismatch to be configurable independently.
insights.solution: Add allowEnvironmentalValidationFailure/OnEnvironmentalValidationFailure enum and expose through Config; ValidateChecksumMojo uses it to decide warn vs error.
insights.lesson_learned: Validation options are best expressed with enumerations (Warn/Error) to avoid boolean ambiguity and allow future extensions.

insights.title: Config defaults rely on ChecksumModes and calculator defaults
insights.problem: Checksum mode and algorithm defaults were hardcoded strings and duplicated.
insights.root_cause: Earlier code used literal strings for default mode and algorithm.
insights.solution: Default checksumMode is now ChecksumModes.MAVEN_LOCAL.name(); checksumAlgorithm defaults to new FileSystemChecksumCalculator(...).getDefaultChecksumAlgorithm(); tie defaults to implementations.
insights.lesson_learned: Centralize default constants where they belong (enum or implementation) to avoid drift and inconsistent defaults across codebase.

insights.title: Create a serializable Config object persisted in the lockfile
insights.problem: No single persisted representation of generation options (includeMavenPlugins, reduced, checksumMode, checksumAlgorithm) existed in the lockfile, causing validation/generation to possibly disagree.
insights.root_cause: Configuration lived only as mojo properties at runtime and was not persisted with the lockfile.
insights.solution: Add Config class capturing includeMavenPlugins, reduced, plugin version, checksumMode and checksumAlgorithm with sensible defaults; include it in LockFile model.
insights.lesson_learned: Persist generation configuration to the lockfile to allow deterministic validation and reproducible regeneration; provide defaults and a clear canonical source of truth.

maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.short_description: Enum of supported checksum calculation modes with string parsing and Gson serialization names
maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.description:
This file defines an enum (ChecksumModes) that centralizes the supported ways the plugin calculates checksums for artifacts. It exists to avoid stringly-typed configuration throughout the codebase by providing canonical names for checksum modes (REMOTE and LOCAL) and a helper (fromName) to parse external input into the enum. The enum members are annotated with Gson's @SerializedName so the textual representation expected in JSON or plugin configuration is explicit and stable.

The design purpose is twofold: (1) to make configuration values for checksum behavior discoverable and type-safe across the plugin, and (2) to ensure consistent serialization/deserialization to/from JSON (e.g., lockfile format or plugin configuration). fromName enforces exact matching against the enum's internal name field and throws an IllegalArgumentException when an unknown name is provided. This enforces immediate failure for unsupported modes rather than silently falling back.

ChecksumModes is referenced by checksum calculator selection/creation logic (where the plugin chooses Remote vs FileSystem checksum calculators) and likely by Mojo configuration parsing and serialization code that persists plugin settings or reads them from JSON. Because the enum members carry @SerializedName values, these textual values are part of the plugin's external contract (configuration/serialization), so they should be treated as stable by any editing agent.

key_constructs.name: ChecksumModes
key_constructs.purpose: Enum representing supported checksum calculation modes (REMOTE, LOCAL).
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the canonical type for checksum mode configuration used across the plugin. It holds the external string name and provides the enum constants used to drive runtime logic (selecting appropriate checksum calculators and serialization values). Any code changes that affect how modes are represented, serialized, or parsed must account for existing uses and persisted data.

key_constructs.name: fromName
key_constructs.purpose: Parses a string into a ChecksumModes enum value, throwing IllegalArgumentException for unknown names.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to convert external configuration or CLI-provided names into the enum. It performs an exact match against the enum's stored name (not the @SerializedName) and throws on mismatch—so callers rely on strict validation behavior. Editing this method changes how input strings are interpreted (case sensitivity, fallbacks, or defaulting), which impacts plugin configuration handling and validation logic.

key_behaviors: parses checksum mode from a string
provides canonical enum constants used by checksum selection logic
exposes stable serialized names for Gson-based JSON representations

pitfalls.mistake: Changing the @SerializedName values ("remote", "local") or the enum internal 'name' strings without coordinating consumers
pitfalls.consequence: Breaks JSON serialization/deserialization and plugin configuration compatibility; persisted lockfiles or external configs may no longer match and deserialization may fail or produce unexpected results.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the existing serialized names and 'name' strings unless you also update all consumers, persisted data formats, and migration logic.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering fromName to perform case-insensitive matching or return a default instead of throwing
pitfalls.consequence: Changes validation semantics that other code (and tests) may depend on; silent acceptance of invalid names could hide configuration errors and produce unexpected runtime behavior.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect exact-match semantics; if behavior must change, ensure callers and tests are updated and version compatibility/migration is considered.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing the fromName method or changing its exception type
pitfalls.consequence: Code relying on IllegalArgumentException to detect invalid modes may break; upstream validation and error handling could behave differently.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the method and its contract stable or update all call sites and tests in a coordinated change.

key_sections: ChecksumModes: enum constants REMOTE and LOCAL and their @SerializedName annotations (defines external textual form)
fromName: parsing helper and its exact-match behavior (validation contract)
skip_unless_needed: the private 'name' field accessor details (simple string holder)

related_files.relationship: Defines the checksum calculator API; ChecksumModes drives which implementation is chosen at runtime.
related_files.reason_to_check: If you change mode names or parsing semantics, update selection logic and any factory that maps modes to implementations.

related_files.relationship: One implementation of checksum calculators associated with LOCAL mode.
related_files.reason_to_check: Verify that mode-to-implementation mapping remains consistent after edits.

related_files.relationship: One implementation of checksum calculators associated with REMOTE mode.
related_files.reason_to_check: Verify serialization/usage in remote-based checksum flows if mode names are modified.

related_files.relationship: Mojo(s) that accept plugin configuration options including checksum mode; they parse inputs that may be converted via fromName.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to parsing or expected string values affect how MoJos interpret user configuration.

tests_to_run: mvn -DskipTests=false test (to run full test suite)
data_constants_to_check: Serialized names: "remote", "local" (these are part of external contract)
Any configuration documentation that mentions mode strings

insights.title: Enum for checksum modes
insights.problem: Checksum mode strings used in multiple places risk typos and duplication.
insights.root_cause: Previously modes were magic strings.
insights.solution: Added ChecksumModes enum with fromName helper to centralize mode names.
insights.lesson_learned: Represent configuration options as enums with helper parsing to reduce stringly-typed bugs.

maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.short_description: Immutable model representing a POM with GAV, resolution metadata, checksum and parent chain
maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.description:
This class is the canonical in-memory representation of a Maven POM as recorded in the project's lockfile. It captures both locally authored POMs (via relativePath) and POMs resolved from repositories (via GAV fields plus ResolvedUrl and RepositoryId). It also stores checksumAlgorithm and checksum for the POM itself and a reference to a parent Pom to represent the POM parent chain. The class is immutable: all fields are final and exposed via getters so instances are safe to use as data values stored in LockFile objects and used for equality/ordering during lockfile generation and validation.

Pom exists to enable deterministic comparison, hashing and serialization of POM metadata inside lockfiles. The compareTo(), equals(), and hashCode() implementations are carefully coordinated to treat nulls for relativePath, resolved and repositoryId as explicit canonical default values (empty string, ResolvedUrl.Unresolved(), RepositoryId.None()). This design allows the rest of the system to compare POMs consistently whether the POM was defined by a relative path in the project or was resolved from an external repository. The parent chain is represented recursively: constructRecursivePom (in LockFileFacade) builds nested Pom objects for parents, and Pom.compareTo/equals walk that chain.

Because Pom participates in equality checks for LockFile contents and in ordering (compareTo), consumers (the Mojos, LockFileFacade and serialization logic) rely on stable, null-safe semantics. Tests exercise these semantics (see LockfileTest and IntegrationTestsIT). The commit history indicates this model replaced a simpler representation that could not distinguish local relative POMs from externally-resolved POMs; fields like resolved and repositoryId were added intentionally so validation/freeze logic can identify where a POM came from and include repository metadata in the lockfile output.

key_constructs.name: Pom
key_constructs.purpose: Immutable data model representing a POM with GAV, resolution metadata, checksum and parent chain
key_constructs.reasoning: Central data structure for lockfile POM entries; used by LockFile and read/compare logic. An agent modifying related code must preserve field meanings (relativePath vs resolved/repositoryId) because consumers and serialization rely on these semantics.

callers.context: new Pom(groupId, artifactId, version, "pom.xml", null, null, "SHA-256", "POM-CHECKSUM", null),
new Pom(groupId, artifactId, version, "pom.xml", null, null, "SHA-256", "POM-CHECKSUM", null),

key_constructs.name: compareTo
key_constructs.purpose: Provides a total ordering of Pom objects for deterministic sorting
key_constructs.reasoning: Used when deterministic ordering of POMs is required (e.g., set-to-list conversions, stable serialization and equality checks). It implements null-handling by mapping nulls to canonical defaults; maintain this when changing fields or equality logic to keep ordering deterministic and consistent with equals.

key_constructs.name: equals
key_constructs.purpose: Defines logical equality of two Pom instances used by collection/set equality and tests
key_constructs.reasoning: Equality intentionally treats null relativePath/resolved/repositoryId as canonical defaults and compares parent chains recursively. Any changes must keep equals consistent with compareTo and hashCode to avoid breaking set semantics and tests that assert lockfile equality regardless of insertion order.

key_constructs.name: hashCode
key_constructs.purpose: Produces a hashcode consistent with equals for use in hashed collections
key_constructs.reasoning: Hashes all stored fields (including parent), so changes to equality behavior require corresponding hashCode updates to preserve contract.

key_constructs.name: getResolved
key_constructs.purpose: Accessor for the ResolvedUrl (where a POM was fetched from), or null for local relative POMs
key_constructs.reasoning: Other code paths inspect this value to determine whether a POM is local or externally-resolved. Treat null as meaning 'local/unknown' and ResolvedUrl.Unresolved() as canonical unresolved value.

key_behaviors: represents a POM with either a project relative path or resolved repository metadata
encodes checksum algorithm and checksum for the POM
represents parent POM chain recursively
provides deterministic compareTo ordering and equals/hashCode for lockfile comparisons

pitfalls.mistake: Treating fields that are sometimes null (relativePath, resolved, repositoryId) as always non-null or changing their canonical default handling.
pitfalls.consequence: NullPointerExceptions in compareTo/equals or inconsistent equality/ordering leading to test failures (Lockfile equality/ordering issues) or nondeterministic lockfile serializations.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect existing null-to-default mapping: relativePath -> "", resolved -> ResolvedUrl.Unresolved(), repositoryId -> RepositoryId.None(). Keep compareTo and equals semantics aligned.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying compareTo without modifying equals/hashCode (or vice versa).
pitfalls.consequence: Violation of the general contract between compareTo/equals/hashCode; collections that rely on ordering or hashing (sorted sets, hash sets) may behave inconsistently, breaking tests like LockfileTest.shouldLockFilesEqualWhenOrderIsChanged.
pitfalls.prevention: Any change to ordering logic must be reflected in equality and hashing semantics so they remain consistent.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming parent chains cannot be deep or contain cycles.
pitfalls.consequence: Recursive compareTo/equals calls could lead to deep recursion or, if cycles are introduced elsewhere, infinite recursion / StackOverflowError.
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure code constructing Pom parent chains (LockFileFacade.constructRecursivePom) prevents cycles and limits depth if appropriate; preserve current acyclic parent-chain assumptions.

pitfalls.mistake: Setting checksumAlgorithm or checksum to null in some code paths without updating null handling.
pitfalls.consequence: compareTo/equals call methods on these strings directly (e.g., checksumAlgorithm.compareTo(...)) and would throw NPE if null.
pitfalls.prevention: Treat checksumAlgorithm and checksum as required (non-null) values when constructing Pom instances; if making them optional, update compareTo/equals to handle null safely.

reading_guide.start_here: Pom (class): inspect fields and constructor to understand which metadata is captured
key_sections: compareTo: deterministic ordering logic, null-to-default mapping and recursive parent compare
equals: equality semantics matching compareTo including canonical defaults
hashCode: mirrors fields used in equals
skip_unless_needed: the simple getter methods (getGroupId, getArtifactId, etc.) — straightforward accessors

mvn -pl maven_plugin verify  (to run integration/verification steps if configured)
