Integration tests that generate lockfiles and assert POM/checksum/plugin metadata presence — these tests exercise how POMs are constructed and serialized into lockfiles.

related_files.relationship: constructs Pom instances (constructRecursivePom) and orchestrates POM parent chain creation and checksum/resolution metadata
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change to Pom fields, null semantics, or equality/ordering must be reflected in constructRecursivePom and serialization logic so produced lockfiles remain consistent.

related_files.relationship: contains Pom as project POM and set of other Pom entries (BOMs); relies on Pom equality and ordering for lockfile equality comparison
related_files.reason_to_check: LockFile.equals/hashCode and serialization expect Pom behavior; changing Pom affects LockFile behavior and tests (LockfileTest).

related_files.relationship: canonical unresolved/resolved URL values are compared inside Pom; Pom maps null resolved field to ResolvedUrl.Unresolved() for comparisons
related_files.reason_to_check: If ResolvedUrl's equality/ordering semantics change, Pom.compareTo/equals behavior must be re-evaluated.

tests_to_run: mvn -pl maven_plugin verify (for integration tests and full verification)

insights.title: Represent POMs with GAV, relativePath, resolved URL, repo id and parent chain
insights.problem: Previously only path+checksum was stored for POMs; external parents had no resolved URL information and parent chain wasn't fully represented.
insights.root_cause: Simplistic Pom model couldn't represent both local relative POMs and externally-resolved POMs with repository metadata.
insights.solution: Switch Pom to contain GroupId, ArtifactId, VersionNumber, relativePath, resolved (ResolvedUrl), repositoryId (RepositoryId), checksum algorithm, checksum, and parent Pom. Implement compareTo, equals, and hashCode accordingly. constructRecursivePom builds recursive Pom chain for parents.
insights.lesson_learned: Model POM metadata explicitly (GAV/resolved URL/repo id) so consumers (validate/freeze) can distinguish local vs external parents. Ensure compareTo/equality take resolved/repo info into account only when present. Adding new fields requires updating tests and sample lockfiles.

maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.short_description: Domain model representing a Maven build plugin, with identity, resolved location, checksum and dependency nodes.
maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.description:
MavenPlugin is a value-type domain model used to represent a Maven build plugin in the generated lockfile. It exists so the plugin machinery can record not only runtime dependencies but also build-time plugins with enough information to validate them later (coordinates, resolved URL, repository id, checksum algorithm and checksum, and the plugin's own dependency nodes). The class is immutable (final fields) and designed to be safely serialized into the lockfile JSON model (LockFile). The model is used both during lockfile generation and validation (and appears in integration tests that assert plugin checksums and dependency capture). 

Design decisions visible in this class: it implements Comparable<MavenPlugin> to provide deterministic ordering when plugins are serialized (commit history explicitly added a compareTo for stable ordering), it treats null dependencies as an empty set (constructor normalizes null to Collections.emptySet()), and it includes checksumAlgorithm/checksum fields so that plugin binary integrity can be validated later. equals/hashCode include the dependencies set and checksum fields, while compareTo compares only groupId, artifactId, version, checksumAlgorithm and checksum (i.e. compareTo ignores the dependencies and repository/resolved fields). Getters expose the stored metadata; toString intentionally shows only coordinates for concise debugging.

key_constructs.name: MavenPlugin
key_constructs.purpose: Immutable data model for a Maven build plugin including coordinates, resolved location, repository id, checksum, algorithm and plugin dependency nodes.
key_constructs.reasoning: This class is the serialized representation included in the LockFile. It must carry the data necessary for later validation and deterministic sorting/serialization.

key_constructs.name: compareTo
key_constructs.purpose: Implements ordering for deterministic sorting of MavenPlugin instances.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ordering is used when serializing plugins to ensure stable lockfile diffs; the comparator orders by groupId, artifactId, version, checksumAlgorithm, checksum.

key_constructs.name: getDependencies
key_constructs.purpose: Returns the plugin's dependency nodes (Set<DependencyNode>), never null (emptySet if no dependencies).
key_constructs.reasoning: Plugin dependencies are recorded to allow validation of plugin transitive artifacts; the empty-set normalization avoids null checks elsewhere.

key_constructs.name: equals
key_constructs.purpose: Equality test used to identify plugin identity for set membership and testing.
key_constructs.reasoning: Equals includes groupId, artifactId, version, checksumAlgorithm, checksum and dependencies—this affects behavior in collections and must be respected when modifying identity semantics.

key_constructs.name: hashCode
key_constructs.purpose: Hash code consistent with equals for use in hash-based collections.
key_constructs.reasoning: Keeps object identity behavior consistent in hash-based containers; includes same fields as equals.

key_behaviors: represents maven build plugin metadata for lockfile serialization
provides deterministic ordering via compareTo for stable lockfile diffs
stores plugin checksum and algorithm for later validation
exposes plugin dependency nodes (transitive plugin deps) as a set

pitfalls.mistake: Assume compareTo and equals use the same fields.
pitfalls.consequence: Collections relying on compareTo (e.g., TreeSet) may be inconsistent with equals because compareTo ignores dependencies and resolved/repository fields while equals includes dependencies. This can create surprising membership/ordering behavior.
pitfalls.prevention: When editing or using this class, be aware compareTo's fields versus equals/hashCode fields and the resulting consistency implications.

pitfalls.mistake: Passing null for checksumAlgorithm or checksum and relying on compareTo to handle it.
pitfalls.consequence: compareTo dereferences checksumAlgorithm and checksum without null checks and will throw NullPointerException if either is null.
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure checksumAlgorithm and checksum are non-null when compareTo is expected to be used; constructor callers (and code creating instances) should supply values or guard usage.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming getDependencies() may return null.
pitfalls.consequence: Null-checks scattered around code would be redundant or hiding normalization; in fact getDependencies() returns emptySet when constructed with null.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the contract that dependencies are normalized to Collections.emptySet() by the constructor.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying toString() to include more fields without considering serialized output or logging sensitivity.
pitfalls.consequence: May leak large structures or sensitive data into logs; toString is relied on for concise debugging in tests and logs.
pitfalls.prevention: If changing toString(), keep it succinct and consider what is safe to display in CI logs.

key_sections: MavenPlugin: constructor normalization of dependencies and final field assignments
compareTo: ordering logic used for stable serialization and diffs
equals & hashCode: identity semantics (note dependencies are included)
getDependencies: guarantees non-null empty set
skip_unless_needed: toString: intentionally limited output; only for debugging
getResolved/getRepositoryId: these getters are simple accessors; inspect only when resolving plugin locations

test_functions: IntegrationTestsIT::pluginProject (verifies maven plugins captured with checksums and dependencies)

relevant_snippets.description: Unit tests that exercise compareTo ordering across coordinates, checksum algorithm and checksum.
Integration test portions that assert maven plugins are present in the generated lockfile with non-blank checksums and non-empty dependencies.

related_files.relationship: MavenPlugin instances are stored inside the LockFile model for serialization into the lockfile JSON.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changing MavenPlugin fields or serialization format will affect LockFile JSON schema and consumers; check LockFile reader/writer and JSON mapping.

related_files.relationship: ChecksumAlgorithm and checksum fields are produced by checksum calculators; this class must align with how checksums are computed and named.
related_files.reason_to_check: If checksum naming or calculation changes, update how MavenPlugin is populated.

related_files.relationship: Integration tests validate that MavenPlugin entries in lockfile have checksums/algorithm and dependency nodes populated.
related_files.reason_to_check: If the model changes (fields added/removed or nullability altered), integration tests may need updates.

tests_to_run: mvn test (to include integration tests where CI permits)

insights.title: Make MavenPlugin comparable for stable ordering in lockfile
insights.problem: Maven plugins were ordered by checksum previously (unstable or non-intuitive); lockfile ordering should be deterministic across runs.
insights.root_cause: Set comparator used previously (by checksum) could produce inconsistent ordering and made diffs noisy.
insights.solution: Implement Comparable<MavenPlugin> comparing groupId, artifactId, version, checksumAlgorithm, checksum to get stable ordering.
insights.lesson_learned: Domain objects that appear in sets/TreeSets used for serialization should implement deterministic comparison consistent with equals to reduce spurious diffs.

insights.title: MavenPlugin model extended with resolved url, repository id and dependencies
insights.problem: Initial model didn't capture plugin resolved location, repository id, or plugin dependency nodes (needed for full validation).
insights.root_cause: Requirement to validate plugins and their transitive dependencies and to record where plugins were resolved from.
insights.solution: Add fields: ResolvedUrl resolved, RepositoryId repositoryId, Set<DependencyNode> dependencies; update constructors, equals/hashCode, toString; ensure null->emptySet handling.
insights.lesson_learned: When extending persisted models ensure default values (empty sets or sentinel types) to preserve compatibility. Update equals/hashCode consistently to reflect intended identity semantics.

insights.title: Record plugin checksum and algorithm in lockfile
insights.problem: Plugin resolution/validation lacked checksum and algorithm fields; could not validate plugin binary integrity.
insights.root_cause: MavenPlugin model only had group/artifact/version — no checksum data.
insights.solution: Add checksumAlgorithm and checksum fields to MavenPlugin, include them in equals/hashCode, and populate via checksum calculator when gathering plugin artifacts.
insights.lesson_learned: Treat plugins like normal artifacts: capture resolved checksum+algorithm so lockfile can validate plugin binaries too.

insights.title: Model class for Maven build plugins
insights.problem: Build plugins were not represented in lockfile data model.
insights.root_cause: LockFile previously only modelled dependencies and lacked plugin representation.
insights.solution: Added MavenPlugin class (groupId, artifactId, version) with equals/hashCode and included in LockFile.
insights.lesson_learned: Represent build-time artifacts (plugins) separately from dependency graph; include them in reproducibility artifacts if requested.

maven_plugin/src/test/java/io/github/cha.short_description: Unit tests verifying MavenPlugin.compareTo ordering semantics across identity and checksum fields.
maven_plugin/src/test/java/io/github/cha.description: This JUnit 5 unit test class validates the deterministic ordering implemented in MavenPlugin.compareTo. The tests create MavenPlugin instances via a helper createPlugin (which supplies GroupId, ArtifactId, VersionNumber via their .of(...) factories and leaves resolved/repository fields null). The suite checks that compareTo returns zero for identical coordinate+checksum combos, and that ordering falls back in sequence from groupId to artifactId to version to checksumAlgorithm to checksum. These tests codify the expected ordering used to produce stable serialized plugin lists in the lockfile, and they document the relative significance of each field in the comparator.

key_constructs.name: MavenPluginTest
key_constructs.purpose: JUnit test class containing test cases for MavenPlugin ordering.
key_constructs.reasoning: This class provides unit-level contracts for compareTo behavior; changes to MavenPlugin ordering semantics will be caught by these tests.

key_constructs.name: compareToReturnsZeroForIdenticalPlugins
key_constructs.purpose: Asserts compareTo returns zero for two plugins constructed with identical coordinates and checksum data.
key_constructs.reasoning: Serves as a basic equality/order consistency test to ensure comparator treats identical plugins as equal in ordering.

key_constructs.name: compareToOrdersByGroupIdFirst
key_constructs.purpose: Asserts ordering primarily by groupId.
key_constructs.reasoning: Verifies the top-level ordering key is groupId, which is key for deterministic plugin lists.

key_constructs.name: compareToFallsBackToArtifactIdWhenGroupIdEqual
key_constructs.purpose: Asserts artifactId is used if groupIds are equal.
key_constructs.reasoning: Documents and asserts second-level ordering semantics.

key_constructs.name: compareToFallsBackToVersionWhenGroupIdAndArtifactIdEqual
key_constructs.purpose: Asserts version is used as third-level ordering key.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures version participates in comparator tie-breaking.

key_constructs.name: compareToFallsBackToChecksumAlgorithmWhenCoordinatesEqual
key_constructs.purpose: Asserts checksumAlgorithm is used when coordinates are identical.
key_constructs.reasoning: Validates that checksumAlgorithm affects ordering, which is important because lockfile includes algorithm name.

key_constructs.name: compareToFallsBackToChecksumWhenAllElseEqual
key_constructs.purpose: Asserts checksum string is last tie-breaker.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures distinct binaries for same coordinates are ordered deterministically by checksum.

key_behaviors: validates compareTo returns zero for identical plugins
validates comparator priority: groupId -> artifactId -> version -> checksumAlgorithm -> checksum

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming tests cover null/edge-case inputs for compareTo (e.g., null checksumAlgorithm or checksum).
pitfalls.consequence: NPEs or different behavior in production code won't be caught by these tests.
pitfalls.prevention: When editing or extending MavenPlugin behavior, remember the unit tests only cover non-null fields; add tests if nullability is changed.

pitfalls.mistake: Relying only on these unit tests to validate overall lockfile plugin handling.
pitfalls.consequence: Integration-level behaviors (dependency resolution, checksum calculation, serialization) are not covered; changes may break integration scenarios.
pitfalls.prevention: Run IntegrationTestsIT (or full test suite) for end-to-end verification when changing plugin model or comparator.

test methods: each method demonstrates one comparator tie-breaker rule

maven_plugin/pom.xml.short_description: Maven module POM configuring the maven-lockfile plugin build, dependencies, and publishing profile
maven_plugin/pom.xml.description:
This file is the Maven POM for the maven_plugin module (artifactId maven-lockfile). It declares the module's coordinates, parent relationship, properties that drive compilation/maven versions, the dependency set used by the plugin and tests, build plugins (including the maven-plugin-plugin that generates mojo descriptors and the failsafe plugin used by integration tests), and a publication profile that attaches javadocs/sources for release. The POM exists to: (1) define the artifact coordinates and packaging as a maven-plugin, (2) provide a curated and pinned dependency list (including provided/test scopes appropriate for Maven plugin development), and (3) configure build-time behavior for test/integration-test and publication phases.

The POM is central to how the module is built and released. Important design decisions encoded here include pinning a specific Maven version via the mavenVersion property (which drives several provided dependencies like maven-core and maven-model), isolating test-scoped dependencies (including explicit pinned assertj-core to mitigate a historical XXE vulnerability), and using specific plugin versions for maven-plugin-plugin, gmavenplus, and failsafe to reproduce CI behavior. The publication profile is intentionally separated under the 'publication' profile to only attach javadocs/sources and enable deployment when that profile is active. The maven-failsafe-plugin configuration includes parallel JUnit Jupiter settings and a rerun strategy (rerunFailingTestsCount) intended to stabilize flaky integration tests.

When making changes to this file, respect the module's role as authoritative metadata for the plugin artifact (coordinates, packaging, parent relation) and the pinned dependency versions that influence runtime/resolution behavior of the plugin and its tests. The parent POM/parent version (declared by relativePath and parent coordinates) is also important: mismatches between child and parent versions have historically caused integration failures and should be kept consistent. The file also contains specific comments and workarounds (e.g., goalPrefix to address MNG-5346) which reflect decisions to maintain compatibility with certain Maven behaviors and the CI/testing environment.

key_constructs.reasoning: This element defines the published coordinate for the module. Changing it affects consumers, CI, and any references elsewhere in the repo (integration tests, README, GitHub Action).

key_constructs.reasoning: These properties are consumed by dependencies and plugins; adjustments alter compile target and dependency versions implicitly resolved from properties.

key_constructs.reasoning: Dependency scopes (provided/test) are important to produce a plugin that compiles against Maven APIs while not shipping the Maven implementation inside the plugin artifact.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing the mavenVersion property or provided-scoped Maven dependencies without coordination
pitfalls.consequence: Can cause compilation failures or subtle runtime incompatibilities when the plugin runs against different Maven API classes; integration tests may fail on CI.
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure any change to mavenVersion is accompanied by appropriate dependency updates and run full module tests and integration tests.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering artifactId, groupId or version without updating references elsewhere
pitfalls.consequence: Will break consumers, integration test resource references, and the GitHub Action that invokes this plugin by coordinate; may also break release automation.
pitfalls.prevention: Verify all repo-wide references (integration test descriptors, GitHub Action config, README) if coordinates change.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or changing explicit dependency pins like assertj-core that were added to mitigate known vulnerabilities
pitfalls.consequence: May reintroduce security issues (e.g., historical XXE vulnerability) or cause test regressions if transitive versions differ.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep or reconcile security-related pins with the project-wide security policy and CVE tracking before modifying.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying failsafe plugin parallel and rerun settings without re-running integration test suite
pitfalls.consequence: May mask flaky tests or surface concurrency-related test failures that were previously suppressed/handled.
pitfalls.prevention: Run 'mvn -pl maven_plugin verify' and the integration tests using the same settings to validate changes.

insights.title: POM edits and plugin version bumps (non-functional behavioral changes)
insights.problem: POM files were updated to pin plugin versions and add descriptions/metadata.
insights.root_cause: Repository maintenance and release process improvements.
insights.solution: Multiple commits updated snapshot versions, descriptions, plugin additions and enforced plugin versions in parent POM.
insights.lesson_learned: Version and plugin pinning are common maintenance tasks — watch for unrelated API changes when bumping Maven plugins which may affect behavior of dependency resolution or plugin execution.

insights.title: Remove long description from maven_plugin/pom.xml to satisfy central/pom quality or brevity
insights.problem: POM included a long description that may have been redundant with centralized docs or violated desired POM formatting for Central checks.
insights.root_cause: Large description text embedded in plugin POM.
insights.solution: Removed the description block from maven_plugin/pom.xml.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep POM metadata concise; prefer longer docs in README/template instead of embedding long paragraphs in plugin POMs if not required by repository metadata standards.

insights.title: Clean up POM description and snapshot version toggles
insights.problem: POM description removed in one commit (likely cosmetic) and snapshot parent version toggled to '-SNAPSHOT' in others; inconsistent versions across modules can break local tooling that expects SNAPSHOT.
insights.root_cause: Release/version bump workflow and edits to parent pom versions required updates in child module POMs.
insights.solution: Made POM versions consistent with parent (switching to 1.0.13-SNAPSHOT) and removed long description block (cosmetic).
insights.lesson_learned: Keep parent and module versions aligned during snapshot/release cycles; changing the POM description is cosmetic and not functional, but version mismatches cause hard-to-diagnose resolution issues.

insights.title: Standardize module naming and bump versions
insights.problem: POM names and parent coordinates were inconsistent (artifactId/name mismatches) and versions were snapshots / older.
insights.root_cause: Initial project scaffolding left inconsistent artifact and module names.
insights.solution: Set consistent names (maven-lockfile-plugin, maven-lockfile-parent) and bump versions to aligned 1.0.0 across modules.
insights.lesson_learned: Consistent POM metadata (groupId/artifactId/version/name) prevents confusion during publication and improves readability; centralize parent versioning.

insights.title: ArtifactId and coordinates changed during rebrand
insights.problem: The maven plugin artifactId and version were renamed from 'integrity-maven-plugin' to 'maven-lockfile' and version bumped; failing to keep all references synchronized causes build/run errors in CI and in the GitHub Action that invokes the plugin.
insights.root_cause: Rebranding/renaming without updating all referencing strings and CI steps in one atomic change.
insights.solution: Update plugin artifact coordinates across the repo (integration test resources, README, GitHub Action constants) and bump versions consistently. Also ensure generated release workflow updates these strings where needed.
insights.lesson_learned: Renaming artifacts is a breaking change; update all references (tests, docs, CI) and consider deprecation steps or compatibility shims if consumers depend on old coordinates.

insights.title: Tidy POM metadata and property formatting
insights.problem: Formatting and property fragments were inconsistent in pom.xml (split tags, whitespace).
insights.root_cause: Various edits over multiple commits caused formatting drift in the parent and module POMs.
insights.solution: Normalize properties formatting (single-line tags), remove or reintroduce SCM/developer/license blocks as appropriate in parent POM.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep POM metadata consistent and ensure SCM and developer information are present in parent POMs for releases.

maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.short_description: Central Gson configuration for (de)serializing lockfile domain value objects to compact JSON
maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.description:
JsonUtils provides a single place where all custom Gson type adapters are registered for the maven-lockfile plugin. Its purpose is to ensure domain-specific small value objects (VersionNumber, ArtifactId, GroupId, NodeId, MavenScope, ResolvedUrl, RepositoryId, ArtifactType, Classifier, etc.) are serialized as JSON primitives (usually strings) and deserialized back into their typed wrappers. This keeps the lockfile JSON compact and stable across versions of the plugin. The GsonBuilder is configured to pretty-print and set lenient parsing, which affects both serialization formatting and tolerance when reading previously generated lockfiles.

The class exposes three entry points: a private getGson() factory that configures and creates a Gson instance with all adapters registered, and two public helpers toJson(Object) and fromJson(String, Class<T>) that delegate to that Gson instance. The design keeps registration centralized so any added or modified small domain types must be reflected here to preserve the lockfile JSON format. There is a commented-out adapter registration for LockFileDependency which indicates historical or potential future considerations for custom handling of composite types.

This file is used by lockfile generation and validation logic to serialize LockFile and its nested types and to deserialize lockfiles back into the Java model. Changes to adapter behavior, registered classes, representation format (e.g., switching from primitive string to object), or leniency will affect lockfile compatibility and tests that assert JSON output, so edits must respect backward compatibility and test coverage.

key_constructs.name: JsonUtils
key_constructs.purpose: Encapsulates Gson configuration and provides JSON (de)serialization helpers for domain value objects.
key_constructs.reasoning: Centralizing Gson adapters prevents duplicated registration across the codebase and ensures a single canonical JSON representation for lockfile-related types; an editing agent should preserve the registry semantics and adapter mappings when modifying domain types.

key_constructs.name: getGson
key_constructs.purpose: Creates and returns a Gson instance with all registered type adapters and formatting options (pretty printing, lenient).
key_constructs.reasoning: All (de)serialization goes through this factory: modifying adapter registrations here directly changes JSON input/output for many domain classes; an agent must maintain adapter order and presence for compatibility.

key_constructs.name: toJson
key_constructs.purpose: Serializes a Java object to a JSON string using the configured Gson instance.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used across the plugin to write lockfile JSON; tests and downstream consumers assert the output shape, so any behavior changes to toJson (via getGson) affect persisted files.

key_constructs.name: fromJson
key_constructs.purpose: Deserializes a JSON string into a Java object of the given class using the configured Gson instance.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to read existing lockfiles; lenient parsing and the registered deserializers are important to ensure older lockfiles and different formatting remain readable.

key_behaviors: serializes domain value objects to JSON primitives
deserializes JSON primitives back into typed wrappers
produces pretty-printed JSON output
parses JSON leniently to tolerate minor format differences

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or renaming a registered type adapter (e.g., for VersionNumber or NodeId).
pitfalls.consequence: Lockfile JSON representation will change (becoming verbose or incompatible) and existing lockfiles may not deserialize correctly; tests asserting JSON output may fail.
pitfalls.prevention: When editing, retain adapter mappings or provide migration/deserialization logic; update tests that depend on representation.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing a value object's toString/fromValue contract (e.g., NodeId) without updating the adapter.
pitfalls.consequence: Deserialization will break or produce incorrect objects; lockfile validation will detect mismatches.
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure adapter logic matches the exact stable string form used by the value type.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering lenient parsing or pretty printing without considering backward compatibility.
pitfalls.consequence: Older lockfiles may fail to parse or outputs may differ in whitespace/formatting expected by tests or consumers.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve setLenient() and pretty-printing unless tests/data are updated in coordination.

setPrettyPrinting()/setLenient() configuration: explains formatting and parsing tolerance

insights.problem: New ArtifactType class needs Gson adapters to (de)serialize to simple string values in lockfile JSON.
insights.root_cause: Gson requires explicit type adapters for custom types to control representation and avoid verbose objects.
insights.solution: Register JsonSerializer and JsonDeserializer for ArtifactType preserving its string value in JSON.
insights.lesson_learned: When adding small value-objects intended to be compact in JSON, add serializer/deserializer adapters to keep the JSON representation stable.

insights.title: JSON (de)serialization for Classifier added
insights.problem: New Classifier type needed custom (de)serializer so lockfile JSON preserves classifier field correctly.
insights.root_cause: Gson default (de)serialization would not map Classifier<String> to the expected JSON primitive form and back.
insights.solution: Register serializer and deserializer for Classifier to convert to/from primitive JSON string.
insights.lesson_learned: When introducing a new value type used in JSON models, ensure custom (de)serializers are registered so existing lockfiles remain readable/writable.

insights.title: Register MavenScope adapter and enable lenient JSON parsing
insights.problem: Custom types (MavenScope) needed specific serialization/deserialization and some lockfile JSON inputs required lenient parsing.
insights.root_cause: Default Gson configuration didn't handle custom enums cleanly and could be strict on some JSON formats.
insights.solution: Register type adapters for MavenScope for both serialization and deserialization and call setLenient() on GsonBuilder.
insights.lesson_learned: When extending serialized models, ensure consistent serializers/deserializers are registered for new types and consider lenient parsing when reading previously produced JSON that might contain non-standard formatting.

maven_plugin/src/test/java/io/github/cha.short_description: Unit test ensuring JsonUtils serializes a Set of DependencyNode without producing "null"
maven_plugin/src/test/java/io/github/cha.description:
This JUnit test verifies a specific serialization behavior: converting a Set<DependencyNode> created from a Guava Sets.difference view into JSON should not result in the literal string "null". The test uses Instancio to produce synthetic DependencyNode instances (randomized test data). It constructs two single-element HashSets with different random nodes, computes their set difference via Guava Sets.difference, wraps that view in a concrete HashSet (important: avoids serializing a Guava view directly), asserts the result size is 1, and then ensures JsonUtils.toJson(result) does not equal the String "null".

The test exists because historical regressions caused serialization of certain set-view implementations or value objects to produce null or otherwise invalid JSON output. It therefore exercises the JsonUtils configuration (type adapters and Gson settings) in a focused way and acts as a regression guard when modifying adapters or the value-object types used by the lockfile.

key_constructs.name: JsonUtilsTest
key_constructs.purpose: Test class grouping tests related to JsonUtils serialization.
key_constructs.reasoning: Naming and placement mean this test is the canonical unit test for the JsonUtils behavior; an editing agent should preserve its assertions when changing serialization representation.

key_constructs.name: set_view_to_json_does_not_return_null
key_constructs.purpose: Asserts that serializing a HashSet created from a Guava set-difference view does not produce the string "null".
key_constructs.reasoning: Targets a past bug: serializing set views or certain graph node instances could yield null; any edits to JsonUtils or related value types should keep this test green or be updated intentionally.

key_behaviors: constructs randomized DependencyNode instances
builds set difference via Guava and converts to a concrete HashSet
verifies JsonUtils.toJson does not emit the literal "null"

pitfalls.consequence: If DependencyNode structure changes, Instancio generation might produce nodes that differ in required fields and the test could fail or become flaky.
pitfalls.prevention: When changing DependencyNode, update the test data generation or switch to deterministic factory instances.

pitfalls.mistake: Serializing a Guava Sets.difference view directly (without wrapping in a concrete collection).
pitfalls.consequence: Gson could attempt to serialize the view implementation and produce unexpected output or null.
pitfalls.prevention: The test wraps the view in new HashSet<>(Sets.difference(...)) — preserve this pattern in other tests or code paths that serialize set views.

maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.short_description: Immutable data model for the JSON lockfile (project identity, POM, deps, plugins, metadata, BOMs).
maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.description:
Represents the project's lockfile structure used by the maven-lockfile plugin. This class is the Java model that is serialized to and deserialized from the lockfile JSON (default: lockfile.json). It encapsulates the project's GAV-like identity (groupId, artifactId/name, version), a reference to the POM (Pom), the resolved dependency graph roots (Set<DependencyNode>), recorded Maven plugins (Set<MavenPlugin>), metadata about the environment and generation configuration (MetaData), and a collection of project-level BOM POMs (Set<Pom>). The class uses Gson @SerializedName to provide JSON field names and alternate names for backwards compatibility (artifactId and groupId alternates).

The constructor normalizes nullable collection inputs to Collections.emptySet() for dependencies, mavenPlugins, and boms so consumers can treat getters as non-null. A static readLockFile(Path) utility reads a file into a String and delegates to JsonUtils.fromJson(...) to obtain a LockFile instance. Accessor methods expose the contained entities; getEnvironment() and getConfig() are convenience accessors delegating to the embedded MetaData. equals() and hashCode() are overridden to support equality checks used by unit/integration tests and logic that compares generated vs. expected lockfiles.

This model is a central piece of the lockfile generation/validation flow: code that constructs a LockFile (LockFileFacade.generateLockFileFromProject) must produce instances that serialize to the exact JSON schema expected by the deserializers used in tests and CI. Tests in the repository (unit LockfileTest and integration tests) instantiate LockFile directly to assert order-insensitive equality and also deserialize lockfile.json produced by plugin runs to assert contents. Because this class participates in equality comparisons and serialization, changes to its fields or JSON names are breaking for consumers and tests; the commit history shows it has been extended (Config, BOMs) and normalized to use Sets to make comparisons order-insensitive.

key_constructs.name: LockFile
key_constructs.purpose: Data model for the generated lockfile JSON; holds project identity, POM, dependencies, plugins, metadata and BOMs.
key_constructs.reasoning: The lockfile must be serializable and comparable; using an immutable-like final-fields pattern with null-to-empty normalization ensures deterministic reads and null-safety for consumers and tests.

key_constructs.name: hashCode
key_constructs.purpose: Generates a hash code for LockFile instances.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used when LockFile instances are placed in hashed collections; should align with equals() to avoid violations of the equals/hashCode contract.

key_behaviors: deserializes lockfile JSON from disk via readLockFile
exposes lockfile contents (dependencies, plugins, BOMs) via getters
normalizes null collections to empty sets for deterministic behavior
participates in equality checks used in tests and validations

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming equals() compares all fields included in hashCode()
pitfalls.consequence: equals() and hashCode() are currently inconsistent (pom is included in hashCode() but not compared in equals()). This can cause incorrect behavior when LockFile instances are used in hashed collections or compared.
pitfalls.prevention: When editing, preserve consistency between equals() and hashCode(); review tests that compare LockFile instances and serialization consumers that may rely on hashCode behavior.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering JSON field names or @SerializedName alternates without updating consumers/tests
pitfalls.consequence: Deserialization of existing lockfiles will break and integration tests that read lockfile.json will fail; CI workflows consuming lockfile JSON (GitHub Action) may be impacted.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the existing @SerializedName mappings (including alternate names like artifactID/groupID) and update tests/resources in lockfile JSON format if schema changes are necessary.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing null-to-empty semantics for collections
pitfalls.consequence: Callers and tests assume getters return non-null Sets; changing this can introduce NPEs or test failures expecting empty collections rather than null.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the constructor normalization and nullToEmpty behavior when adding new collection fields or changing getters.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or renaming metadata fields (MetaData, Config) used by tests
pitfalls.consequence: Integration tests check getEnvironment() and getConfig(); removing or altering these fields will cause test failures and mismatch in validation behavior.
pitfalls.prevention: Treat MetaData and its accessors as part of the public data surface for lockfile consumers and CI validation.

key_sections: constructor: normalization of nullable collections and required fields
readLockFile: file I/O and JsonUtils deserialization
getDependencies / getMavenPlugins / getBoms: how collections are exposed (null-to-empty)
equals & hashCode: how LockFile instances are compared
skip_unless_needed: private nullToEmpty helper (trivial)
field annotations for simple JSON name alternates (boilerplate)

relevant_snippets.description: Unit test shouldLockFilesEqualWhenOrderIsChanged constructs two LockFile instances with different insertion orders and asserts equality (order-insensitivity).
Integration tests that generate and read lockfile.json; verify dependencies, environment and plugin contents after deserialization via LockFile.readLockFile.

related_files.relationship: Deserialization/serialization utility used by readLockFile and by code that writes lockfile JSON.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to JSON format, field names, or Gson configuration must be kept in sync with JsonUtils to preserve (de)serialization behavior.

related_files.relationship: DependencyNode instances are stored in the dependencies Set; equals/ordering semantics of DependencyNode affect LockFile equality and serialization.
related_files.reason_to_check: If DependencyNode's comparator or equality changes it will impact order-insensitive comparisons of LockFile; tests rely on stable behavior.

related_files.relationship: MavenPlugin instances are stored in LockFile.mavenPlugins and are asserted in integration tests for checksum and dependencies.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change to MavenPlugin JSON fields or equality affects LockFile serialization semantics and plugin-related tests.

related_files.relationship: Pom objects are stored in LockFile.pom and LockFile.boms; their checksum/resolution fields are serialized into the lockfile JSON.
related_files.reason_to_check: Modifying Pom fields affects content that tests assert (e.g., BOM checksums) and the overall lockfile JSON schema.

related_files.relationship: MetaData is embedded in LockFile and getEnvironment/getConfig delegate to it; integration tests assert presence of environment and config values.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to MetaData or Config fields require updating LockFile accessors and tests that depend on those values.

tests_to_run: mvn -pl maven_plugin -DskipITs=false verify (for integration tests under src/test/java/it)

insights.title: Add boms collection to LockFile data model
insights.problem: Lockfile JSON schema and LockFile class previously did not include project-level BOMs.
insights.root_cause: BOMs were not being captured; adding them requires schema and equality updates.
insights.solution: Add Set<Pom> boms field, include in constructor, getter, equals and hashCode. New field serializes to JSON (null-to-empty semantics maintained).
insights.lesson_learned: Adding fields to a serialization model is a breaking/forward-compatible change for consumers expecting exact JSON — ensure callers/tests/resources are updated. Use null-to-empty helper for compatibility.

insights.title: LockFile model switched to use Set for dependencies and plugins; embed MetaData
insights.problem: LockFile JSON equality and object equality were order-sensitive; previous Metadata/Config split led to scattered accessors.
insights.root_cause: Use of Lists for dependencies/plugins caused differences depending on insertion order; Metadata and Config values were held separately.
insights.solution: Change dependencies and mavenPlugins fields to Set, default to emptySet(), and store MetaData (environment + config) as one field. Update getters accordingly and ensure equals() performs order-insensitive comparisons.
insights.lesson_learned: Lockfile data models should use order-insensitive collections where ordering is irrelevant; group related metadata into a single container to simplify API.

insights.title: LockFile model extended to include Config
insights.problem: LockFile previously did not contain the Config object, so validation could not consistently reproduce generation settings.
insights.root_cause: Earlier lockfile model omitted generation metadata/configuration.
insights.solution: Extend LockFile constructor and model to store a Config instance and expose getConfig() (nullable).
insights.lesson_learned: When extending serialized models, add nullable getters and handle absent fields gracefully to maintain backward compatibility; update consumers to warn/use defaults when config is missing.

insights.title: Make dependency and plugin lists immutable-safe and add metadata
insights.problem: Null lists in LockFile caused NPEs when consumers assumed lists were non-null; metadata was absent making environment mismatches opaque.
insights.root_cause: Constructor accepted null and stored directly; no metadata field to surface build environment.
insights.solution: Constructor now normalizes null lists to Collections.emptyList(), fields made final where appropriate, added Metadata field and getter, updated equals/hashCode to include groupId and mavenPlugins (null-safe via helper).
insights.lesson_learned: Normalize nullable collections to empty lists in data models and include environment metadata to help diagnose validation failures across different CI/machine conditions.

maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.short_description: Value object combining a resolved repository URL and repository id with equality semantics.
maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.description:
RepositoryInformation is a small immutable value object used to carry two pieces of related information together: a ResolvedUrl and a RepositoryId. It exists so checksum calculators and other resolution code can return both the resolved URL (or an unresolved sentinel) and the repository identifier as a single unit, avoiding multiple return values or scattered Optionals. The class provides a factory method Unresolved() to create a canonical "unresolved" sentinel (wrapping ResolvedUrl.Unresolved() and RepositoryId.None()), getters for both fields, and standard equals/hashCode implementations so instances can be compared or used as cache keys.

The presence of equals/hashCode is intentional: commit history shows this was added to support caching comparisons and storing the Unresolved sentinel. The class is intentionally simple: fields are final, there are no defensive copies, and Objects.equals is used in equality checks, making comparisons null-tolerant. It is designed to be used by checksum calculators, cache layers, and components that need to persist or compare resolution results during lockfile generation/validation flows.

key_constructs.name: RepositoryInformation
key_constructs.purpose: Encapsulates both a ResolvedUrl and a RepositoryId into a single immutable value object.
key_constructs.reasoning: Other parts of the system need to return both resolved URL metadata and repository id together; bundling them reduces API surface and avoids multiple Optionals or parallel return paths. Being immutable and having equality semantics makes it safe to use as a map key or cache value.

key_constructs.name: Unresolved
key_constructs.purpose: Factory static method returning a canonical RepositoryInformation representing an unresolved repository.
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides a clear sentinel value for callers to detect unresolved resolution without creating ad-hoc nulls or special-case fields. The method delegates to ResolvedUrl.Unresolved() and RepositoryId.None() to produce a consistent sentinel.

callers.context: ResolvedUrl resolvedCmp = this.resolved == null ? ResolvedUrl.Unresolved() : this.resolved;
ResolvedUrl oResolvedCmp = o.resolved == null ? ResolvedUrl.Unresolved() : o.resolved;
ResolvedUrl resolvedCmp = this.resolved == null ? ResolvedUrl.Unresolved() : this.resolved;
ResolvedUrl otherResolvedCmp = other.resolved == null ? ResolvedUrl.Unresolved() : other.resolved;

key_constructs.name: getResolvedUrl
key_constructs.purpose: Accessor for the contained ResolvedUrl.
key_constructs.reasoning: Callers (checksum calculators, downstream logic) read the resolved URL to decide whether to use local file checksumming or remote resolution and to persist resolved metadata into the lockfile.

key_constructs.name: getRepositoryId
key_constructs.purpose: Accessor for the contained RepositoryId.
key_constructs.reasoning: Callers use the repository id (when present) to record where an artifact/pom/plugin was obtained from; this is persisted into lockfile data structures and used in validation.

key_constructs.name: equals
key_constructs.purpose: Defines structural equality based on resolvedUrl and repositoryId.
key_constructs.reasoning: Equality is required to support caching and to allow Map/Set membership checks; it must remain consistent with hashCode and the semantics of the contained types.

key_constructs.name: hashCode
key_constructs.purpose: Produces a hash code aligned with equals, using resolvedUrl and repositoryId.
key_constructs.reasoning: Enables correct behavior when RepositoryInformation instances are used as keys in hash-based collections (caches).

key_behaviors: represents resolved repository metadata as a single unit
provides an Unresolved sentinel for resolution-failure cases
supports equality/hash-based collection usage

pitfalls.mistake: Assume instances are mutable and change fields after insertion into maps/sets
pitfalls.consequence: If fields were mutated (they are final now), hash-based collections would become inconsistent and caches would break or fail to find entries.
pitfalls.prevention: Treat RepositoryInformation as immutable and avoid adding mutability to its fields.

pitfalls.mistake: Replace or change the equals/hashCode semantics (fields used or null handling)
pitfalls.consequence: Cache lookups and sentinel comparisons (including Unresolved) will behave differently, potentially causing duplicated cache entries or incorrect resolution-detection.
pitfalls.prevention: When editing, keep equals/hashCode aligned and consistent with the contained types' equality behavior.

pitfalls.mistake: Return nulls from the constructor for resolvedUrl or repositoryId or remove the Unresolved factory
pitfalls.consequence: Callers may rely on the Unresolved sentinel; nulls would change behavior and require null-checks across code that currently uses Objects.equals safely.
pitfalls.prevention: Prefer using ResolvedUrl.Unresolved() and RepositoryId.None() sentinel methods already present in the codebase rather than nulls.

key_sections: Unresolved: canonical factory for unresolved resolution (useful for cache sentinel checks)
equals: structural equality for cache and comparison semantics
hashCode: consistent hashing for use in hash-based collections
skip_unless_needed: Javadoc comments (short accessors); boilerplate getters

related_files.relationship: Defines the ResolvedUrl type used as one of the two fields; Unresolved() factory delegates to ResolvedUrl.Unresolved().
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change to ResolvedUrl equality, sentinel behavior, or factory names affects RepositoryInformation equality and Unresolved semantics.

related_files.relationship: Defines the RepositoryId type used as the second field; Unresolved() delegates to RepositoryId.None().
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to RepositoryId None/None() factory or equality semantics will change RepositoryInformation behavior.

related_files.relationship: Checksum calculators likely return RepositoryInformation instances or use them during resolution.
related_files.reason_to_check: If RepositoryInformation constructor/signature changes, all calculators and their callers need updates.

related_files.relationship: One implementation that will produce or consume RepositoryInformation to indicate resolved POM/artifact locations.
related_files.reason_to_check: Check how Unresolved sentinel is used when local file resolution fails.

related_files.relationship: Remote resolver counterpart that may populate repository id and resolved URL fields.
related_files.reason_to_check: Ensure that remote resolution still produces the expected RepositoryInformation sentinel or populated fields.

insights.title: Add equals/hashCode to RepositoryInformation for caching comparisons
insights.problem: RepositoryInformation lacked equals/hashCode which made cache usage and comparisons awkward.
insights.root_cause: Value object used in caching/resolution comparisons needed equality semantics to store 'Unresolved' sentinel and compare results.
insights.solution: Implement equals and hashCode using resolvedUrl and repositoryId.
insights.lesson_learned: Value objects used in caches should implement equality to allow sentinel values and map key comparisons.

insights.title: Encapsulate resolved URL and repository id in RepositoryInformation
insights.problem: Resolved field and repository id were separate return points; using separate primitives caused scattered handling.
insights.root_cause: Need to return both resolved URL and repository id together from checksum calculators.
insights.solution: Add RepositoryInformation class holding ResolvedUrl and RepositoryId and Unresolved sentinel.
insights.lesson_learned: When multiple related pieces of data are returned together, prefer a small value object to avoid repetitive Optionals and to make intent explicit.

maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.short_description: Value object representing a Maven artifact's packaging type, treating 'jar' as the implicit default (null).
maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.description:
This file defines ArtifactType, a small value object used to model non-default Maven packaging types (for example pom, war). The class exists to keep the generated lockfile compact: the factory method ArtifactType.of(String) returns null for null/empty input and for the canonical default packaging "jar". This design encodes the convention that the default packaging is omitted rather than explicitly serialized, which affects how lockfile JSON is produced and consumed elsewhere in the plugin.

ArtifactType is comparable and implements equals/hashCode so that it can participate in collections, graphs and deterministic sorting when producing the lockfile. The constructor enforces non-null for stored values (Objects.requireNonNull), while the factory returns null to represent the default. The class is small and intentionally immutable (single final String field) and primarily exists to centralize the default-handling semantics and provide a stable lexical ordering for non-default types.

When editing code that interacts with artifact types or serializes lockfile data, respect that callers expect ArtifactType.of(...) to possibly return null (signifying default jar) rather than a non-null ArtifactType representing 'jar'. The compareTo implementation assumes the instance value is non-null (constructor invariant), so null should never be passed into ArtifactType instances. This file is part of the lockfile data model and therefore participates in the plugin's public serialization surface (lockfile JSON) and any code that relies on compact omission of defaults.

key_constructs.name: ArtifactType
key_constructs.purpose: Immutable value object representing a non-default Maven packaging type.
key_constructs.reasoning: The class centralizes handling of packaging default semantics (jar -> omitted/null), provides equality/hash and ordering needed by graph/traversal/serialization code, and ensures stored value is non-null while permitting a null factory result to encode the default.

key_constructs.name: of
key_constructs.purpose: Factory method that converts a string packaging into an ArtifactType or returns null for default/empty inputs.
key_constructs.reasoning: Callers must use this factory to ensure the canonical behavior of treating empty or 'jar' packaging as omitted (null). Changing its behavior will change lockfile serialization (presence/absence of type fields).

key_constructs.name: getValue
key_constructs.purpose: Accessor that returns the non-null underlying packaging string.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by serializers and any logic that needs the raw packaging string; it assumes the instance is non-null and the stored value is non-null because construction enforces it.

key_constructs.name: compareTo
key_constructs.purpose: Defines a lexical ordering between ArtifactType instances based on their underlying string values.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used for deterministic ordering when ArtifactType values appear in sets or when sorting is needed for reproducible lockfile generation.

key_behaviors: represents non-default Maven packaging types
returns null for default/empty packaging strings (jar)
provides deterministic ordering and equality for packaging values

pitfalls.mistake: Treating ArtifactType.of(...) as always returning a non-null instance.
pitfalls.consequence: Code will NPE when callers assume non-null; the lockfile convention (omitting default 'jar') will be violated if callers wrap nulls incorrectly.
pitfalls.prevention: Always handle the possibility of a null return from ArtifactType.of and only call new ArtifactType(...) via the class internals which guarantee non-null.

pitfalls.mistake: Creating ArtifactType instances with a null value (bypassing the factory).
pitfalls.consequence: Constructor uses Objects.requireNonNull and will throw a NullPointerException; invariants about compareTo and getValue being non-null will be broken if circumvented.
pitfalls.prevention: Use the provided factory method or respect constructor contract; do not allow null values to be passed into the constructor.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing equality/hashCode or compareTo semantics without coordinating serialization consumers.
pitfalls.consequence: Sorting/deduplication and JSON determinism could change, making lockfile diffs and comparisons unstable across versions.
pitfalls.prevention: If compare/equality semantics must change, update any serialization/graph code and consider migration or versioning of the lockfile format.

key_sections: of: shows how default ('jar' or empty) is represented as null and is the primary contract to callers
constructor: enforces non-null invariant for stored value (Objects.requireNonNull)
compareTo: lexical ordering used for deterministic operations
skip_unless_needed: toString, equals, hashCode: standard boilerplate; review only if changing equality or serialization

related_files.relationship: ArtifactType values (or null) are serialized into the lockfile model; LockFile is the root model that will include artifact entries that may reference ArtifactType
related_files.reason_to_check: If default-omission semantics or serialization format changes, update LockFile serialization and any consumers that read/wrote the lockfile.

related_files.relationship: Dependency nodes represent artifacts that may carry a packaging/type; ordering/equality used by graphs may rely on ArtifactType.compareTo/equals for deterministic outputs
related_files.reason_to_check: Changing ordering or equality of ArtifactType may affect graph traversal, deterministic output, and lockfile diffs.

insights.title: Add ArtifactType value object and treat 'jar' as default (null)
insights.problem: Lockfile previously lacked explicit 'type' field for non-default artifact types (pom/war etc.).
insights.root_cause: Omitting type made it impossible to distinguish artifacts whose packaging is not 'jar'.
insights.solution: Add ArtifactType class that returns null for jar/empty to avoid redundant JSON; implements Comparable/equals/hashCode.
insights.lesson_learned: Model defaults (jar) as null to keep JSON compact; tests should assert non-jar types are present when needed.

maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.short_description: Immutable value object representing a Maven repository identifier (with a None sentinel).
maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.description:
RepositoryId is a small value-type used throughout the lockfile data model to represent the repository id string declared in POMs or used when resolving artifacts. The class centralizes equality, ordering, and a canonical string wrapper for repository identifiers so callers don't pass raw strings around. It exists to provide a type-safe representation that can be serialized into the lockfile JSON and compared deterministically when validating or generating lockfiles.

Design-wise, RepositoryId is immutable (final field) and implements Comparable so it can be sorted consistently in graphs and sets. It exposes factory helpers: of(String) which enforces non-null inputs, and None() which returns a sentinel empty identifier. The class provides standard Object contract implementations (equals, hashCode, toString) so it behaves predictably when used as graph nodes keys, map keys, or set members. The presence of a None sentinel and the class javadoc indicate an intention to allow explicit empty repository ids to represent missing repository declarations while still being a RepositoryId instance.

This file is small but important because it is part of the lockfile data model: other model classes (Pom, MavenPlugin, LockFile) will reference or embed RepositoryId values when recording where artifacts/poms/plugins were resolved from. When editing or extending serialization, ordering, or equality semantics of lockfile entries, changes here must preserve deterministic compareTo/equals/hashCode behavior so generated lockfiles remain stable across runs and test assertions.

key_constructs.name: RepositoryId
key_constructs.purpose: Immutable value object wrapping a repository id string and implementing Comparable.
key_constructs.reasoning: Centralizes representation, equality, ordering and toString for repository identifiers used in lockfile models; changes here affect serialization, sorting, and comparisons across the data model.

key_constructs.reasoning: Enforces non-null input at construction time for normal RepositoryId instances and documents the intended factory usage.

key_constructs.name: None
key_constructs.purpose: Static factory returning a sentinel RepositoryId representing absence (empty string).
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides a canonical empty RepositoryId for use where a repository id is not present, enabling callers to avoid nulls and have a consistent representation.

callers.context: RepositoryId repoIdCmp = this.repositoryId == null ? RepositoryId.None() : this.repositoryId;

key_constructs.purpose: Accessor that returns the wrapped repository id string.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by serializers and callers that need the raw string value; its return value is relied upon when composing lockfile entries or debugging output.

key_constructs.name: compareTo
key_constructs.purpose: Implements Comparable to give a deterministic string ordering for RepositoryId instances.
key_constructs.reasoning: Sorting and deterministic graph traversal rely on this lexical ordering; changing compareTo would affect ordering in generated lockfiles and any tests asserting ordering.

key_behaviors: wraps a non-null repository id string into a typed object
provides a canonical None sentinel (empty string) for missing repository ids
implements equality, hashCode and Comparable for deterministic ordering

pitfalls.mistake: Assume of(String) enforces non-empty strings.
pitfalls.consequence: Empty strings can still be created (e.g., RepositoryId.None() or via constructor), which contradicts the class javadoc's claim that values are 'not null or empty' and may lead to unexpected empty repository ids in lockfiles.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect that None() is the intended sentinel; do not substitute raw empty strings where a non-empty repository id is expected and be aware serialization consumers may see empty values.

pitfalls.mistake: Modify compareTo/equals/hashCode semantics without coordinating other model classes and tests.
pitfalls.consequence: Ordering and equality used in sets, maps and lockfile serialization may change, causing non-deterministic diffs or failing tests that assert structure/order.
pitfalls.prevention: Any change to comparison or equality must be validated across lockfile generation, serialization, and consumer tests to preserve deterministic output.

pitfalls.mistake: Treat repository id instances as nullable in calling code.
pitfalls.consequence: The class is intended to avoid nulls (factories use requireNonNull); introducing nulls undermines the value-object contract and may cause NPEs where callers assume non-null instances.
pitfalls.prevention: Use RepositoryId.None() to represent absence and avoid passing null repository ids into APIs that expect a RepositoryId.

key_sections: RepositoryId: class-level behavior (immutability, equals/hashCode, compareTo) — critical for deterministic lockfile generation
of: factory method enforcing non-null inputs — typical construction path
None: sentinel construction — used where repository is absent

related_files.reason_to_check: When altering representation/serialization or equality of RepositoryId, check Pom and lockfile serialization code to maintain consistency and deterministic output.

related_files.relationship: Root lockfile model that contains repository-related fields; RepositoryId values will be embedded in lockfile JSON
related_files.reason_to_check: LockFile JSON output and consumers may depend on RepositoryId string shapes and ordering; verify generated artifacts and tests after edits.

related_files.relationship: MavenPlugin model includes resolution metadata similar to artifacts (resolved repository info) which may use RepositoryId
related_files.reason_to_check: Ensure plugin-resolution metadata remains consistent if RepositoryId serialization/semantics change.

insights.title: Strongly-typed RepositoryId introduced
insights.problem: Repository identifier used as raw string in various places; need explicit type to serialize and manage repository metadata.
insights.root_cause: Adding repository id to lockfile required consistent type with validation.
insights.solution: Added RepositoryId class with None() sentinel, comparable, equals/hashCode and toString.
insights.lesson_learned: Use small value types for semantic concepts (RepositoryId) to prevent accidental misuse of plain strings and to enable custom JSON adapters.

maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.short_description: Typed value object representing the resolved URL where an artifact was downloaded from.
maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.description:
ResolvedUrl is a small immutable value type used to represent the resolved location (URL or repository indicator) for an artifact, parent POM, or plugin in the generated lockfile. It exists to replace raw strings for the lockfile "resolved" fields, providing a typed wrapper that centralizes equality, ordering, and a sentinel for an unresolved value. The class offers factory methods for construction (of and Unresolved) and implements Comparable to allow stable ordering of resolved values when building or serializing lockfile data structures.

The type is deliberately minimal: it holds a single non-null String value, enforces non-null at construction (via Objects.requireNonNull), supplies an explicit Unresolved sentinel (an empty string), and delegates equality, hashing, and lexicographic ordering to the underlying String. It is used by higher-level lockfile data classes (e.g., LockFile, Pom, MavenPlugin) and participates in stable comparisons and serialization operations. The commit history indicates this type was added to avoid raw string handling and to provide a consistent default/Unresolved representation across the lockfile generation and validation code paths.

key_constructs.name: ResolvedUrl
key_constructs.purpose: Immutable wrapper type for the resolved URL string used in lockfile fields.
key_constructs.reasoning: Treats resolved locations as a first-class typed value so equality, ordering, and an explicit unresolved sentinel are centralized and consistent across lockfile data models.

key_constructs.reasoning: Provides a canonical representation for 'not resolved' states used in lockfile generation, avoiding scattered empty-string literals.

key_constructs.reasoning: Allows construction from raw string (ensures non-null). Note: public constructor exists alongside factories; callers in the codebase may use either.

key_constructs.name: compareTo

key_constructs.reasoning: Enables deterministic ordering when ResolvedUrl instances are sorted (important for stable lockfile outputs).

key_constructs.reasoning: Supports set/map membership and equality checks used by graph/serialization code.

key_constructs.reasoning: Matches equals to allow correct behavior in hashed collections.
