key_behaviors: represents resolved location strings for artifacts and POMs
provides a canonical Unresolved sentinel
enforces non-null values at construction
compares instances lexicographically for stable ordering

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming the class prevents empty strings except for the Unresolved sentinel.
pitfalls.consequence: Code may accidentally create ResolvedUrl.of("") which is permitted by the of method (only null is checked), causing ambiguity between deliberate empty values and the Unresolved sentinel.
pitfalls.prevention: When creating instances, respect the intended use: use Unresolved() for unresolved state and use of(...) only with known non-empty resolved locations.

pitfalls.mistake: Passing null to the public constructor or factory.
pitfalls.consequence: Objects.requireNonNull will throw a NullPointerException at runtime.
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure upstream resolution code never passes null; use Optional or explicit checks if resolution may be absent.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing compareTo/equals implementations to be inconsistent with String-based behavior.
pitfalls.consequence: Would break deterministic ordering and collection semantics used by lockfile generation and validation (potentially causing non-deterministic JSON outputs).
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain the current string-based semantics for equality, hashing, and comparison.

key_sections: of: factory enforcing non-null resolved strings
Unresolved: canonical empty-string sentinel for unresolved state
compareTo: lexicographic ordering policy (used for deterministic outputs)
equals/hashCode: equality semantics used by data structures
skip_unless_needed: toString: simple debug representation (low importance)
public constructor: only check if construction pattern in callers differs from factory usage

related_files.relationship: LockFile is the root model that contains resolved fields (artifacts/poms/plugins) which use ResolvedUrl values; changes to ResolvedUrl affect serialization and equality behavior in LockFile structures.
related_files.reason_to_check: Serializing/deserializing lockfile JSON and equality checks will be impacted if ResolvedUrl representation changes.

related_files.relationship: Pom data objects record parent/relative resolved information likely using ResolvedUrl; ensure the Unresolved sentinel aligns with Pom's expectations.
related_files.reason_to_check: Pom logic may treat an empty resolved value specially (e.g., external parent URLs); verify behavior if changing ResolvedUrl semantics.

related_files.relationship: Plugin entries in the lockfile include resolved repository and resolved fields that can be represented by ResolvedUrl.
related_files.reason_to_check: Plugin checksum/resolution logic relies on resolved location representation; modifications to ResolvedUrl could require updates to plugin resolution code.

insights.title: ResolvedUrl value type to represent artifact resolved location
insights.problem: Resolved URLs were raw strings in the lockfile, which risks null/empty differences and parsing errors.
insights.root_cause: Need typed representation for resolved field, with Unresolved sentinel and consistent JSON serialization.
insights.solution: Added ResolvedUrl class with of/Unresolved, equals/hashCode/compareTo.
insights.lesson_learned: Wrap commonly serialized primitives (like URLs) in typed classes to control serialization and default behavior.

maven_plugin/src/test/java/it/Integratio.short_description: Integration test suite that runs Maven projects and asserts produced lockfile/pom outcomes
maven_plugin/src/test/java/it/Integratio.description:
This file contains a comprehensive set of integration tests (JUnit 5 with the com.soebes Maven IT extension) that exercise the maven-lockfile plugin end-to-end against small fixture projects. The tests invoke real Maven builds (via the MavenJupiterExtension) and then inspect files written into the project's target/base directory (for example lockfile.json, pom.lockfile.xml, pom.xml). The assertions validate: generated lockfile structure and content (dependencies, checksums, resolved URL/repositoryId, config flags like includeEnvironment, checksumAlgorithm), plugin inclusion and plugin-dependency resolution, freeze mojo output behavior, classifier and artifact-type handling, ordering determinism, reduced-mode filtering, and expected failure/warning behavior when lockfile validation detects differences.

These tests exist to detect regressions in the lockfile generation, checksum calculation/resolution, BOM & parent-POM discovery, plugin dependency resolution, and freeze mojo behavior. They are intentionally end-to-end: they read the serialized LockFile model (LockFile.readLockFile) and then inspect typed fields (ArtifactId, GroupId, VersionNumber, Classifier, MavenScope, ResolvedUrl, RepositoryId). The test suite assumes deterministic ordering and exact checksum values produced by the checksum implementations (FileSystemChecksumCalculator/RemoteChecksumCalculator) and the shape of produced POM files. Many tests rely on resources under src/test/resources-its/it/IntegrationTestsIT which define the fixture Maven projects and expected outputs. Historical commit messages show tests were updated frequently when model collection types changed (List -> Set), checksum formatting changed, and when features (BOMs, parent POMs, classifier support, reduced mode) were added; tests mirror those features and are fragile to related changes.

key_constructs.name: IntegrationTestsIT
key_constructs.purpose: Container for integration test cases that run Maven builds and validate generated lockfiles and frozen poms.
key_constructs.reasoning: This class is the test-suite entrypoint used by the Maven IT junit extension; edits affecting test discovery, lifecycle annotations, or method signatures can change which tests run and how Maven executions are configured.

key_constructs.name: simpleProject
key_constructs.purpose: Test that an empty project generates an empty lockfile (no dependencies).
key_constructs.reasoning: Validates basic generation path and LockFile.readLockFile integration; used as minimal correctness check for lockfile generation.

key_constructs.name: singleDependency
key_constructs.purpose: Test that a project with a single dependency records that dependency with exact groupId/artifactId/version and checksum.
key_constructs.reasoning: Asserts exact checksum strings and version values; sensitive to checksum algorithm/formatting and resolution behavior.

key_constructs.name: pluginProject
key_constructs.purpose: Test that includeMavenPlugins=true records maven plugins in the lockfile, computes checksums, and records plugin dependencies.
key_constructs.reasoning: Exercises LockFileFacade.getAllPlugins, ProjectBuilder, and checksum-calculators; changes to plugin resolution behavior or checksum calculation affect this test.

key_constructs.name: freezeJunit
key_constructs.purpose: Test that the freeze mojo produces a pom.lockfile.xml (the frozen POM) with specific dependency versions.
key_constructs.reasoning: Touches FreezeDependencyMojo behavior (updateDependencies, convertSoftToExactVersionString) and requires the lockfile to be present and contain specific dependency versions.

key_constructs.name: checkFreeze
key_constructs.purpose: Helper used by freezeWithDepManagement and freezeWithoutDepManagement to compare actual vs expected frozen POM contents and assert the original pom.xml is unchanged.
key_constructs.reasoning: Compares POM models using MavenXpp3Reader and performs set-based equality checks of dependencies and dependencyManagement; sensitive to formatting, ordering, and dependencyKey composition.

key_constructs.name: classifier
key_constructs.purpose: Helper that asserts classifier-handling: tests that sources/javadoc/classic artifact classifiers are recorded as separate nodes with correct checksums.
key_constructs.reasoning: Validates classifier support in DependencyGraph/DependencyNode and checksum mapping for classifier-qualified artifacts.

key_constructs.name: flattenDependencies
key_constructs.purpose: Recursively flattens a dependency tree (DependencyNode) to a list for assertions across the whole sub-tree.
key_constructs.reasoning: Supports reduced-mode tests (reduceLog4jAffected / reduceLog4jNotAffected) and detects presence/absence of transitive dependencies; relies on DependencyNode.getChildren traversal and is susceptible to cycles if traversal logic changed.

key_constructs.name: findFile
key_constructs.purpose: Searches the Maven execution result's target base directory for a file whose name contains a given substring, returning the first match.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by many tests to locate produced files in the test project's build tree; changes to file naming, target directories, or test resource layout require updates here.

key_constructs.name: fileExists
key_constructs.purpose: Boolean variant of findFile to check presence of a file containing a name substring.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by tests that confirm files are absent or present (skipLockfile, differentLockfileName).

key_constructs.name: readPom
key_constructs.purpose: Reads a pom.xml into a Maven Model using MavenXpp3Reader.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used in freeze checks to perform structural comparisons; exceptions thrown here are surfaced as test failures.

key_constructs.name: getDependencyKeys
key_constructs.purpose: Converts a list of Maven Model Dependencies to string keys group:artifact:scope:version for comparison.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to compare expected/actual dependency lists irrespective of ordering of entries in the POM; relies on dependency.getScope presence and exact string composition.

key_behaviors: runs Maven builds for fixture projects and inspects produced outputs
validates dependency entries (groupId, artifactId, version, classifier, checksum)
verifies plugin inclusion and plugin dependency resolution
checks freeze mojo produces expected frozen POM and leaves original POM unchanged
asserts ordering and reduced-mode behavior of dependency lists

pitfalls.mistake: Change checksum algorithm name, formatting, or encoding in source code without updating expected checksum literals in tests.
pitfalls.consequence: Many assertions that compare exact checksum strings (sha hex) will fail, leading to widespread broken integration tests.
pitfalls.prevention: When editing checksum-related code, update the tests' expected checksum strings or central test fixtures; be mindful of leading zeros, lowercase hex, and algorithm selection (SHA-256 vs SHA-1).

pitfalls.mistake: Modify serialization order of sets/lists or change collection types (List <-> Set) used in produced LockFile JSON.
pitfalls.consequence: Tests that rely on deterministic ordering (they convert sets to arrays and assert an element at index 0 or assert ordering via comparator strings) will break.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve deterministic ordering (TreeSet + comparator) in production code or update tests/fixtures to reflect new ordering.

pitfalls.mistake: Rename or relocate test fixtures under src/test/resources-its/it/IntegrationTestsIT or change expected file names (lockfile.json, pom.lockfile.xml).
pitfalls.consequence: findFile and fileExists helper methods will not find outputs; tests will fail with FileNotFoundException or false negatives.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep fixture project structure and produced filenames stable, or update findFile usages and expected names consistently across tests.

pitfalls.mistake: Change plugin dependency resolution behavior (ProjectBuilder / LockFileFacade.getAllPlugins) such that plugin dependencies become empty or change scope.
pitfalls.consequence: pluginProject and pluginUserDependency tests assert non-empty plugin dependency sets and specific scope overrides; these tests will fail.
pitfalls.prevention: If making changes to plugin resolution logic, run these integration tests and check the plugin-related assertions and freeze behavior.

pitfalls.mistake: Change reduced-mode selection algorithm (which determines included/selected versions) without updating test fixtures or expectations.
pitfalls.consequence: reduceLog4jAffected and reduceLog4jNotAffected will flip or fail, since they assert presence/absence of specific transitive artifact versions.
pitfalls.prevention: Run reduced-mode integration tests and inspect flattenDependencies-based assertions after any change to resolution or Conflict winner computation.

key_sections: simpleProject: minimal generation correctness
singleDependency & singleDependencyCheck* tests: concrete checksum/version expectations
pluginProject / pluginUserDependency: plugin resolution & dependency scope assertions
freezeJunit, freezeWithDepManagement, freezeWithoutDepManagement: freeze mojo output and comparison helpers
reduceLog4jAffected / reduceLog4jNotAffected: reduced-mode behavior and flattenDependencies helper
skip_unless_needed: verbose repetitive assertions across many similar test cases (scan for patterns rather than reading every assertion)
console System.out.println lines (only useful for observing test start in logs)

relevant_snippets.description: Top-level test methods including simpleProject and assertions reading lockfile.json
Plugin-related tests and freeze helpers (checkFreeze, readPom)
Classifier and reduced-mode tests plus helper flattenDependencies/findFile

related_files.reason_to_check: If you change how LockFile is assembled (roots, plugin inclusion, boms), update tests/fixtures and expected assertions here.

related_files.relationship: Provides checksum calculation used in default test runs (local checksums); tests assert exact checksum strings produced by this class when running locally.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changing checksum algorithm, hex encoding, or prewarming behavior will break many string-equality checks in tests.

related_files.relationship: Alternative checksum mode that tests also exercise in specific fixtures; tests include behavior for remote checksum lookup and fallback verification.
related_files.reason_to_check: Modifications that change remote lookup semantics or supported algorithms will require updates to tests that expect remote-mode behavior.

related_files.relationship: Converts Maven's dependency graph into DependencyNode trees that tests read; tests validate included/selected versions, classifier and artifact-type propagation from this conversion.
related_files.reason_to_check: If you change node inclusion rules, checksum assignment, or ordering, update tests that assert presence/absence of transitive nodes and ordering invariants.

related_files.relationship: Freeze mojo produces pom.lockfile.xml which the freeze tests inspect; tests validate how dependencies and dependencyManagement are written.
related_files.reason_to_check: Edits to freezing logic (version wrapping, exactVersionStrings behavior, plugin dependency injection) will affect freeze-related tests.

related_files.reason_to_check: Changing equality, compareTo, or serialization of MavenPlugin may impact tests that rely on presence/non-empty plugin dependencies and determinism.

related_files.reason_to_check: Adjusting BOM discovery or parent POM traversal may change expected Pom contents and require test updates.

insights.title: Integration tests expanded to cover BOMs, parent POMs, freeze plugin deps and logging
insights.problem: New features (BOMs, parent POMs, freeze plugin deps, artifact types) need integration coverage to prevent regressions.
insights.root_cause: Feature additions changed lockfile shape and logic requiring end-to-end validation.
insights.solution: Add multiple integration tests (bomPom, bomPomWithParent, freezePluginDependencies, externalParentPom, relativeParentPom, artifactTypeProject) and add logging printouts at the start of each test run for observability.
insights.lesson_learned: When modifying serialization schema or resolution behavior, add or update integration tests that read produced lockfile.json and assert presence/structure of expected fields (boms, parent chains, resolved URLs, repositoryId, plugin dependencies).

insights.title: Integration tests extended to cover resolved fields, remote checksum modes, plugin dependencies, pom & environment validation
insights.problem: New features (resolved, repositoryId, remote checksums, plugin dependencies, pom checksum, environment validation) require integration tests to assert behavior and prevent regressions.
insights.root_cause: Feature additions across many components need end-to-end tests to verify JSON lockfile output and mojo behavior.
insights.solution: Added/updated multiple integration tests: resolvedFieldShouldResolve, checksumModeRemote, pluginProject, pomCheckShouldFail, environmentalCheckShouldFail and assertions checking resolved URLs, repository ids, plugin dependency sets, and checksums.
insights.lesson_learned: When changing serialized format or adding resolution/verification logic, add integration tests that assert lockfile contents (resolved, checksumAlgorithm, checksum, repositoryId, plugin dependencies) to detect regressions quickly.

insights.title: Integration tests updated to work with Set representations and new Model changes
insights.problem: Tests referenced List.get(0) and string-based assertions for checksum formatting; multiple refactors required updating tests.
insights.root_cause: Model refactors (Lists -> Sets), checksum algorithm/format changes (leading zeros preserved, SHA-256 default), and classifier support changed expected lockfile structure and fields.
insights.solution: Replace List.get(0) with toArray(...)[0] access for Set, update assertions to use typed extractors (ArtifactId::getValue, VersionNumber::getValue), add new classifier-related tests, update expected checksum strings, and adjust how lockfile/pom files are located and compared.
insights.lesson_learned: When refactoring model collection types and serialization formats, update integration tests to assert new deterministic ordering and exact field formats; prefer stable access patterns (convert to array) over positional assumptions.

insights.title: Integration tests added for reduced lockfile behavior
insights.problem: Need assurance that reduced mode correctly filters out non-selected dependency versions and includes expected winners.
insights.root_cause: New 'reduced' feature filters nodes based on selected versions; this must be verified with real dependency graphs.
insights.solution: Add two Maven integration tests reduceLog4jAffected and reduceLog4jNotAffected verifying that, with reduced=true, a particular transitive artifact version is either present or absent depending on resolution winner. Also add flatten helper to traverse DependencyNode trees in tests.
insights.lesson_learned: Feature changes to dependency selection semantics require integration tests that use real Maven resolution scenarios to catch subtle regression; tests exercise LockFile reading and DependencyGraph->DependencyNode output.

insights.title: Add integration test for freeze and plugin inclusion
insights.problem: No integration test covered the new freeze mojo or plugin detection.
insights.root_cause: New features were added without corresponding IT coverage initially.
insights.solution: Added MavenTest methods (freezeJunit, pluginProject) validating pom modifications and that mavenPlugins are present in generated lockfiles.
insights.lesson_learned: When adding Mojos or features that mutate poms or lockfile contents, add ITs exercising the plugin lifecycle and verify output files (pom, lockfile) contents. Use resource-based test projects.

maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.short_description: Static holder providing Maven's Log to non-Mojo classes with a SystemStreamLog fallback
maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.description:
This small utility centralizes logging for the Maven plugin code by exposing a static setter/getter for org.apache.maven.plugin.logging.Log. It exists so non-Mojo classes (library/utility classes invoked by Mojos) can obtain and use the Maven-provided Log instance. Mojos (plugin goals) are expected to call PluginLogManager.setLog(getLog()) at startup so that downstream code logs through Maven's logging infrastructure rather than a separate logging framework. If no Log has been set, the manager lazily instantiates a SystemStreamLog as a sensible default for CLI or test usage.

The design is intentionally minimal: a private static Log field, a public static setLog(Log) to inject the Mojo's Log, and a public static getLog() that returns the injected Log or a lazily-created SystemStreamLog if none was injected. This makes the class a centralized bridge between Mojo lifecycle logging and other classes, enabling consistent output captured by Maven. The implementation is not synchronized; it uses simple lazy initialization for the fallback and permits replacement by subsequent setLog calls.

This file is part of a refactor (commit insight ca99ed8) to remove direct usage of other logging frameworks (e.g., log4j) inside plugin code and ensure all plugin output flows through Maven's Log API. It therefore has cross-cutting importance: many code paths in the plugin expect PluginLogManager.getLog() to return a valid Log instance and will call its methods directly.

key_constructs.name: PluginLogManager
key_constructs.purpose: Container class that centralizes access to a Maven Log instance for plugin code
key_constructs.reasoning: Editing agents must treat this as the single access point for logging in non-Mojo classes; changes affect how all plugin internals obtain their Log and how logs are emitted during Maven execution and tests.

callers.context: PluginLogManager.getLog().warn(String.format("Could not build project for plugin %s", pluginArtifact
PluginLogManager.getLog().warn(String.format("Could not calculate checksum for artifact %s", artifac

key_constructs.name: setLog
key_constructs.purpose: Sets/injects the static Log instance to be returned to callers of getLog
key_constructs.reasoning: Mojo implementations are expected to call this early in execution; changes to its signature or semantics will break the contract between Mojos and consumers.

key_constructs.name: getLog
key_constructs.purpose: Returns the currently set Log, or lazily creates and returns a SystemStreamLog if none has been set
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by library classes that don't have direct access to Mojo.getLog(); its fallback behavior must be preserved to avoid NPEs in contexts where setLog was not invoked.

callers.context: PluginLogManager.getLog().warn(String.format("Could not build project for plugin %s", pluginArtifact
PluginLogManager.getLog().warn(String.format("Could not calculate checksum for artifact %s", artifac

key_behaviors: provides a Maven Log instance to non-Mojo classes
accepts injection of Mojo-supplied Log via setLog
falls back to SystemStreamLog when no Log was injected

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or altering the lazy fallback to SystemStreamLog
pitfalls.consequence: Code paths that rely on getLog() returning a non-null Log may throw NullPointerException or lose logging during tests/CLI runs.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve fallback behavior; if changing, ensure callers still always receive a usable Log instance.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming thread-safety and adding complex concurrency around the static field without coordination
pitfalls.consequence: Race conditions or initialization ordering bugs when multiple threads call getLog/setLog (e.g., during parallel test suites) could lead to inconsistent logs or lost injection.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect that the current implementation is a simple static holder; if concurrency changes are required, audit all call sites (Mojos/tests) for expectations.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing the public setter or changing its signature/visibility
pitfalls.consequence: Mojos will no longer be able to inject their Log instances, breaking integration with Maven's logging.
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain the public setLog(Log) contract so Mojos can continue to call PluginLogManager.setLog(getLog()).

pitfalls.mistake: Treating PluginLogManager as a replacement for Maven's getLog in Mojos
pitfalls.consequence: Mojos must still call getLog() themselves; failing to inject the Mojo log will change runtime logging semantics.
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure Mojos continue to explicitly call setLog(getLog()) during initialization.

key_sections: getLog: fallback logic to SystemStreamLog — critical to avoid NPEs
setLog: injection entrypoint used by Mojos
skip_unless_needed: class boilerplate and private constructor

related_files.relationship: Mojos are expected to call PluginLogManager.setLog(getLog()) during execution; they supply the authoritative Log instance.
related_files.reason_to_check: If changing the manager's API or behavior, update Mojos to preserve injection and logging semantics.

related_files.relationship: Another Mojo consumer that should inject its Maven Log into this manager.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes impacting default/fallback behavior or API will affect plugin validation logging.

related_files.relationship: Package-level utilities and classes that call PluginLogManager.getLog() to emit messages.
related_files.reason_to_check: Internal classes depend on the manager returning a usable Log; adjust them if manager semantics change.

tests_to_run: Integration: execute plugin goals in a sample project (e.g., mvn io.github.chains-project:maven-lockfile:generate) and observe logs

insights.title: Centralize logging to Maven's Log for plugin code
insights.problem: Code used org.apache.logging.log4j (or log4j) loggers in plugin classes, causing inconsistent logging when running inside Maven (Maven provides its own Log tied to the Mojo).
insights.root_cause: Logging used library loggers rather than the Maven-provided Log; Mojos have getLog() that should be used to integrate with Maven's lifecycle logging.
insights.solution: Introduce PluginLogManager with static setLog/getLog. Mojos call PluginLogManager.setLog(getLog()) at start; other classes use PluginLogManager.getLog() for logging instead of direct log4j usage.
insights.lesson_learned: Maven plugins should use the Maven Log API for output. Centralizing access via a small manager eases migration and ensures consistent logging context (and avoids runtime mismatch between CLI/IDE/CI).

maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.short_description: Helper that locates a POM for a given GAV and builds a MavenProject from it (local or remote).
maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.description:
ProjectBuilder centralizes the logic for producing a MavenProject instance from a groupId:artifactId:version triple. It exists so callers (notably plugin resolution logic in LockFileFacade and plugin checksum collection) do not need to replicate low-level resolution and Maven container lookups. The class first attempts a fast local repository lookup for a matching POM file and falls back to resolving the artifact via Maven's ArtifactResolver (using container lookups for deprecated components). If a POM file is obtained from either path, it delegates to Maven's ProjectBuilder to construct a MavenProject with dependency resolution enabled but plugin processing disabled.

The design intentionally returns Optional<MavenProject> and logs warnings rather than throwing exceptions: callers get a best-effort result and can decide how to proceed if the project cannot be built. The implementation tolerates the current Maven API by using deprecated container lookups (ArtifactFactory, ArtifactResolver, ProjectBuilder) and suppresses deprecation warnings in a small, well-contained area. The builder also populates a ProjectBuildingRequest copied from the current session's request and injects the provided remote repositories list into resolution calls. This preserves repository resolution semantics consistent with the active Maven session.

key_constructs.name: ProjectBuilder
key_constructs.purpose: Encapsulates logic to find a POM (local repo or remote) and build a MavenProject from a GAV.
key_constructs.reasoning: Centralizes deprecated container lookups and artifact/POM resolution behind a single helper that returns Optional<MavenProject>, simplifying callers and standardizing warning behavior.

key_constructs.name: buildFromGav
key_constructs.purpose: Primary entry point: given groupId, artifactId, version, returns Optional<MavenProject> after local lookup or remote resolution.
key_constructs.reasoning: Callers should use this method to obtain a MavenProject for plugin or external parent resolution; it performs both local and remote strategies and preserves the non-throwing, logged behavior.

key_constructs.name: lookForPomFileInLocalRepository
key_constructs.purpose: Attempts to construct a file path to the artifact POM inside the local repository and checks whether that POM exists.
key_constructs.reasoning: This function is a fast-path that avoids remote network calls when the local repository already contains the POM; its path construction must match Maven's local repo layout (groupId -> path by replacing '.' with '/').

key_constructs.name: resolvePomFile
key_constructs.purpose: Uses ArtifactFactory and ArtifactResolver (via session container lookups) to resolve the POM artifact and return the underlying file if available.
key_constructs.reasoning: This fallback performs network resolution using the provided repositories list and wraps container lookups and resolver exceptions into Optional and logged warnings to avoid failing the whole build process.

key_constructs.name: buildProjectFromPomFile
key_constructs.purpose: Given a local File pointing to a POM, uses Maven's ProjectBuilder to build a MavenProject with dependencies resolved but plugins not processed.
key_constructs.reasoning: Keeping processPlugins=false avoids running plugin lifecycles while building the project; resolveDependencies=true ensures transitives are available for dependency-introspection consumers.

key_behaviors: tries local repository lookup for a POM before remote resolution
resolves a POM artifact via ArtifactResolver when not found locally
builds a MavenProject from a POM file with dependencies resolved and plugins disabled
returns Optional<MavenProject> and logs warnings instead of throwing on failures

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or changing the Optional-returning behavior to throw exceptions or propagate errors directly
pitfalls.consequence: Callers that expect a best-effort Optional may cause hard build failures where previous behavior only logged warnings and continued.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the Optional return convention and logging semantics when editing public behavior.

pitfalls.mistake: Replacing or removing the deprecated container lookup usage (session.getContainer().lookup(...)) without providing equivalent behavior compatible with the Maven runtime in use
pitfalls.consequence: Resolution logic may break at runtime if the replacement does not behave the same across supported Maven versions, or if a new API is used incorrectly.
pitfalls.prevention: If changing the lookup mechanism, ensure compatibility across target Maven versions and keep exception handling for ComponentLookupException.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering buildingRequest flags (e.g., setting processPlugins=true or resolveDependencies=false)
pitfalls.consequence: Enabling plugin processing could cause side effects (plugin execution) during project building; disabling dependency resolution will prevent callers from getting dependency info required by plugin dependency collection.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep processPlugins=false and resolveDependencies=true unless caller semantics are explicitly adapted and thoroughly tested.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying local repository path computation (groupId.replace(".", "/") and filename scheme)
pitfalls.consequence: Incorrect path computation will fail to find local POM files even when present, causing unnecessary network resolution and potential failures.
pitfalls.prevention: Retain Maven's standard groupId -> path mapping and artifactId-version.pom filename convention.

key_sections: buildFromGav: main entry point used by callers to obtain a MavenProject for a GAV
lookForPomFileInLocalRepository: fast-path lookup matching Maven local repo layout
resolvePomFile: remote resolution using ArtifactFactory and ArtifactResolver (container lookups)
buildProjectFromPomFile: builds MavenProject with processPlugins=false and resolveDependencies=true
skip_unless_needed: constructor and member field initializations (straightforward)
log messages formatting

mvn -DskipTests=false test
(or run the integration test profile as configured by the project; the repository uses com.soebes integration test harness so run mvn verify or the project-specific integration test invocation)

related_files.relationship: ProjectBuilder is used by LockFileFacade when resolving plugin projects and external parents; changes here directly affect plugin dependency collection and POM chain construction.
related_files.reason_to_check: If ProjectBuilder behavior or return semantics change, LockFileFacade must adapt to avoid failing lockfile generation or plugin resolution logic.

related_files.relationship: ProjectBuilder uses PluginLogManager.getLog() for logging; consistency of logging and test assertions about warnings may depend on this logger.
related_files.reason_to_check: Log behavior and message content may be relied upon in integration tests or CI diagnostics.

insights.title: Encapsulate POM resolution and MavenProject building
insights.problem: Multiple places duplicated logic to find POM files in the local repo and resolve them via ArtifactResolver and ProjectBuilder lookups (with container lookups to deprecated components).
insights.root_cause: LockFileFacade and plugin-resolution code directly performed repository/local-file lookups and container component access.
insights.solution: Create ProjectBuilder buildFromGav that tries local repository path, falls back to artifact resolution using ArtifactFactory/ArtifactResolver and builds a MavenProject using ProjectBuilder.build(pomFile,...). Returns Optional<MavenProject> and logs warnings instead of throwing.
insights.lesson_learned: Wrap low-level, deprecated container lookups and artifact-resolve logic behind a single helper returning Optional to simplify callers and centralize warning behavior.

maven_plugin/src/main/java/org/apache/ma.short_description: Reflection helper to extract the conflict 'winner' version from VerboseDependencyNode.
maven_plugin/src/main/java/org/apache/ma.description:
This utility exists because the public DependencyNode API does not expose conflict-resolution details (the "winner" version) that the plugin needs when building its dependency graph. The class provides a single static accessor that attempts a reflective read of a private field named 'data' on an internal implementation (VerboseDependencyNode), casts it to ConflictData and returns the winner version wrapped in an Optional. The method is defensive: it only runs the reflection path when the supplied node is an instance of VerboseDependencyNode and returns Optional.empty() on any reflection failure.

The design is intentionally minimal and brittle-by-necessity: it performs a setAccessible(true) on a private field and handles several reflection exceptions by logging a warning through PluginLogManager and returning an empty result. The utility is used to determine the effective version of a dependency (the one chosen by Maven's conflict resolution) during lockfile generation or validation. Because it reaches into Maven-internal types, it is sensitive to changes in those classes' internals and to runtime constraints (security manager or Java module access rules).

Commit history indicates this reflective access is a known hack introduced because the needed data is not available via the public API; logging was changed to use PluginLogManager.getLog().warn on reflection failures. Consumers of this helper expect an Optional<String> that is present when (and only when) the internal structure matches the assumed layout and is accessible at runtime.

key_constructs.name: SpyingDependencyNodeUtils
key_constructs.purpose: Container for reflection helper(s) to read non-public conflict data from DependencyNode implementations.
key_constructs.reasoning: The class groups the unsafe reflection logic into a single place so callers can get a winner version without embedding reflection everywhere; an agent editing code that depends on Maven internals should treat this class as the canonical place for such hacks.

key_constructs.name: getWinnerVersion
key_constructs.purpose: Return the conflict winner version for a DependencyNode by reflectively reading VerboseDependencyNode.data. Returns Optional.empty() on any failure or if the node is not the expected implementation.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the single public API of the file; modifications to consumers' behavior regarding winner-version resolution will touch this method. It must preserve the Optional contract and the logging behavior for reflection failures.

key_behaviors: reads a private 'data' field from VerboseDependencyNode via reflection
casts the private field to ConflictData and calls getWinnerVersion()
returns Optional.empty on type mismatches or reflection failures
logs reflection failures via PluginLogManager.getLog().warn

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming the internal field name, type, or accessibility of VerboseDependencyNode is stable.
pitfalls.consequence: Reflection will fail (NoSuchFieldException, IllegalAccessException, InaccessibleObjectException on newer JDKs) and callers will receive Optional.empty() where a winner version might otherwise exist; behavior may silently change if exceptions are swallowed.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect that callers must handle Optional.empty; when making changes keep the exception handling and logging intact and verify against the Maven dependency-graph implementation used by CI.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or changing the PluginLogManager logging call or altering exception coverage.
pitfalls.consequence: Loss of diagnostics when reflection fails; harder to detect API breaks during upgrades.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the warning log and the broad exception catch (NoSuchFieldException | SecurityException | IllegalArgumentException | IllegalAccessException) or ensure equivalent diagnostic coverage is produced if exception types change.

pitfalls.mistake: Calling this method expecting it to always return a value for non-VerboseDependencyNode instances.
pitfalls.consequence: Incorrect assumptions in callers may lead to incorrect dependency versions used in lockfile generation/validation.
pitfalls.prevention: Treat Optional.empty() as a legitimate outcome and ensure callers have fallback behavior when the winner version cannot be determined.

pitfalls.mistake: Ignoring Java module / security restrictions (setAccessible may be blocked).
pitfalls.consequence: Reflection will throw an exception at runtime (InaccessibleObjectException) on modular JDKs or under restrictive security policies.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep reflection calls guarded and logged; tests and CI should exercise the runtime environments where the plugin is expected to run.

key_sections: getWinnerVersion: checks instance type, uses reflection to access 'data', casts to ConflictData and returns winner version
exception handling block: catches reflection-related exceptions and logs via PluginLogManager
skip_unless_needed: import lines
Javadoc comment above the method (brief descriptive text only)

related_files.relationship: The runtime implementation inspected by reflection; changes to its private fields/types will break this utility.
related_files.reason_to_check: If the external VerboseDependencyNode implementation changes field names/types or is replaced by a different implementation, update or re-evaluate this reflection logic.

related_files.relationship: The reflected field is cast to ConflictData and getWinnerVersion() is invoked; its API and method name must match assumptions.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change in ConflictData (method name, return type, or semantics) will impact this method's correctness.

related_files.relationship: Used for logging warnings on reflection failures; logging API must remain available and stable.
related_files.reason_to_check: If the logging API is refactored, preserve equivalent logging behavior so reflection failures remain visible.

tests_to_run: integration tests that exercise lockfile generation/validation in CI environments

insights.title: Reflection-based access retains logging but moves to PluginLogManager
insights.problem: Utility used reflection to access conflict data on DependencyNode; failure of reflection previously logged via log4j.
insights.root_cause: DependencyNode API didn't expose winnerVersion; hack via reflection required and may fail across versions.
insights.solution: On exceptions, now log via PluginLogManager.getLog().warn rather than log4j.
insights.lesson_learned: Reflection hacks should log descriptive warnings and are brittle across library updates. Prefer contributing a fix upstream or isolating reflective logic so it can be adapted easily.

insights.title: Reflective extraction of winner version from VerboseDependencyNode
insights.problem: The code needs the conflict winner version during DependencyGraph creation but Maven's public API doesn't expose it.
insights.root_cause: VerboseDependencyNode's conflict data field is private and not accessible through public API.
insights.solution: Add SpyingDependencyNodeUtils.getWinnerVersion(DependencyNode) that checks for VerboseDependencyNode, reflectively accesses its private 'data' field, and reads ConflictData.getWinnerVersion(); returns Optional.empty() on any reflection failure.
insights.lesson_learned: Reflection into third-party internal classes should be used only as a last resort. Wrap such logic with safe fallbacks (Optional.empty) and add tests to detect API breaks when upgrading dependencies.

maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.short_description: Immutable value object wrapping a non-null, non-empty Maven artifactId with comparison and equality semantics
maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.description:
ArtifactId is a small domain value-object that encapsulates the artifactId string used throughout the lockfile generator and dependency-graph code. It exists to provide a typed representation of the artifact identifier (the final segment of a Maven coordinate), to centralize validation rules (via the static factory), and to provide well-defined equals/hashCode/compareTo semantics so it can be safely used as keys in collections (maps/sets) and be ordered in sorted collections. The class is intentionally tiny and immutable: its single field is a final String value exposed via getValue().

This class fits into the larger system as a core building block for dependency nodes and lockfile data structures (e.g., dependency graph nodes, plugin identifiers). It is referenced by other data model classes that need to identify artifacts consistently and perform deterministic ordering or set membership checks. Because ordering is required for deterministic lockfile serialization, the class implements Comparable and provides a stable compareTo based on String.compareTo.

There are two ways to create an instance: the static factory ArtifactId.of(String) which enforces non-null and non-empty strings, and the public constructor ArtifactId(String) which enforces non-null only. The difference between these two constructors is a non-obvious behavior to respect when editing related code: some code paths may rely on the stricter factory validation while other code (or serialization frameworks) might directly call the public constructor. The class uses standard Object-based implementations of equals and hashCode based solely on the wrapped String value and uses case-sensitive string comparisons.

Because the class is small, changes to equality, hashing, or ordering will ripple into any code that stores ArtifactId in maps, sets, or sorted collections (including the lockfile generation and validation flows). It is also potentially involved in JSON (de)serialization or reflection (for example by libraries that instantiate via constructors), so any change to constructors or field visibility must consider those consumers.

key_constructs.name: ArtifactId
key_constructs.purpose: Encapsulates a Maven artifactId string as an immutable, comparable value-object used by lockfile and dependency graph code.
key_constructs.reasoning: When working on code that uses artifact identifiers, rely on this class for typed identity, ordering, and hashing semantics. Respect the existence of both the static factory (which enforces non-empty) and the public constructor (which only enforces non-null) because callers may use either; changing validation or constructors can affect downstream code and serialization.

key_behaviors: validates non-null artifact id
validates non-empty artifact id when created via of(...)
wraps the artifact id string as an immutable value
provides deterministic ordering via Comparable
implements equals/hashCode for use in maps/sets

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming the public constructor enforces non-empty strings the same way as ArtifactId.of(String).
pitfalls.consequence: Code may accidentally create ArtifactId instances with empty strings if it calls new ArtifactId(""), leading to inconsistency with places that used of() and potential incorrect behavior in validation/serialization and ordering.
pitfalls.prevention: When modifying or using this class, be aware that of() and the constructor differ in validation. Avoid creating instances with empty strings unless that behavior is intentionally allowed by the wider system; search the codebase for direct constructor usages and serialization points before changing validation.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering equals, hashCode, or compareTo semantics (e.g., switching to case-insensitive comparison) without auditing consumers.
pitfalls.consequence: Maps, sets, and sorted collections that rely on current semantics may break determinism of lockfile generation or cause incorrect deduplication/ordering, producing differing lockfiles or failing validations.
pitfalls.prevention: If equality or ordering must change, audit all places that store or sort ArtifactId instances (dependency graph, lockfile serialization) and update serialization/validation tests accordingly.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or modifying the public constructor signature without considering deserialization frameworks.
pitfalls.consequence: JSON (de)serializers or reflection-based tools that construct instances via the constructor may fail to instantiate ArtifactId, breaking lockfile IO or test utilities.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep constructor compatibility or provide adapters/annotations for serializers and update related code before removing/changing constructors.

key_sections: of: factory method that enforces non-null and non-empty input (important for validation callers)
constructor ArtifactId(String): enforces non-null only (check callers that may use it directly)
compareTo: defines deterministic ordering used by sorted collections and serialization
equals/hashCode: used for collection membership and map keys
skip_unless_needed: toString: simple representation used for debugging/logging

related_files.relationship: DependencyNode likely uses ArtifactId as part of identity/comparator when representing nodes in the dependency graph; changes to ArtifactId ordering/equality affect graph behavior and lockfile determinism
related_files.reason_to_check: If changing equals/compareTo/validation semantics, verify how DependencyNode builds comparator strings and how graph sorting/serialization depends on them.

related_files.relationship: LockFile is the serialized output that depends on stable artifact identity and ordering; changes to ArtifactId semantics can alter serialization order or contents
related_files.reason_to_check: When modifying ArtifactId behavior, ensure lockfile generation remains deterministic and compatible with existing validation logic.

maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.short_description: Resolves BOM (type=pom, scope=import) POMs and attaches Pom chain metadata (including checksums) to projects and dependency nodes.
maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.description: BomResolver locates and resolves BOMs imported via dependencyManagement entries in MavenProject models and produces Pom objects that contain resolved URL, repository id, checksum algorithm, checksum and parent chain. It exists so BOMs (Bill of Materials) used by a project or by dependencies can be recorded into the generated lockfile, mirroring how POM parents are handled. The resolver uses a ProjectBuilder to construct MavenProject instances from GAV coordinates and an AbstractChecksumCalculator to read resolved repository information and to compute checksums for artifact POMs. The resulting Pom objects are TreeSet-ordered and either returned for a single project or attached (mutated onto) DependencyNode instances in a DependencyGraph.

key_constructs.name: BomResolver
key_constructs.purpose: Main class that exposes BOM resolution functionality for projects and dependency graphs.
key_constructs.reasoning: Agents editing code that calls or modifies BOM resolution should start here: it wires session, repositories and checksum calculator, and defines the public API (resolveForProject, resolveBomsForDependencies). Respect its constructor contract (session, repositories, checksumCalculator) when instantiating.

callers.context: BomResolver resolver = new BomResolver(session, project.getRemoteArtifactRepositories(), checksumCal

key_constructs.name: resolveForProject
key_constructs.purpose: Scans a given MavenProject's dependencyManagement for import/type=pom entries and returns a sorted Set<Pom> of resolved BOM POMs (including parent chains).
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the primary entry point for resolving BOMs for a single project. It interprets dependency entries, interpolates simple ${...} placeholders (via resolveVersionFromPlaceholder), builds MavenProject via ProjectBuilder.buildFromGav, warns on failures and constructs Pom chains via resolveBomParents.

key_constructs.name: resolveBomsForDependencies
key_constructs.purpose: Iterates all dependency nodes in a DependencyGraph, attempts to resolve BOMs for each dependency's MavenProject and sets the found set on the node via node.setBoms(...).
key_constructs.reasoning: This integrates BOM resolution into the dependency graph path used when generating lockfiles. It mutates graph nodes in-place, so callers and agents must account for this side-effect; the method builds its own ProjectBuilder and BomResolver instances and logs warnings when a project's GAV cannot be built.

key_constructs.name: resolveVersionFromPlaceholder
key_constructs.purpose: If a version string is a simple ${property} placeholder, attempts to resolve it from the project's interpolated model properties and returns the resolved version or the raw version token when unresolved.
key_constructs.reasoning: This handles the common case where dependencyManagement versions are provided via properties. It only handles exact ${...} forms and looks up properties on project.getModel().getProperties().

key_constructs.name: resolveBomParents
key_constructs.purpose: Given a MavenProject (the BOM), walks the parent chain up to the root and constructs a linked Pom parent chain (root first) using mavenProjectToBom.
key_constructs.reasoning: BOMs may themselves have parent POMs; this method replicates the project's parent traversal used elsewhere so that Pom objects in the lockfile capture parent metadata and checksums consistently.

key_constructs.name: mavenProjectToBom
key_constructs.purpose: Converts a single MavenProject into a Pom data object containing groupId, artifactId, version, repo resolved URL and id, checksum algorithm, checksum and parent Pom reference.
key_constructs.reasoning: This centralizes how a MavenProject is converted into the lockfile's Pom representation, and invokes checksumCalculator APIs (getArtifactResolvedField, calculateArtifactChecksum, getChecksumAlgorithm) — changes here affect checksum/resolution metadata everywhere BOMs are produced.

key_behaviors: resolves BOM POMs declared as dependencyManagement type=pom scope=import for a MavenProject
builds parent Pom chains for BOMs and returns them as a sorted set
attaches resolved BOM sets to DependencyNode instances in a DependencyGraph
interpolates simple ${property} placeholders in dependencyManagement version fields

pitfalls.mistake: Treating resolveBomsForDependencies as a pure function (non-mutating)
pitfalls.consequence: DependencyGraph nodes are mutated in-place via node.setBoms; callers that assume immutability or attempt to parallelize mutation may get inconsistent or surprising state.
pitfalls.prevention: Be aware resolveBomsForDependencies mutates nodes; if immutability is required, copy graph data before calling or refactor callers to accept in-place updates.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming resolveVersionFromPlaceholder handles complex interpolation
pitfalls.consequence: Only exact ${property} tokens are resolved; more complex expressions or nested placeholders will remain unresolved causing ProjectBuilder.buildFromGav to fail to locate a BOM.
pitfalls.prevention: When changing placeholder handling, check tests that rely on simple property interpolation (bomPom, bomPomWithParent).

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming checksumCalculator behavior is deterministic across environments
pitfalls.consequence: Checksum calculation may vary depending on checksum mode (filesystem vs remote) or environment/OS (platform-specific artifacts), which will cause different Pom.checksum values and may break integration tests or CI validation.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the AbstractChecksumCalculator abstraction and its mode; run the same test command used by CI (mvn test/verify) on the target environment to validate behavior.

pitfalls.mistake: Ignoring ProjectBuilder.buildFromGav empty Optional returns
pitfalls.consequence: The resolver logs a warning and skips the BOM; failing to handle this may cause missing BOMs in lockfile and test failures rely on explicit warnings or absence of BOM entries.
pitfalls.prevention: Check logs in CI or tests for 'Could not resolve BOM' messages; maintain warning behavior rather than throwing exceptions unless explicitly desired.

key_sections: resolveForProject: identifies import/pom entries and orchestrates BOM project build and parent resolution
resolveBomsForDependencies: how BOMs are attached to dependency graph nodes (mutation)
resolveBomParents: parent chain traversal and order reversal to produce root-first Pom chains
mavenProjectToBom: where checksumCalculator is invoked and Pom objects are constructed
skip_unless_needed: Constructor field declarations (boilerplate assignment)
Minor logging warnings (single-line warn calls)

relevant_snippets.lines: unknown (integration test file, search for bomPom and bomPomWithParent)
relevant_snippets.description: Integration tests that verify BOM POMs and BOM parent chains are resolved and recorded into the lockfile.

related_files.relationship: Used to construct MavenProject objects from GAVs for BOM resolution via buildFromGav.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to ProjectBuilder.buildFromGav affect BOM resolution success and must be considered when modifying BomResolver behavior or tests.

related_files.relationship: BomResolver delegates resolved URL and checksum calculation to this abstraction; mavenProjectToBom calls its methods.
related_files.reason_to_check: Altering the checksum API or semantics will change Pom.checksum values produced here; tests may need updating.

related_files.relationship: resolveBomsForDependencies iterates graph.getDependencySet() and mutates DependencyNode instances inside the graph.
related_files.reason_to_check: Code that relies on graph immutability or traversal order may be affected if BomResolver's mutation behavior or timing changes.

related_files.relationship: Nodes receive BOM sets via node.setBoms; the data structure's contract determines how BOMs are later serialized into LockFile.
related_files.reason_to_check: If the node API changes (setter semantics, immutability), BomResolver must be updated accordingly.

related_files.relationship: BomResolver uses PluginLogManager.getLog().warn to report unresolved BOMs and skipped resolutions.
related_files.reason_to_check: If logging expectations change, tests asserting log contents or behavior may need updates.

related_files.relationship: LockFileFacade orchestrates lockfile generation and calls into BOM resolution in the overall flow; changes in how BOMs are attached will affect LockFile generation.
related_files.reason_to_check: LockFile construction and serialization depend on the Pom objects produced by BomResolver.

tests_to_run: mvn verify (full integration test lifecycle used by CI)

insights.title: Resolve BOM POMs (dependencyManagement imports) and include in lockfile
insights.problem: BOMs imported via dependencyManagement (type=pom, scope=import) were previously not recorded in the lockfile outputs or attached to dependencies.
insights.root_cause: No dedicated resolution step for BOM POMs existed; dependency graph nodes lacked a place to store BOM metadata.
insights.solution: Add BomResolver that scans dependencyManagement for import/pom entries, resolves BOM POMs via ProjectBuilder, resolves parent chains (constructRecursive style), converts MavenProject -> Pom with checksum and resolved URL/repo info, and sets BOMs on DependencyNode objects. Exposes resolveForProject(project) for top-level project BOMs.
insights.lesson_learned: BOMs are first-class metadata that affect resolution and must be resolved similarly to POM parents; centralize property interpolation (placeholder handling) and parent resolution. Note: current implementation mutates DependencyNode (setBoms) — consider returning an immutable mapping to avoid hidden side effects.

maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.short_description: Immutable data holder for environment metadata (OS, Maven and Java) used in generated lockfiles
maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.description:
Represents the environment in which a lockfile was produced: it records the operating system name, the Maven version and the Java version. This class exists to capture and persist environment-related metadata into the LockFile model so consumers (and validation logic) can compare or display the environment that produced the lockfile. It is intentionally immutable: fields are final and only accessible via getters. Equality and hash-code are defined to enable comparisons between Environment instances (for example, when validating an existing lockfile against the current run).

The class is small and focused: it provides a constructor, three getters, and overrides toString, equals and hashCode. It is used by the larger LockFile model and by Mojo logic that assembles metadata during lockfile generation. Historical context: this class was renamed from Metadata to Environment to make semantics clearer; some legacy artifacts remain (e.g., toString still prints "Metadata [...]"), which may be relied on by tests or text comparisons, so care must be taken when editing.

Because this class is part of the JSON data model for the lockfile, its shape (getter names and field names) and equality semantics are significant. Changes to getter names, field names, or serialization behavior can affect produced lockfile JSON and validation in CI. Also be mindful that the recorded strings (OS, Maven, Java) can vary across platforms and JDK distributions, which is a known source of non-determinism in cross-platform CI runs.

key_constructs.name: Environment
key_constructs.purpose: Immutable data model capturing osName, mavenVersion and javaVersion for the lockfile's environment metadata.
key_constructs.reasoning: This class is the canonical in-memory representation of environment metadata persisted to the LockFile; maintain its immutability and equality contract so lockfile generation and validation are stable and comparable.

key_constructs.name: Environment
key_constructs.purpose: Constructor Environment(String osName, String mavenVersion, String javaVersion)
key_constructs.reasoning: Sets final fields used by getters and equality. Any changes to constructor semantics (e.g., validation or defaulting) will affect how environment metadata is created during lockfile generation.

key_constructs.name: getJavaVersion
key_constructs.purpose: Returns javaVersion recorded in this Environment instance.
key_constructs.reasoning: Getter used by JSON serializers and comparison logic; changing name or signature impacts serialization and consumers expecting this property.

key_constructs.name: getMavenVersion
key_constructs.purpose: Returns mavenVersion recorded in this Environment instance.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by serialization and equals comparisons; consistent naming is required for JSON compatibility.

key_constructs.name: getOsName
key_constructs.purpose: Returns osName recorded in this Environment instance.
key_constructs.reasoning: Exposed for serialization into lockfile and for comparisons in validation; renaming/removing will break consumers.

key_constructs.name: toString
key_constructs.purpose: Produces a human-readable representation of the Environment instance.
key_constructs.reasoning: Currently returns a string prefixed with 'Metadata [...]' (historical artifact). Textual output may be used in logging or tests, so changes to this format can affect test assertions or logs.

key_constructs.name: hashCode
key_constructs.purpose: Provides a hash code consistent with equals for use in hashed collections.
key_constructs.reasoning: Relies on Objects.hash(osName, mavenVersion, javaVersion); keep this consistent with equals to preserve collection semantics and test expectations.

key_constructs.name: equals
key_constructs.purpose: Compares two Environment instances based on osName, mavenVersion and javaVersion.
key_constructs.reasoning: Equality is used by validation and tests that compare environments; maintain null-safety and field order used here because test assertions may depend on these semantics.

key_behaviors: records OS/Maven/Java versions as strings for lockfile metadata
provides stable equality and hashCode for comparison operations
exposes getters used by JSON serialization for lockfile output

pitfalls.mistake: Renaming or removing getters (getOsName, getMavenVersion, getJavaVersion) or changing their return types
pitfalls.consequence: Breaks JSON serialization / deserialization and any consumers that parse the lockfile or call these methods directly, causing incompatibilities in lockfile format and validation failures.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve getter names and signatures; if breaking changes are required, ensure coordinated updates across serialization code, LockFile model and consumers.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering equals/hashCode semantics or field order used in hash computation
pitfalls.consequence: Can change comparison results used during validation and tests, potentially causing spurious mismatches or failing collection lookups.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep equals and hashCode consistent and based on the same fields; update dependent tests/logic if any change is unavoidable.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming environment values are stable across platforms (e.g., javaVersion or osName)
pitfalls.consequence: Cross-platform CI may produce differing lockfiles or validation failures due to platform-specific strings.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect that these strings can differ by OS/JDK distribution and avoid hard-coding them in tests that run across different environments.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying toString output without checking logs/tests
pitfalls.consequence: Because the toString currently uses the historical label 'Metadata', changing it may break textual comparisons or log parsing in integration tests or CI diagnostics.
pitfalls.prevention: Treat toString as part of the observable output surface; review any tests or tooling that may assert on its format before changing it.

key_sections: constructor: sets the three final fields that define the object's identity
getOsName/getMavenVersion/getJavaVersion: used for serialization and by callers that embed environment metadata into lockfiles
equals/hashCode: important to understand for comparison and hashing behavior
skip_unless_needed: toString: mainly for human-readable output (but see pitfalls around its historical 'Metadata' label)

exercised_by: integration tests that compare lockfile environment

related_files.relationship: LockFile contains an Environment instance as its metadata; changes to Environment affect lockfile JSON shape and validation.
related_files.reason_to_check: Ensure JSON serialization/deserialization and equality expectations remain compatible when editing Environment.

related_files.relationship: Mojo that assembles metadata for the lockfile; it constructs Environment instances during generation.
related_files.reason_to_check: Confirm how environment strings are populated and that any changes to constructor or getters are compatible with Mojo usage.

related_files.relationship: Validation logic may compare the current environment with one stored in a lockfile; equality semantics matter here.
related_files.reason_to_check: Validation may rely on equals/hashCode; verify behavior after edits.

tests_to_run: Run integration tests that generate and validate lockfiles across platforms (if available in your environment)

insights.title: Rename Metadata -> Environment and expose environment fields
insights.problem: Class named Metadata was ambiguous; name changed to Environment to represent OS/Maven/Java info.
insights.root_cause: Semantic clarity: metadata contained only environment info previously.
insights.solution: Rename class to Environment and update usages in Mojos and LockFile model.
insights.lesson_learned: Choose class names that clearly describe their content (Environment vs Metadata) to avoid confusion when adding Config later.

maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.short_description: Value object representing an optional Maven artifact classifier with comparison and equality semantics
maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.description:
This class is a small domain value object to represent a Maven artifact classifier (an optional arbitrary string appended to artifact filenames, e.g. 'sources' or 'javadoc'). It exists to make classifier handling explicit and comparable across the codebase and to ensure consistent semantics for empty and null classifier inputs. The factory method Classifier.of(String) encodes the project's chosen canonical behavior: treat null or empty classifier strings as absent by returning null (not Optional). The Classifier instances are immutable and implement Comparable so they can be ordered consistently when building or serializing dependency lists.

The class is used by higher-level models (e.g., lockfile data models and dependency nodes) and by integration tests that exercise classifier behavior. It provides equals/hashCode for value equality, compareTo for ordering, and a simple toString for diagnostics. The constructor enforces non-null value once an instance is created, so callers that use Classifier.of must be prepared to accept null as the signal for 'no classifier'. The implementation uses Guava Strings.isNullOrEmpty in the factory to collapse empty strings into the absent/null case, following the commit history which added this domain type to make empty/null handling explicit.

key_constructs.name: Classifier
key_constructs.purpose: Immutable value object representing a Maven artifact classifier.
key_constructs.reasoning: Edits to LockFile and dependency models rely on a stable, comparable representation of optional classifier strings; this class centralizes that logic and enforces non-null invariants for created instances.

key_constructs.name: of
key_constructs.purpose: Factory method that returns a Classifier instance for non-empty strings or null when input is null/empty.
key_constructs.reasoning: This encodes the project's canonical interpretation of empty classifier values as absent (null). Any code constructing Classifier must use this factory or otherwise preserve this semantics.

key_constructs.name: getValue
key_constructs.purpose: Accessor returning the underlying classifier string.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by serializers and callers that need the raw classifier; callers can assume non-null when they have a Classifier instance (constructor prevents null).

key_constructs.name: compareTo
key_constructs.purpose: Implements lexicographic ordering of classifier values for sorting/comparison.
key_constructs.reasoning: Dependency ordering and deterministic serialization rely on consistent compareTo behavior; compareTo assumes the other operand is non-null.

key_behaviors: represents optional artifact classifier strings
returns null for null/empty input via Classifier.of
ensures non-null internal value for created instances
compares classifiers lexicographically for deterministic ordering

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming Classifier.of(...) always returns a non-null Classifier.
pitfalls.consequence: Callers may dereference null and get NullPointerException when no classifier was present in input.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect that Classifier.of returns null for null/empty input; guard or test for null before using methods like getValue() or compareTo().

pitfalls.mistake: Calling compareTo with a null argument or comparing against a null Classifier instance.
pitfalls.consequence: A NullPointerException will be thrown; compareTo does not defensively handle null operands.
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure both operands are non-null (e.g., use Classifier.of semantics or null checks) before calling compareTo.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering Classifier.of to return an Optional or non-null sentinel without updating call sites.
pitfalls.consequence: Breaks existing code that expects null for absent classifier (including tests and serialization), leading to subtle runtime or test failures.
pitfalls.prevention: If the factory semantics change, update all call sites, tests, and serialization logic consistently; treat the current null-return contract as authoritative unless coordinated.

pitfalls.mistake: Treating empty string and a classifier-holding instance equivalently without using Classifier.of.
pitfalls.consequence: Inconsistent lockfile content (empty string written vs. no classifier) and non-deterministic behavior across code paths.
pitfalls.prevention: Use Classifier.of to normalize inputs so empty strings are collapsed to absent.

maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.short_description: Immutable value object representing a Maven groupId with ordering and basic validation
maven_plugin/src/main/java/io/github/cha.description:
GroupId is a small immutable value object used to represent the Maven groupId coordinate in the lockfile data model. It centralizes simple validation (non-null, non-empty), equality, hashing and ordering logic for group identifiers. It is designed as a minimal wrapper around a String to provide type-safety and consistent compare/equals semantics across the codebase.

The class exposes a single factory method GroupId.of(String) that enforces the input requirements and returns an instance with a private final value field. It implements Comparable<GroupId> using String.compareTo on the raw value, and overrides equals, hashCode and toString. The design choice to use a factory rather than a public constructor enforces the runtime invariant that no GroupId instance can be constructed with a null or empty string value. The class is small and intentionally lightweight to serve as a typed domain primitive consumed by lockfile data objects (e.g., LockFile, Pom, DependencyNode) and by any code that needs a typed group identifier.

Because this class is part of the data model package, it is likely involved in serialization/deserialization and in collections or graph structures. It does not provide a no-arg constructor or JSON-specific annotations; serialization frameworks that require a default constructor may need to be considered elsewhere in the codebase. The compareTo uses the raw String.compareTo which is case-sensitive and respects lexicographic ordering; there is no trimming or normalization of input, so stored values are exact as provided by callers.

key_constructs.name: GroupId
key_constructs.purpose: Represents an immutable Maven groupId string with validation, equality, hash and ordering.
key_constructs.reasoning: Agents editing code that consumes or produces lockfile data must preserve the factory validation semantics, equality/hash contract, and ordering behavior (String.compareTo) because other parts of the system likely rely on stable comparisons and canonicalization. The private constructor enforces controlled creation through of(), so changes that bypass the factory will break invariants.

key_constructs.name: of
key_constructs.purpose: Factory method that validates input and returns a new GroupId instance.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the canonical entry point for constructing GroupId instances. It throws NullPointerException for null inputs (via Objects.requireNonNull) and IllegalArgumentException for empty strings, establishing the allowed domain. Callers and agents must respect that these exceptions are part of the contract.

key_constructs.name: compareTo
key_constructs.purpose: Provides a natural ordering of GroupId instances via lexicographic comparison of the underlying string.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ordering is used when GroupId objects are sorted or used in ordered collections; it performs a case-sensitive String.compareTo on the raw value. Any component that relies on ordering should be aware of this exact behavior (no normalization, case sensitivity).

key_constructs.name: equals
key_constructs.purpose: Compares GroupId instances for equality by delegating to Objects.equals on the wrapped string.
key_constructs.reasoning: Equality is defined strictly on the contained value. The implementation uses instanceof (so subclasses would be considered) and compares the raw value; maintainers must preserve this if other types or subclasses are introduced.

key_constructs.name: hashCode
key_constructs.purpose: Returns the hash code of the wrapped string using Objects.hashCode.
key_constructs.reasoning: Consistent with equals; required for correct behavior when GroupId is used as a key in hash-based collections.

key_behaviors: creates GroupId instances after validating non-null and non-empty input
provides deterministic ordering via lexicographic comparison
implements equality and hashing based on the raw string value

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming null inputs produce IllegalArgumentException from of()
pitfalls.consequence: Null inputs will produce a NullPointerException (Objects.requireNonNull) before the empty-string check; tests or callers expecting IllegalArgumentException for null will fail.
pitfalls.prevention: Treat null as a possible NPE from of() and ensure callers avoid passing null; check callers that construct GroupId to avoid nulls.

pitfalls.mistake: Normalizing or trimming strings elsewhere while assuming GroupId normalizes
pitfalls.consequence: GroupId stores the value exactly as provided; differences in whitespace or case across call sites can lead to inequality, different ordering, and inconsistent lockfile entries.
pitfalls.prevention: When constructing GroupId, ensure callers provide the canonical form expected by downstream consumers; do not rely on GroupId to perform normalization.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying toString format or equality semantics without checking consumers
pitfalls.consequence: Log parsing, test assertions, or serialization that depend on the current toString or equals behavior can break.
pitfalls.prevention: When editing this file, audit call sites and any serialization or logging expectations that rely on the exact output or equality semantics.

compareTo: shows ordering semantics (String.compareTo used, case-sensitive)
equals & hashCode: demonstrate equality contract used in collections

related_files.reason_to_check: If Pom's serialization or equality depends on GroupId's string shape or construction, changes to GroupId may require updates in Pom.

related_files.reason_to_check: Lockfile serialization, comparisons during validate goal, and tests may rely on exact GroupId behavior.
